races.race_name: Betfred Tattenham Corner Stakes (Group 3) (Formerly The John Of Gaunt Stakes)

runners.name: Never So Brave
runners.spotlight: Merely mid-division in the Group 1 QEII at Ascot (1m, good) last October and again below his best on reappearance at Sandown (1m, good) in April; however, he pulled hard last time and holds leading claims if rediscovering the form of his Group 1 City Of York win (7f, good to firm) last August; mixed messages in his form regarding the suitability of slow ground.
runners.comment: Pulled hard on comeback run at Sandown; leading claims on last August's Group 1 York win

runners.name: Alcantor
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who won three Group 3s in France for Andre Fabre (1m, testing ground) and was a very close fourth in the Group 1 Prix du Moulin at Longchamp (1m, good to soft) last September; below-par fourth on stable debut at Leopardstown (1m, good; Listed) four weeks ago but may have needed the run; of interest if rediscovering his best French form.
runners.comment: Three Group 3 wins in France and may have needed the run on last month's stable debut

runners.name: Golden Mind
runners.spotlight: 5yo who won three handicaps last year (including on heavy ground) and has been better than ever in such races this season, finishing second at Musselburgh (7f, good to soft) then fourth of 28 in the Victoria Cup at Ascot (7f, good); however, he has something to find against today's opponents.
runners.comment: Better than ever in handicaps this season but has something to find today

runners.name: Ten Bob Tony
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent and he disappointed in Listed race at Haydock (7f, good) last time; however, he reportedly lost a shoe and prior to that was an encouraging fourth on his reappearance at the Curragh (7f, soft to heavy); won this race last year (good; at its old home Haydock) and posted fine third in Group 1 Foret at Longchamp (7f, very soft) last October; player if bouncing back.
runners.comment: Disappointing last time but won this race last year (at its old home Haydock)

runners.name: Poet Master
runners.spotlight: Group 2 winner at the Curragh (7f, good) in July 2024 and runner-up in two 7f Group races last autumn (very soft/good); not at his best on his two runs this season but each-way claims if back on song today.
runners.comment: Runner-up in two Group races last autumn; below best this term; e-w claims if back on song
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Witness Stand
runners.spotlight: Listed winner on soft ground who won the Group 2 Lennox Stakes at Goodwood (7f, good) last July before excellent second in the Group 2 Hungerford at Newbury (7f, good to firm) in August; he's lost his form on his last four starts but perhaps wind surgery will have done the trick; wears a first-time tongue-tie and capable of having a big say if rediscovering his best.
runners.comment: Strong Group 2 form last summer; struggled since but perhaps a wind op will do the trick

runners.name: Chicago Critic
runners.spotlight: Short-headed in a Group 2 at Meydan (7f, good) in January but he needs a bigger performance if he's to take this and he hasn't been at the top of his game on his three starts since, most recently at the Curragh.
runners.comment: Short-headed in Meydan Group 2 in January but needs a bigger performance to take this

runners.name: Balmacara
runners.spotlight: Listed runner-up at Sandown (1m, soft) last September and third in the same grade at Haydock (7f, good) four weeks ago on reappearance; however, he was comfortably beaten in that latest start and is 0-11 since winning novice contests at Doncaster in spring 2024 on second and third starts; up against it.
runners.comment: Has run well in defeat in Listed races but looks up against it in today's company

races.race_name: Princess Elizabeth Stakes (Group 3) (Fillies & Mares) (Sponsored By Chapel Down)

runners.name: Shes Perfect
runners.spotlight: All three runs last season were in France; suffered a heartbreaking demotion for connections when placed second in the French 1,000 Guineas at Longchamp (1m, good) and failed to stay an extended 1m2f in the Diane at Chantilly last June; not seen again until third in 1m Goodwood Listed event five weeks ago, fading late on as if the run was needed; should be spot on now.
runners.comment: Led for long way when third in Goodwood Listed on return and should be spot on now

runners.name: Pacific Mission
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in the Group 2 May Hill at Doncaster (1m, good to soft) and the Grade 1 Breeders' Cup Juvenile Fillies Turf at Del Mar (1m, firm) last autumn; below that level when fifth in 1m York Listed race on reappearance but it's possible she simply needed the outing; player if back to her best.
runners.comment: Second in Group 2 and Grade 1 over 1m last year; may have needed return; player if at best

runners.name: Sparks Fly
runners.spotlight: Four-time Listed winner who made Group 3 breakthrough at Saint-Cloud (1m, very soft) last October; ran well when 2l fourth in Group 2 Huxley Stakes at Chester (10.3f, good) four weeks ago; third in this last year and a penalty shouldn't prevent her being competitive again.
runners.comment: Third in this last year; won French Group 3 last autumn; creditable 4th at Chester latest

runners.name: Breckenbrough
runners.spotlight: French raider who won a 1m Saint-Cloud Listed race on soft last autumn; arrives on the back of creditable in-frame efforts in similar races at Longchamp (7f/1m) this spring; often races prominently.
runners.comment: Listed winner in France last autumn; creditable efforts in similar races there this spring

runners.name: Love Dynasty
runners.spotlight: Very lightly raced 5yo who left the impression 1m2f just stretched her when fourth in Nottingham Listed race on good-ground reappearance; raced only on soft/heavy prior to that, so won't be inconvenienced however much rain arrives.
runners.comment: Very lightly raced 5yo who has raced only on good or softer; drop back in trip is a help

runners.name: Princess Child
runners.spotlight: Group 3 winner at Tipperary (7.5f, yielding) at the end of last summer; matched that form when second in 1m Group 3 at the Curragh (soft/heavy) but needs to bounce back from a lesser effort at the Curragh last time.
runners.comment: Group 3 winner last year; second at same level this year but latest effort was below par

runners.name: Pina Sonata
runners.spotlight: Came up well short in Group 1/Listed events in the second half of 2025 but different story on Kempton AW reappearance, readily seeing off ten rivals in 1m Listed race; winner on good to soft; capable of making her presence felt, with further progress a distinct possibility after only six starts.
runners.comment: AW Listed winner on reappearance and further progress is possible after only six starts

runners.name: Betty Clover
runners.spotlight: Won a Listed race at York (5f, good) as a 2yo but she's 0-13 since and finished behind Shes Perfect and Arisaig in a Goodwood Listed event last time.
runners.comment: Listed win at 2 but 0-13 since; held by Shes Perfect and Arisaig on latest Goodwood form

races.race_name: Coolmore Coronation Cup (Group 1)

runners.name: Calandagan
runners.spotlight: Superstar gelding who has a record of 222211111 since racing at Group 1 level and is the highest-rated racehorse in the world; couldn't quite get past Jan Brueghel in this race last year but turned the tables in the King George and is on an impressive roll going into another rematch with that rival; prevailed at Meydan (Sheema Classic) last time despite the race not panning out ideally; duly strongly respected, assuming the ground is okay (not certain to run if it's a slog scenario).
runners.comment: Second in this race last year; has since become the world's highest-rated racehorse

runners.name: Jan Brueghel
runners.spotlight: High-class performer who has a record of 6-8 overall and 2-3 at Group 1 level, adding to his 2024 St Leger win when knuckling down the better to beat Calandagan in this contest (good to soft) 12 months ago; combination of cheekpieces and setting an unsuitably steady pace perhaps contributed to his King George defeat behind that rival; reappearance success in the Ormonde at Chester (1m5f, good) sets him up nicely for another Coronation Cup bid.
runners.comment: High-class horse who knuckled down the better to beat Calandagan in this race last year
runners.owner: Westerberg & Mrs Magnier & M Tabor & D Smith

runners.name: Bay City Roller
runners.spotlight: Consistent colt whose Group 1 form is non-domestic, namely a success in Germany (1m4f, soft) last November and good second at the Curragh (10.5f, good) latest start; only a neck behind Lambourn on worse terms at Chester on reappearance, albeit with that rival rusty and likely to improve for the run, according to Ryan Moore; needs a personal best to land this prize.
runners.comment: Consistent but his Group 1 form is non-domestic; needs a personal best to win this race

runners.name: Lambourn
runners.spotlight: Most wins on good ground but has form on soft; held on by only a neck from Bay City Roller on better terms in the Huxley (1m2f Group 2) at Chester on seasonal debut but proved game in first-time cheekpieces and, based on Ryan Moore's post-race comments, will come on for the run; notable contender in the retained headgear returned to the scene of his biggest moment, namely a clearcut success in last year's Derby (followed up in the Irish version).
runners.comment: Notable contender returned to the scene of his biggest moment, namely 2025 Derby win
runners.owner: Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith

runners.name: Illinois
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in two Cup races last term; only a neck behind stablemate Jan Brueghel in the 2024 St Leger but was beaten about 5l by that opponent last month when bidding for a second success in the Ormonde at Chester, doing nothing to close their current ratings differential; perhaps in here to set the pace and ensure a proper test.
runners.comment: One of three Ballydoyle runners; perhaps in here to set the pace and ensure a proper test
runners.owner: Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor

runners.name: Convergent
runners.spotlight: Scored on soft ground on debut; flopped in the German race won by Bay City Roller but otherwise progressive and overcame trouble to defy a Group 2 penalty in Group 3 contest at Newbury (1m4f, good) on seasonal debut, taking record to 5-8; about 3l behind Lambourn at Chester last term but is now in the same ball park as that opponent on ratings, with a chance of further improvement to boot; adds great interest to this race.
runners.comment: Overcame trouble to win at Newbury on reappearance; progressive and adds great interest

races.race_name: Betfred 'Dash' Handicap (Heritage Handicap)

runners.name: Kinswoman
runners.spotlight: Progressive when dropped to 5f in the second half of 2025, winning a Windsor novice (good to firm) and adding handicap wins at Chester (good to firm) and Haydock (good); unable to justify odds-on favouritism at Yarmouth (5f, good to soft) 24 days ago on her return from seven months off but it was still a solid effort and she remains capable of better; another 3lb higher today but sure to be popular.
runners.comment: Progressive in 2025 and reappearance was encouraging; up in weights but better to come

runners.name: Ziggy's Triton
runners.spotlight: Saw off 11 rivals when scoring at Musselburgh in April (5f, good; Class 4) but he has had his limitations exposed in stronger company twice since; beaten only 3l at York two weeks ago but others are more appealing in today's field.
runners.comment: Musselburgh win in April came in Class 4; held in stronger company twice since

runners.name: Dyonisos
runners.spotlight: Yet to win for Ian Williams but he's down in the weights and has run well in two of his last three starts, pipped at Goodwood last month (5f, good); wouldn't be sure to confirm those placings with Dream Composer this time.
runners.comment: Second in two of his last three; not sure to confirm latest placing with Dream Composer

runners.name: Betsen
runners.spotlight: 6f AW win on his stable debut in November; added to his tally when seeing off five rivals at Chepstow 16 days ago (Class 4; 5f, good to soft); in deeper today.
runners.comment: Drop back to 5f saw him win a Class 4 at Chepstow last month; in much deeper today

runners.name: Almaty Star
runners.spotlight: Better on AW than turf but took advantage of a good opportunity at Yarmouth in April (5f, good); hit with an 8lb rise for that, bringing his turf and AW marks into line; blew the start back at Yarmouth last month but the stewards deemed him a non-runner due to having his head turned as the stalls opened; risky on the back of that and there are other pace influences to contend with today.
runners.comment: Better on AW; in a much deeper race than the one he won at Yarmouth eight weeks ago

runners.name: Star Chorus
runners.spotlight: In good form on AW for Mike Murphy this winter, winning twice at Southwell (5f/6f); slowly away (blindfold removed late) at Goodwood two weeks ago; looks vulnerable at this level and goes without accessories this time.
runners.comment: Two AW wins this winter; slowly away at Goodwood latest; vulnerable at this level

runners.name: Arklow Lad
runners.spotlight: 2-4 since joining Mick Appleby, notably a Class 2 handicap win at Pontefract (5f, good to soft; cheekpieces first time) in April; solid run at York three weeks ago and one for whom rain would be no concern.
runners.comment: 2-4 for Mick Appleby; solid effort at York last month; one to consider

runners.name: Cindy Lou Who
runners.spotlight: Progressive in 2025 and she took her form to a new level when second of 14 in a Bath Listed event on her reappearance (5f, good); not in the same form at Newbury (6f, good) when last seen seven weeks ago and she is now 11lb higher; others look stronger.
runners.comment: Bath 2nd on return was a fine effort but disappointing last time and now 11lb higher

runners.name: Rhythm N Hooves
runners.spotlight: Admirable campaigner who is gradually edging down the weights; type to pop up soon but perhaps in a slightly less competitive affair.
runners.comment: Handicapped to win soon but this race might prove a shade too competitive

runners.name: Lexington Blitz
runners.spotlight: Picked off late in the 3yo Dash 12 months ago; gelded prior to his seasonal return and he has run well on both starts in 2026, following his close third at Musselburgh with an impressive win at Goodwood five weeks ago (5f, good); a 9lb rise asks a stiffer question but he is progressive.
runners.comment: Second at this meeting last year; impressed at Goodwood latest; up 9lb but on the upgrade

runners.name: Dream Composer
runners.spotlight: Trained by James Evans when winning this race in 2024 off a 5lb higher mark (good to soft); well beaten in it last year but he arrives in good form for his new stable, winning at Ripon in April and not enjoying the cleanest run when fourth at Goodwood 15 days ago; rain won't hurt his chance and he's of obvious interest.
runners.comment: Two good runs since joining Tony Carroll; won this race in 2024 off 5lb higher; big player

runners.name: Eclairage
runners.spotlight: All four wins have come on the AW at Dundalk and she wasn't beaten far in a Group 3 there on her final run in 2025; fully effective on turf and ran a cracker at Navan three weeks ago, showing her trademark early speed before being picked off late on (front two pulled 6l clear); 8lb higher today but this track should play to her strengths; chance.
runners.comment: Speedy front-runner; fine second at Navan 3 weeks ago; 8lb higher but foolish to discount

runners.name: King Of Light
runners.spotlight: Won a 5f handicap (good to soft; first time in tongue-tie/cheekpieces combination) at Haydock last September off a 6lb lower mark; stayed on for second behind the impressive Lexington Blitz at Goodwood on last month's return; withdrawn at York two weeks ago having got upset in the stalls (9-1 at the time of withdrawal); the more rain the better; no accessories today.
runners.comment: Encouraging seasonal return at Goodwood; sharper today and the more rain the better

runners.name: Rogue Enforcer
runners.spotlight: Suited by good ground or slower; won a 5f Listed race in Italy on his final start for a French yard; big prices and finished down the field in two 5f handicaps last month; dropped 7lb for his troubles but he arrives with a point to prove.
runners.comment: Listed win in Italy last October; well held at big prices in two starts for his new stable

runners.name: Emperor Spirit
runners.spotlight: Appreciated the return to turf when finishing second of 17 at Ascot five weeks ago; 1lb rise still leaves him on a feasible mark; rarely seen on slower than good.
runners.comment: Good 2nd at Ascot on last month's return to turf; rain would be a concern

runners.name: Stormy Impact
runners.spotlight: In 2025 she took a big step forward from her seasonal return when sweeping through late to collar Lexington Blitz in the 3yo Dash on this card; also won at Wolverhampton in July (6f, AW) off 1lb higher than today; never a threat at York last month on return to action but it's not unreasonable to expect much better today and she is sure to get a strong pace to aim at.
runners.comment: Fast finish to peg back Lexington Blitz in 3yo Dash in 2025; can leave reappearance behind

runners.name: Brazen Bolt
runners.spotlight: Won big-field 5f handicaps at York and Goodwood last July; two solid efforts in defeat this season and he won on his only visit to Epsom, albeit over 7f; not impossibly treated but he wouldn't want the ground to be any slower than good.
runners.comment: Two solid efforts this year and 1-1 at Epsom; suited by good/firmer

runners.name: Democracy Dilemma
runners.spotlight: Front-runner; second in this race in 2024 off 3lb higher; ran away with a 5f handicap at Haydock (good ground) last September off this mark; unable to hit the same heights since but his AW third in February was a fair run; won't be inconvenienced should there be a marked deterioration in the going but a full revival is required; visor returns having been left off latest.
runners.comment: Second here in 2024 off 3lb higher; has not found very best in 2026; needs a full revival

runners.name: Vintage Clarets
runners.spotlight: Prolific winner down the years and he arrives off an 11lb lower mark than when fifth in this 12 months ago; yet to shine this year (poor draw at Chester on penultimate start) but it wouldn't be a huge shock to see him fare better today.
runners.comment: Prolific winner down the years; on a dangerous mark and revival isn't out of the question

runners.name: Another Baar
runners.spotlight: Acts on any ground; having dropped in the weights he enjoyed a revival last August/September, rattling off a four-timer (5f-5.3f); had a stint in Bahrain over the winter, unplaced on four occasions but recording a career best when winning a 5f event (good to firm) in January; looks on a tough mark back in Britain.
runners.comment: Reeled off a four-timer last term; 5f win in Bahrain in January; looks high in weights

races.race_name: Betfred Derby (Group 1) (No Geldings)

runners.name: Item
runners.spotlight: Well on top last September in a maiden at Kempton (7f, AW) and novice at Bath (1m, good to firm); non-runner in Group 1 Futurity due to heavy going; not the easiest to settle when reappearing in Group 2 Dante at York (10.2f, good; 11-2) last month but he mastered front-running Action in the last half furlong to win it well, putting Christmas Day in the shade as well; settling a bit better (fine as 2yo) would cement claims for 1m4f but this son of Frankel won going away and brings clear potential, plus leading form and an unbeaten record.
runners.comment: Settling a bit better would help but he won going away in Group 2 Dante at York (10.2f)

runners.name: Benvenuto Cellini
runners.spotlight: Frankel colt who was dominant in a maiden last July, a Group 2 at Leopardstown (1m) in September and Group 3 Chester Vase (12.5f, good) one month ago; his free-flowing, long-striding action was seen to good effect in those races, but not on heavy ground when third in the Group 1 Futurity at Doncaster (1m) in October; soft ground would also introduce something for him to prove but otherwise he brings strength of stamina and among the best form; if the ground is okay, a bold show looks assured.
runners.comment: Stays well, good form; something to prove on soft/heavy, otherwise bold show looks assured

runners.name: Maltese Cross
runners.spotlight: Debut was on soft; all four of his races were tight finishes and he won the last three; pulled it out of the fire against useful rivals in a valuable novice event at Newbury (1m2f, good) and narrowly maintained an advantage over Bay Of Brilliance as the pair drew clear in a ding-dong battle for the Lingfield Derby Trial (11.6f, good to firm) four weeks ago; his pedigree is all about 1m4f, including with his sire and dam's sire both Derby winners, and he also shapes as if every yard today will suit; needs to pull out more again but has to be in each-way calculations.
runners.comment: Won his last three; pedigree and performance are all about 1m4f; firmly in the e-w picture

runners.name: Action
runners.spotlight: Closely related to 2025 Derby winner Lambourn; carries his head high but ended last season with a close, battling second in the Group 1 Futurity at Doncaster (1m, heavy) and front-running second in the Dante at York (10.2f, good) last month was of a similar order, albeit no match for Item in the final 1f; a disappointing, sluggish display on reappearance was his only start with tongue tied; likely to stay 1m4f, with a possible place role but it's unlikely to be more unless the new cheekpieces have a major positive effect.
runners.comment: 2nd in 2yo Group 1 (heavy) and to Item in Dante; carries head high and cheekpieces go on
runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Pierre Bonnard
runners.spotlight: Asserted late on to finish firmly on top last October in a Group 3 at Newmarket (good to firm) and Group 1 Criterium de Saint-Cloud (very soft) and they were over 1m2f; 3yo campaign got off to a muted start but he briefly hit the front in Derby Trial at Leopardstown before James J Braddock swooped and Aidan O'Brien has stressed that he went gently with him at home until after that race; his sire and dam's sire were Derby winners (dam won 1m2f Group 1 Nassau) and he now moves up from 1m2f; he looked every inch a serious Derby contender last term and that's probably still the impression to go with.
runners.comment: Looked every inch a serious Derby contender as 2yo and that impression still holds sway

runners.name: Christmas Day
runners.spotlight: Maiden success was on heavy going; Group 3 Ballysax at Leopardstown (1m2f, good to yielding) on April reappearance made it three wins in a row and his Dante third at York (10.2f, good) next time was at least as good on form; while lacking anything like the same dash as winner Item at York, and also unable to get to grips with stablemate Action, he did plug on as if 1m4f could help; it clearly needs to.
runners.comment: Put in his place by Item in the Dante at York, albeit plugging on as if 1m4f could help

runners.name: Balzac
runners.spotlight: Novice winner whose rallying third of six in Listed race here (1m2f, good) in April suggested that longer trips would suit; tried 11.6f in the Lingfield Derby Trial (good to firm) and recorded his highest rating, but Maltese Cross and Bay Of Brilliance were just over 6l ahead of him; major work to do.
runners.comment: Lingfield Derby Trial 3rd but Maltese Cross and Bay Of Brilliance were just over 6l ahead

runners.name: Bay Of Brilliance
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to smart Flat and hurdle winner Absurde; odds-on winner of a maiden at Goodwood (1m2f, soft) and novice at Redcar (1m1f, good to soft; made all) on last two starts as 2yo; pushed Maltese Cross all the way in Lingfield Derby Trial (11.6f, good to firm) and could improve on that first-time-out display; more to do but he's heading the right way.
runners.comment: Pushed Maltese Cross all the way in the Lingfield Derby Trial (11.6f) on reappearance

runners.name: James J Braddock
runners.spotlight: Second start as 2yo was a 6l win in Curragh maiden (1m, heavy); never able to challenge on return in the Group 3 Ballysax won by Christmas Day and couldn't have left it any later in the Derby Trial back over Leopardstown's 1m2f (good) four weeks ago, but upstaging Pierre Bonnard so dramatically in the latter reinforces the feeling that 1m4f will suit him well; his sire's sole Group 1 win was at this trip; each-way player for trainer and jockey who won the Oaks yesterday.
runners.comment: Swooped on Pierre Bonnard in the 1m2f Leopardstown Derby Trial and 1m4f will suit him well
runners.owner: Aziz Kheir Aust Partners & Kevin Blake

runners.name: A Taste Of Glory
runners.spotlight: Won a Brighton maiden and 1m2f AW handicap at Lingfield; however, he ended last season with last of ten in a French Group 1 (1m2f, very soft) and subsided rapidly to be last of six in the Lingfield Derby Trial (11.6f, good to firm; 33-1); his owner is renowned for Derby contenders who have run well at big prices, but hopes for this one may be stretching it.
runners.comment: 33-1, subsided rapidly to be last of six in Lingfield Derby Trial (11.6f, good to firm)

runners.name: Ancient Egypt
runners.spotlight: Frankel colt bought for 1,100,000gns as a yearling; all four races on good to firm; Beverley and Goodwood novice wins last summer before the Group 2 Royal Lodge saw a comprehensive end to his unbeaten record; he returned to Newmarket, however, with an ultimately authoritative win in a five-runner Listed race (1m2f) five weeks ago, helping to make the running; 1m4f beckons strongly after that and he's included in each-way calculations.
runners.comment: 1m4f beckons strongly after Newmarket Listed win, putting him in each-way calculations

runners.name: Alderman
runners.spotlight: Travelled well and challenged from the rear at Newbury (1m3f, good; 17-2) on reappearance seven weeks ago, sticking to his task manfully too while chasing home an exciting prospect at a respectful distance; a promising, scopey sort from a famous middle-distance family but his progressive form in maidens, without a win, does not make him a serious Derby contender.
runners.comment: Promising, scopey sort from famous middle-distance family but he's been running in maidens

runners.name: Rebel Rocker
runners.spotlight: Strong late on to grab a maiden at Kempton (1m, AW) in November and came from last to second in the six-runner Listed Trial here (1m2f, good; 33-1) in April; hopes for the latter form seem to have been punctured and although he looks capable of better and shapes as if 1m4f will suit him well, 1m4f in something other than the Derby would seem much more realistic.
runners.comment: 2nd in the Listed Trial here; shapes as if 1m4f will suit him well but faces a stiff task

runners.name: Poker
runners.spotlight: Having fetched 4,300,000gns in 2024, he is the most expensive yearling colt ever sold at auction in Europe; by Wootton Bassett out of a sister to Oaks winner Was; on the other hand, he was left nearly 9l behind by Bay Of Brilliance at Redcar (1m1f, good to soft) in October and sported cheekpieces at 10-1 when a front-running second of four to a stablemate in Haydock novice (1m4f, good to firm) in April; admirably tenacious in the latter but the dream was once of so much more.
runners.comment: Front-running second of four in a 1m4f Haydock novice in April, so has a mountain to climb

races.race_name: Cherryfield (Croydon) Lester Piggott Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Folk Pageant
runners.spotlight: Second on good to soft on her only 2yo start; last of nine on handicap debut at Southwell (1m, AW) in April but won her other three starts this season, in 1m2f handicaps at Newmarket and Chester on last two occasions; 6lb rise is a sterner reaction since latest start but she's seen those last two races out strongly and could have more to give; major player.
runners.comment: Saw it out well when winning 1m2f handicaps at Newmarket and Chester on last two starts
runners.owner: Sheikh Hamdan bin Mohammed Al Maktoum

runners.name: Allegresse
runners.spotlight: Won from the front in two Kempton novices (1m, AW); weakened out of it in a Group 3 at Newmarket (1m2f) on final 2yo run and mastered by his three rivals late on in a conditions stakes at Goodwood (1m, good; unraced on softer) on reappearance, so he does not present the most glittering case for this handicap debut, but stamina in family suggests he should stay.
runners.comment: Limitations on last two starts; handicap debut; stamina in family suggests he should stay

runners.name: York Tower
runners.spotlight: Second over 7f at Yarmouth (good) and Chelmsford (AW) on his only two starts as 2yo; respectable third to Savvy Disko in novice at Lingfield (1m, AW) on return last month; handicap newcomer, with plenty to suggest that 1m2f+ will suit and best days are ahead of him.
runners.comment: Handicap newcomer; plenty suggests that 1m2f+ will suit and best days are ahead of him

runners.name: Pendella
runners.spotlight: Shaped like a non-stayer over 1m4f at York on turf debut in May, but reeled in only late in the day when second of ten there over 1m2f (again good ground; raised 2lb today) nine days later; ridden by Jack Nicholls last time and that performance puts her on the shortlist.
runners.comment: Reeled in late in the day when second of ten at York (1m2f, good) last time; raised 2lb

runners.name: Silver State
runners.spotlight: Usually ridden by Rose Dawes; won four of seven handicaps, all once visored, including from the front over 1m at Bath (good to soft) and Doncaster when returned to turf; helped to set a strong gallop when fifth of ten at Haydock latest but he needs to resume improvement and this new trip is not sure to help.
runners.comment: Needs to resume improvement after helping to set strong pace in bid for 1m turf hat-trick

runners.name: Savvy Disko
runners.spotlight: Minor honours at Newmarket (7f, good) in October on first two starts; 7-1 for 12-runner novice at Lingfield (1m, AW; York Tower third) last month and always close up to win by a neck; getting up in the final strides there boosts hope for 1m2f (despite pedigree) and he's a promising newcomer to handicaps.
runners.comment: Won Lingfield novice (1m, AW) last month, always close up and leading in final strides

runners.name: Lake Como
runners.spotlight: Unraced on softer than good; found serious improvement over 7f/1m on AW but, although running respectably, he's not transferred the best of that form to 1m turf handicaps on last two outings; worth a go at this new trip.
runners.comment: Has not transferred best AW form to two 1m turf handicaps; worth a go at this new trip

runners.name: Starlight Time
runners.spotlight: Won a small-field maiden here (7f, good) last July on debut; well held in eighth of 26 in a valuable sales race at Newmarket (7f, good) but he was on his own through the final 1f when making most to win at 1-2 in a novice at Chelmsford (1m, AW) in November; 1m2f is plausible and he should bring potential to this seasonal and handicap debut.
runners.comment: 2-3 as 2yo; 1m2f plausible and he should bring potential to this seasonal/handicap debut

runners.name: Hell Yeah He Did
runners.spotlight: All three turf races as 2yo were on soft or heavy, making most and rallying stoutly to win a maiden at Nottingham (1m) on final outing; gelded since; seemingly no doubt that he will still be suited by 1m2f+ and nothing to prove if the ground is testing; one to note on seasonal/handicap debut.
runners.comment: Will be suited by 1m2f+ and nothing to prove if ground is testing; seasonal/handicap debut

runners.name: Tailgunner Joe
runners.spotlight: Better in Meydan campaign in December-March, including a 1m win and he shaped as if he stayed 1m1f on final start there; needs to rebound from a poor show at Newmarket (1m2f) in April and he was gelded the next day.
runners.comment: Needs to rebound from a poor show (1m2f) in April and he was gelded the following day

races.race_name: HKJC World Pool Northern Dancer Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Gordon Grey
runners.spotlight: The second runner for local trainer George Baker and this one has an enviable 3-4 record here, having picked up two Ladies' Derbys (2024 and 2025) in addition to the Amateurs' Derby last year when trained by Ben Brookhouse; not discounted on that score but this is a good bit hotter than those events; refitted tongue-tie could be a plus.
runners.comment: Won three "Derbys" over C&D; not discounted on that score but this is a good bit hotter

runners.name: Too Soon
runners.spotlight: Housed in a stable that rarely misidentifies its geese as swans, so the fact he's been highly tried on occasions is interesting; 1-14 record is therefore frustrating but the win did come here as a juvenile (8.5f, heavy) and he gets first-time cheekpieces today; suspicion he's capable of a big run with Warren Fentiman an unusual booking for the yard.
runners.comment: Sole win came here (8.5f, heavy); cheekpieces go on and he may be capable of a big run

runners.name: Lord Melbourne
runners.spotlight: Won 23-runner November Handicap (1m4f, soft) in 2024 and he's 2-3 here (1m2f and 1m4f) including a win on good to soft last September; those are the clear positives but he's been poor in three starts at 1m2f (snow/AW/good) this year; has a tempting mark if he can stage a revival, so needs a market check.
runners.comment: 2-3 here but poor this year; tempting mark if he can stage a revival; market check needed

runners.name: Spinning Wheel
runners.spotlight: Unraced as a juvenile but now boasts 3-6 record, winning his first two and back on the scoresheet when making all at Leicester (1m4f, good to soft) in October; bumped into a quite well-regarded sort when second at Goodwood (1m6f, good) a fortnight ago and this unexposed 4yo looks a big contender to build on that encouraging return.
runners.comment: Boasts 3-6 record; unexposed and looks a big contender to build on his encouraging return
runners.owner: Sheikh Hamdan bin Mohammed Al Maktoum

runners.name: Give It To Me Oj
runners.spotlight: Acts on soft; can make the running; won six (four over hurdles, two on Flat) of seven starts in a golden spell last February-October, including over C&D in September in both the Jump Jockeys Derby (soft) and Apprentices' Derby (good); recent run over 2m at Goodwood should have teed him up nicely for what could be a plan from last year's winning yard; considered.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner; recent run over 2m should have teed him up nicely for this; considered

runners.name: Elsass
runners.spotlight: Previously susceptible to the odd stinker but he's 2-2 since being gelded, scoring at Southwell (AW) in April and Hamilton (again 1m3f, good) three weeks ago; 8lb rise in a much tougher contest demands more but maybe he's putting it all together now.
runners.comment: 2-2 since being gelded; 8lb rise demands more but maybe he's putting it all together now

runners.name: Regal Ulixes
runners.spotlight: Won on soft (1m) as a 2yo; gelded after just two starts last year (included 1m2f win) and he scored off 5lb lower on AW in February; various excuses in three subsequent outings when he frequently looked as though this step back up in trip is well worth another go; respected.
runners.comment: Various excuses last three starts and looks like this trip is worth a go again; respected

runners.name: Bulletin
runners.spotlight: Extraordinarily consistent sort who is yet to finish out of the frame in 15 starts and his profile isn't short of improvement either; stays this trip, acts on good to soft (unraced on soft) and he also ran well on his only visit here; very likeable and should go well again.
runners.comment: Not out of the frame in 15 starts; acts on good to soft and ran well on sole visit here

runners.name: Hengest
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo who has swiftly found his feet for this yard; built on a big run in novice here (8.5f, good) in April by going unbeaten in his three handicaps (1m2f and 11.5f, good/good to firm), penalised on the last two occasions; now bumped up markedly in grade (2lb wrong) and softer ground would pose a query but there's no telling where this improvement will end up.
runners.comment: Lightly raced and unbeaten in three handicaps; ground a possible query but he's on a roll
runners.owner: Gee Gees Racing, P Gleeson & N Barnes

runners.name: Asgard's Captain
runners.spotlight: Track regular who finished third in this race (good to soft) in 2024 and stayed on from the rear to be fourth in last year's edition; also won last season's Great Metropolitan over C&D (good) and got back in the winning groove when taking Newmarket handicap three weeks ago; latter success puts him on a career-high mark but he's unlikely to be far away.
runners.comment: Goes well here and won at Newmarket latest; career-high mark but unlikely to be far away

runners.name: Antrim
runners.spotlight: Often a front-runner; 3-7 on AW but he's 0-4 on turf and latest third to Elsass at Hamilton (1m3f, good) was no more than respectable; untested on good to soft or worse but his pedigree may given some encouragement, as his dam is closely related to outstanding performer Enable whose 15 wins included the 2017 Oaks over C&D and four of her Group 1 wins were on softer than good.
runners.comment: 0-4 on turf and latest third (1m3f, good) was just respectable; softer ground a query

runners.name: Night Breeze
runners.spotlight: Finished fifth in this last year and it was just his second visit to Epsom when impressively landing the five-runner Great Metropolitan over C&D (good) in April; 4lb rise (arguably could have been worse) pushes him into uncharted territory but he has won on good to soft and is a player under Billy Loughnane.
runners.comment: Impressed over C&D (good) in April; 4lb rise arguably could have been worse; player

runners.name: Prydwen
runners.spotlight: Prolific 8yo who gained his 11th success when taking 1m4f Ripon handicap (good) in April; got a 2lb nudge after that and received a further 3lb rise after finishing fifth in 2m Group 2 in Germany four weeks ago; while that's not ideal, he's won off higher marks and won't mind how the ground is riding.
runners.comment: Edging up the weights again but has won off higher and won't mind how the ground is riding

runners.name: Dancing In Paris
runners.spotlight: He hasn't won since August 2024 but ran some fine races in defeat last season, including when second of 14 at Glorious Goodwood (1m6f, good to soft) in August; those efforts have ensured his mark is holding up, however, and he makes his comeback over a trip probably short of his best these days.
runners.comment: No win last term but ran some fine races; returns over a trip probably short of his best

runners.name: Military Academy
runners.spotlight: 1m4f AW Listed winner (also acts on good to soft and softer) for the Gosdens but he left that yard under a cloud in the autumn; rediscovered some form at 1m2f on AW this winter but he's been below par on his last three starts; soft ground would enhance his cause but others are more solid.
runners.comment: Below par on last three starts; soft ground would enhance his cause, but others more solid

runners.name: Max Mayhem
runners.spotlight: Locally trained runner who offered something to work on when seventh of 13 at Kempton (1m4f, AW) on his British return ten days ago; also has form on good to soft but latest effort still leaves him with work to do and he finished tailed off in the 2023 running of this.
runners.comment: Not disgraced latest but that still leaves work to do; tailed off in the 2023 renewal

races.race_name: JRA Tokyo Trophy Handicap

runners.name: Gold Star Hero
runners.spotlight: Record of 4-5 on AW features a hat-trick since being gelded; chance depends on whether he can transfer the form back to turf; only 1-11 in this sphere and he is 16lb higher than last attempt.
runners.comment: Record of 4-5 on AW features a hat-trick since being gelded; only 1-11 on turf

runners.name: Sondad
runners.spotlight: Versatile as regards ground; broadly consistent form last term featured a success (off 7lb lower) in this contest; likely needed his York reappearance; interesting back here with that outing under his belt.
runners.comment: Won this race last year; interesting back here with York reappearance under his belt

runners.name: Eye Of Dubai
runners.spotlight: Has the ability to take a serious hand but he ran well below market expectations in this race last year and another reappearance duck egg doesn't augur well; has shown peak form in autumn months.
runners.comment: Ran below market expectations in this race last year; best form in autumn months

runners.name: Fine Interview
runners.spotlight: Well suited by soft/good to soft ground; has a record of 3-4 since wearing cheekpieces, most recently winning at Doncaster (6f) ten weeks ago; respected in the retained headgear, with further progress possible.
runners.comment: Record of 3-4 since wearing cheekpieces; respected with further progress possible

runners.name: Topwarrior
runners.spotlight: Record of 2-9 in his first season last year, winning in blinkers at Newbury (6f, soft) final outing; may improve further this term; a possible danger, provided the removal of headgear doesn't backfire.
runners.comment: May improve further, provided the removal of blinkers doesn't backfire; reappearance

runners.name: Badri
runners.spotlight: The oldest contender in this field but he's still quite useful and prevailed at Redcar (6f) most recently; record in this race includes a success in 2023 (off 6lb higher) and 1l third in 2025; may remain competitive.
runners.comment: Record in this contest includes a success in 2023 and 1l third in 2025

runners.name: Kylian
runners.spotlight: Form has dipped in last two starts, taking record for current yard to 0-5; doesn't look as if he's about to prevail in this warm affair.
runners.comment: 0-5 for current yard; doesn't look as if he's about to prevail in this warm race
runners.owner: Middleham Park Racing Xii & Mrs G Cowell

runners.name: Saint Lawrence
runners.spotlight: Proved resurgent with close second at Ascot (6f, good) on stable/seasonal debut; defied a stone higher mark in the Wokingham three years ago and has possibilities even though he hasn't won since.
runners.comment: Resurgent at Ascot on stable/seasonal debut; won the 2023 Wokingham off a stone higher

runners.name: Roman Dragon
runners.spotlight: Has gained all eight domestic wins at Chester, the most recent on penultimate start; Epsom is an unknown and he's on a difficult mark, conceding weight all round to boot.
runners.comment: Has gained all domestic wins at Chester; Epsom is an unknown and mark is difficult

runners.name: Partisan Hero
runners.spotlight: Unproven on soft/heavy ground; has got faster over time and prevailed narrowly at York (5f, good) most recently, registering a first sprint win; peaking at the right time and record of 2-2 at Epsom (7f) includes a success at this meeting 12 months ago; major player.
runners.comment: Peaking at the right time; record of 2-2 at Epsom includes a win at this meeting last year

runners.name: Strike Red
runners.spotlight: Has lost his form a bit in two starts since an April success and is far from certain to raise his game much, having been beaten in this race for the last three years (finishing positions 756).
runners.comment: Beaten in this race for last three years (form figures 756)

runners.name: Solar Aclaim
runners.spotlight: Two duck eggs on good ground this term; ideally needs soft/heavy, so chance depends on the weather; favourably handicapped off 4lb below last winning mark.
runners.comment: Ideally needs soft/heavy; favourably treated off 4lb below last winning mark

runners.name: Purest Time
runners.spotlight: Prevailed at Windsor (6f, good to firm) most recently, proving suited by the return to turf from AW and belatedly registering a first win since joining Ian Williams, who has landed this prize twice in the last decade; one for the shortlist kept to this sphere.
runners.comment: Suited by the return to turf last time, winning at Windsor; one for the shortlist

runners.name: Twilight Jet
runners.spotlight: Won by 4l last Saturday to complete a Lingfield turf double; raised a whopping 12lb but is still well treated on best form, which includes close second (off 4lb higher) in this contest in 2025; fighting chance.
runners.comment: Close second in this race in 2025; has won last two starts; fighting chance

runners.name: Invictus Gold
runners.spotlight: Useful sort whose latest effort is best forgiven (chance compromised by early trouble); however, he has something to prove away from Newmarket, where most of his turf form lies.
runners.comment: Useful sort but has something to prove away from Newmarket

runners.name: Apollo One
runners.spotlight: Acts on any ground; ran okay behind younger rivals at Newmarket last time; losing run is mounting up but he has been placed in this race twice, finishing second in 2023 and third in 2024; again has each-way hopes.
runners.comment: Placed twice in this contest, finishing second in 2023 and third in 2024

races.race_name: Edinburgh Gin Rhubarb And Ginger Handicap

runners.name: Wee Mary
runners.spotlight: Just one win to her name but she comes here in good order, most recently bumping into a well-handicapped opponent at Hamilton eight days ago (5f, good); should be in the thick of it again.
runners.comment: Infrequent winner but she arrives in good form and should be in the thick of it again

runners.name: Classy Clarets
runners.spotlight: Two wins for Iain Jardine this year, the latest at Hamilton last month off a 3lb lower mark; good second over C&D 16 days ago; makes plenty of appeal.
runners.comment: Two wins this year and latest C&D second was a good effort; solid contender

runners.name: Fear And Fast
runners.spotlight: 18-race maiden; dropping down the weights but his two runs this year have seen him finish last at Ayr and over C&D; hood replaces a visor today; likely to pop up at some point but stable have more obvious claims with Wee Mary.
runners.comment: 0-18 and he's finished last in both runs this year; change of headgear today; not solid
runners.owner: Let's Be Lucky Racing 38 & Steven Wilson

runners.name: Albegone
runners.spotlight: Finished behind Wee Mary when fifth of ten at Carlisle last week; best on good or slower ground so wouldn't be ideally suited by drying conditions.
runners.comment: On a dangerous mark but drying conditions wouldn't be in his favour
runners.owner: D B & Mrs C Lamplough & Partner

runners.name: Royal Duke
runners.spotlight: Two of his three wins have come over C&D, the latest last August off a 4lb higher mark; not found his best in two runs this year but he's the type to pop up at some point.
runners.comment: Two-time C&D winner; on a good mark but yet to shine so far this year

runners.name: Sixcor
runners.spotlight: Conditions no issue but neither run last month suggested he was the answer to today's puzzle.
runners.comment: Conditions no problem but neither run last month suggested he was the answer here

runners.name: Hard Nut
runners.spotlight: His only win since July 2019 came over this C&D but it is easy enough to look elsewhere now he returns from a 213-day break.
runners.comment: Just one win since July 2019; easy to swerve after 213 days off

races.race_name: Edinburgh Gin Hugo Spritz Selling Stakes

runners.name: Furturra
runners.spotlight: Finished second in a C&D maiden last month; similar form when third in a 6f seller at Chester last week; solid claims once again for a stable also represented by Turnstile.
runners.comment: Placed on his last two starts, including C&D; leading form claims once again; yard run two

runners.name: Turnstile
runners.spotlight: No improvement for 6f on his last two starts; shouldn't be inconvenienced by returning to 5f and he has the rail to help him; one of two leading contenders for Tim Easterby.
runners.comment: Not at his best over 6f last time; down into a seller and looks a key player back at 5f

runners.name: Dee's Star
runners.spotlight: She has shown some speed in a couple of 5f fillies' novice events to date, finishing in midfield on both occasions; drops into a seller and should have a more realistic chance today.
runners.comment: She has shown enough in two fillies' novices to give her a shout now dropped into a seller

runners.name: Havana Gift
runners.spotlight: Her Thirsk debut wasn't without encouragement but she failed to build on it at Southwell 11 days later; improvement essential.
runners.comment: Her Thirsk debut was OK but failed to build on it on AW 11 days later; improvement needed

runners.name: Tree Wizard
runners.spotlight: Down the field in a 5f maiden at Bath on debut; improved when second of seven in a Beverley claimer ten days ago (5f, good to firm; cheekpieces on for the first time); further progress will be required but it is not out of the question.
runners.comment: Second in Beverley claimer on latest start; can do better but the needs to if he's to win

runners.name: Tango Hotel
runners.spotlight: No match for a smart filly on her Beverley debut in April (5f, good to soft) but it was still a fair run and the form has received some boosts; could be capable of a fair bit better.
runners.comment: Debut fourth has been franked since; drops into a seller and can do much better

runners.name: Needin' U
runners.spotlight: 16-1 into 4-1 ahead of her Redcar debut 19 days ago (6f, good) but having shown some pace she dropped away in the final furlong; down to 5f/selling company so interesting to see if her supporters return.
runners.comment: Market springer on Redcar debut (6f, good) but dropped away late on; down in trip/grade

races.race_name: Edinburgh Gin Classic Handicap

runners.name: Modern Times
runners.spotlight: Maiden winner who has had lots of chances in handicaps but ran well to be sixth of 22 at York (good), running on over 7f; drawn in 1 and can race prominently; considered.
runners.comment: Had lots of chances in handicaps but ran well to be sixth of 22 at York (good)

runners.name: Yaaser
runners.spotlight: Won over this trip on the AW in December but has been out the back in subsequent races.
runners.comment: Won over this trip on the AW in December but has been out the back in subsequent races

runners.name: Black Storm
runners.spotlight: AW winner in Ireland; mostly consistent in defeat for current yard, after wind surgery in last two races; this is his trip and unlikely to be too far away.
runners.comment: AW winner in Ireland; mostly consistent in defeat for current yard; this is his trip

runners.name: Abduction
runners.spotlight: Multiple winner who won over C&D in April and ran pretty well last time at Hamilton as 6f is too sharp these days; still on a competitive mark; chance.
runners.comment: C&D winner in April and ran pretty well last time at Hamilton as 6f is too sharp

runners.name: Zubaru
runners.spotlight: Mark took a hit for back-to-back wins (7f, good to firm) but only went down by a head at Yarmouth and now just 1lb higher; hard to knock, other than being drawn wide.
runners.comment: Handicap mark has taken a hit but he's still hard to knock in current form

runners.name: Royalty Bay
runners.spotlight: Should be on a good mark but last year was mostly underwhelming and so too her comeback effort over C&D; opposable.
runners.comment: Last year was mostly underwhelming and so too her comeback effort over C&D

runners.name: Mae Amor
runners.spotlight: Only scraped home by a short head at Hamilton (6f) last month and was never in contention at York the following week; has done all his winning at 6f and yet to shine over 7f.
runners.comment: Won in May but never going at York a week later; has done all his winning at 6f

runners.name: Wreck It Ryley
runners.spotlight: Kept trying when third at Doncaster last time (6f) but was beaten over 3l and he's not looked well handicapped since scoring by 6l in April; he's also only 1-16 over 7f.
runners.comment: He's not looked well handicapped since scoring by 6l in April; also better at 6f

runners.name: Society Man
runners.spotlight: His best efforts in Britain have been on AW and he was ideally suited by testing ground when trained in France; none too consistent, either.
runners.comment: His best efforts in Britain have been on AW and prefers slower ground on grass

races.race_name: Edinburgh Gin Queen Of Scots British EBF Fillies' Stakes (Listed Race)

runners.name: La Brodeuse
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced French winner (1m, soft) who, after changing hands for 125,000euros, was a well-backed runner-up on her stable and seasonal debut in the Group 3 Chartwell Stakes at Lingfield (7f, good to firm) four weeks ago; leading claims if confirming that promise this afternoon.
runners.comment: Well-backed runner-up on stable debut in Group 3 at Lingfield; leading claims on that form
runners.owner: Mrs R G Hillen And Partner

runners.name: Magic Basma
runners.spotlight: 4yo who has shown improved form in Listed races this term, finishing fourth at Newmarket (6f, good to firm) and runner-up at Haydock (6f, good to soft); she's 0-11 since her debut win in August 2024 and the return to 7f poses a question, but this is an easy track and she's a major player on form.
runners.comment: Return to 7f poses a question but major player on this year's improved 6f Listed form

runners.name: Figjam
runners.spotlight: Promising 2yo last season, finishing second in a Listed race at Doncaster (6f, heavy) in October; respectable seventh in the Group 3 Fred Darling at Newbury (7f, good) on reappearance and fourth of 15 in Listed race at Haydock (6f, good to soft) a fortnight ago, about 4l behind the runner-up Magic Basma; shaped last time as though the return to 7f could help; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Behind Magic Basma when Listed fourth at Haydock but today's extra furlong could help

runners.name: Havana Pusey
runners.spotlight: Won a handicap at Yarmouth (7f, good) in April and another big run, despite an awkward start, when third in the Group 3 Chartwell Stakes at Lingfield last month (7f, good to firm; 1l behind runner-up Magic Basma); also effective on good to soft (yet to run well on soft); she could be thereabouts.
runners.comment: Group 3 third at Lingfield four weeks ago and she could be thereabouts

runners.name: Golden Palace
runners.spotlight: Kept on for fifth of 19 in 2yo Listed race at Newmarket (6f, good) last October and she could be sharper for her reappearance run there in April (7f, good), when seventh in the Group 3 Nell Gwyn; however, others have more substance to their claims.
runners.comment: Could improved for her comeback in the Nell Gwyn but others have more substance to claims

runners.name: Brazilian Rose
runners.spotlight: Four handicap wins on the Southwell AW this January-March (7f/6f) and has also shown promise on turf (albeit 0-6 on grass); however, she needs a sizeable step forward if she's to pose a threat in this Listed contest.
runners.comment: Four AW handicaps this year but needs sizeable step forward to pose a threat in this

runners.name: Circe
runners.spotlight: Went off the boil in Listed races at the end of last season; however, a break may have helped and she was progressive last term, with three handicap wins (6f, good/good to firm) and a good fourth of 18 in the Group 3 Sceptre Stakes at Doncaster (7f, soft); can be in the mix if she's back to her best.
runners.comment: Went off the boil at the end of last term but can be in the mix if she's back to her best
runners.owner: Pineapple Stud And Miss C Douglas

races.race_name: Edinburgh Cup Handicap

runners.name: Magical Merlot
runners.spotlight: Brighton novice winner last season at 1m; gelded in November; perhaps needed his first run back and then he stuck on gamely for a close third at Windsor (1m, good to firm); could step up again but will need to in this better race.
runners.comment: Recent Windsor third; could step up again but will need to in this better race

runners.name: Blues And Royals
runners.spotlight: Maintained his progress when repelling Victory Ace for a second AW success in May (1m) and Sandown last time didn't go entirely to plan after hitting traffic; remains of interest.
runners.comment: Dual AW winner and Sandown last time didn't go entirely to plan; remains of interest
runners.owner: D Fish, S Jones & J Barnett

runners.name: Victory Ace
runners.spotlight: Picked up from Roger Varian for 40,000gns; edged home on his handicap debut over 7f and again did his best work late at Kempton (1m, AW) when closing in on Blues And Royals with whom he's closely matched; goes further again here with little weight to carry and looks a major player.
runners.comment: Lightly raced gelding who is improving steadily and he's raised again in distance

runners.name: Strength Of Spirit
runners.spotlight: Has done well for one who opened his account in a seller, bagging his second handicap over 1m here in April; however, progress has stalled, markedly so in his last two races (competitive ones admittedly).
runners.comment: Three wins but progress has stalled, markedly so in his last two races
runners.owner: Hambleton Racing Ltd XXXV & E Burke

runners.name: Evening Blues
runners.spotlight: AW winner for Ger Lyons, for whom he was 1-7; weak in the betting when fifth of 11 on yard debut at Wetherby but, although he wasn't beaten far, that was a Class 4; the hood that worked well for him in Ireland now returns.
runners.comment: Close fifth at Wetherby and the hood that worked well for him in Ireland now returns

runners.name: Pandemonium
runners.spotlight: Last year's two wins were over 7f on soft/good to soft before finishing tailed off in a Group 2; he worryingly suffered the same fate on handicap debut at the Craven meeting (1m, good) since when he's been gelded; has a few questions to answer now and the cheekpieces are new.
runners.comment: Won his first two races but desperate since; now gelded and tried in cheekpieces

runners.name: Monoceros
runners.spotlight: Pulled up on his final run for Godolphin but his first two efforts read well; joined current yard for 25,000gns and kept on takingly for third behind Strength Of Spirit over 1m here in April (handicap debut); open to improvement.
runners.comment: Useful for Godolphin and still lightly raced; likeable stable debut when third here

races.race_name: Edinburgh Gin Seaside Holyrood Handicap

runners.name: Mighty Magnus
runners.spotlight: Placed in two of his three starts this year, latterly when third at Thirsk six days ago on his return to 5f; fairly exposed and more minor money perhaps his best hope.
runners.comment: Placed in two of his three runs this year; fairly exposed and others stronger for the win

runners.name: Lebron Power
runners.spotlight: Won three times as a 2yo (5f and 6f, soft and good to firm); not so good in two runs this time around but this represents a drop in class and going back over 5f should be fine.
runners.comment: 3-6 as a 2yo; not fired yet this year but drops in trip and class today

runners.name: Dandy Breeze
runners.spotlight: Useful ability in three AW starts, including a 5f win, but he was 20-1 and never a threat switched to turf at Hamilton three weeks ago (6f, good); needs to bounce back quickly.
runners.comment: Promising on AW but he was a well-beaten 20-1 shot on last month's handicap debut

runners.name: Merlier
runners.spotlight: Didn't have to match his earlier efforts to win a Beverley seller 25 days ago (5f, good to firm); this will demand much more and the addition of cheekpieces is not enough to tempt.
runners.comment: Won a seller last month but this is much tougher; cheekpieces now reached for

runners.name: Justmyluck
runners.spotlight: Made all in a 5f handicap at Newcastle in January but little to cheer in three runs since; risky.
runners.comment: Made all in a 5f handicap at Newcastle in January but little to cheer in three runs since

runners.name: Major Neigh Sayer
runners.spotlight: Won a 7f novice as a 2yo and he was tried over 1m on his seasonal/handicap debut in April; dropped to 5f at Sandown nine days ago (tongue tied first time; well backed) and travelled well but met some trouble at a crucial stage; that race set up for the closers and this is a sharper test but he could well have more to offer as a sprinter; cheekpieces now added.
runners.comment: Good 2nd at Sandown (5f, good) on h'cap debut nine days ago; sharper test but still feared

runners.name: Kind Touch
runners.spotlight: Led late when making a winning debut at Ripon last August (5f, good to firm); beaten at odds-on at Pontefract (6f, good to soft) the following month but that form looks solid; gelded afterwards; brings clear potential to this handicap debut.
runners.comment: First and second in two 2yo runs; open to improvement and this mark looks workable

runners.name: Arduis Invicta
runners.spotlight: She has performed with credit in stronger handicaps at Chester and York on her last two starts and can race off 2lb lower today; C&D winner; not discounted.
runners.comment: Creditable efforts in stronger company on her last two starts; C&D winner; not discounted

races.race_name: Edinburgh Gin Cannonball Handicap

runners.name: Nakatomi
runners.spotlight: Has yet to win a race but he comes here on the back of a good second of eight in 1m handicap at Hamilton; should go well.
runners.comment: Comes here on the back of a good second of eight in 1m handicap at Hamilton
runners.owner: S McMillan, F Reay & A D Brown

runners.name: Samra Star
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner on AW but 0-8 on turf and was beaten over 7l into second here two weeks ago.
runners.comment: 0-8 on turf and was beaten over 7l into second here two weeks ago

runners.name: On The Bubble
runners.spotlight: 2-32 and one of those wins was over hurdles; runner-up over 1m1f here in April but wilted into a remote third last time.
runners.comment: Runner-up over 1m1f here in April but wilted into a remote third last time

runners.name: Mavetheforcebewivu
runners.spotlight: 15-race maiden who hasn't threatened too often and April's efforts were modest.
runners.comment: 15-race maiden who hasn't threatened too often and April's efforts were modest

runners.name: Little Ted
runners.spotlight: Age might be catching up but he's a multiple winner on a potentially good mark and Tuesday's run on the AW was respectable.
runners.comment: On a potentially good mark and Tuesday's run on the AW was respectable

runners.name: Pebble Dash
runners.spotlight: 0-14 and hard to enthuse over after latest 10l defeat here when behind two of these.
runners.comment: 0-14 and hard to enthuse over after latest 10l defeat here when behind two of these

runners.name: Zebra Star
runners.spotlight: Thoroughly exposed three-time winner who may need further now, with her last win coming at 1m2f; reappearance effort was underwhelming.
runners.comment: Thoroughly exposed three-time winner who may need further now; underwhelming return

runners.name: War Memorial
runners.spotlight: Backed when winning over 7f here in April (good) and first-time cheekpieces perhaps backfired at Doncaster; those aids are now left off but has stamina to prove over this far.
runners.comment: Won in April; cheekpieces removed after last run but there are stamina queries

runners.name: Monhammer
runners.spotlight: No win since May 2024 but ran well on occasions last year, albeit over 7f which is more his trip; others might see this out better but has each-way claims.
runners.comment: No win since May 2024 but ran well on occasions last year, albeit over 7f

runners.name: Doon The Glen
runners.spotlight: Only 1-32 and vast majority of his form is at sprint distances; likely to get outstayed.
runners.comment: Only 1-32 and vast majority of his form is at sprint distances; likely to get outstayed

runners.name: Approaching Dawn
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 6yo who has yet to offer much off his basement mark; very opposable.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 6yo who has yet to offer much off his basement mark; very opposable
runners.owner: D P Van Der Hoeven & D G Pryde

runners.name: Freak Encounter
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent 4yo who is 1-24 and showed no benefit for wind surgery when last seen in February.
runners.comment: Inconsistent 4yo; 1-24 and showed no benefit for wind surgery when last seen in February

races.race_name: Northwick Group Handicap

runners.name: Desert Master
runners.spotlight: Triple AW winner but he's 0-12 on turf; had a close call over 5f at Southwell in April but he was always behind after missing the break at Beverley (5f, good to firm) ten days ago; down the list.
runners.comment: 0-12 on turf and he was always behind after missing the break at Beverely ten days ago
runners.owner: John And Tony Jarvis And Partner

runners.name: Candy Warhol
runners.spotlight: Front-runner; 0-11 on turf but he's been placed at Windsor (6f, good to firm) and Bath (5.7f, firm) this spring and ran another good race when a close fourth at Bath 11 days ago; should give it another good shot and he's in the mix back up in trip.
runners.comment: 0-11 on turf but he's been running well this spring and shouldn't be far away
runners.owner: Homecroft Wealth, Paul J Dixon & Partners

runners.name: Moonhall Lass
runners.spotlight: 0-22 in Ireland but she's 2-4 since joining James Owen; still looks feasibly treated on her clearcut win at Salisbury (6f, good to firm) last month but she didn't fire when favourite at Windsor last time and needs to step up again after that.
runners.comment: Didn't fire in her hat-trick bid at Windsor and needs to step up again after that

runners.name: Mickey Mongoose
runners.spotlight: Two 7f AW wins at Southwell this year but he's not easy to predict and was only fourth of seventh at Newmarket (7f, good) last time; that took his turf record to 1-19 and he has something to prove back at this trip.
runners.comment: Record of 1-19 on turf and he was well held at Newmarket last time; others preferred

runners.name: Instant Bond
runners.spotlight: 5yo who has won two of his last four starts including a last-gasp success at Leicester (6f, good to firm) in April; remains feasibly treated off only 2lb higher and he's still unexposed on turf; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Unexposed on turf and he was a last-gasp winner at Leicester in April; respected up 2lb
runners.owner: David Tate, Danny Lawson & Kevin Broadley

runners.name: Canaria Sun
runners.spotlight: Some creditable efforts in handicaps including when third at Redcar (6f, good) last month, but his form nosedived at Ayr two weeks ago and he's now 0-10.
runners.comment: Some fair efforts in handicaps but his form nosedived at Ayr latest and he's now 0-10

runners.name: Melissa Honey
runners.spotlight: 0-14 but she reached the frame over 6f at Southwell (AW) in April and was a creditable third back on turf at Leicester (7f, good to firm) 12 days ago; continues to edge down the weights and has each-way claims again.
runners.comment: 0-14 but she's reached the frame in last two starts and should be in the mix again

runners.name: Bibendum
runners.spotlight: Stayed on well to win a Newcastle handicap (5f, Tapeta) in November but he's not really threatened since and was well held over 6f at Newcastle in February last time; that took his record to 2-30 and he's not easy to predict.
runners.comment: 5yo who is not easy to predict and has a modest strike-rate

runners.name: Ticktyboo
runners.spotlight: Just one win from 21 starts and that was in a maiden in August 2024; has dropped a long way in the weights but he generally struggled for Nigel Tinkler last year and was last of ten on recent stable debut at Newcastle (7f, AW); visor is now added.
runners.comment: On much-reduced mark but sole win was in 2024 and he struggled on recent stable debut

runners.name: Asadjumeirah
runners.spotlight: Three wins on AW last year but his last turf success was in April 2022 and he returned to this sphere with a well-held sixth at Thirsk (6f, good; 12-1) last month; others are more appealing.
runners.comment: His last turf win was in 2022 and he was well held at Thirsk (6f) three weeks ago

runners.name: Mu Mu Land
runners.spotlight: Back to form when runner-up at Southwell (6f, AW) in April but he failed to back that up on his return visit two weeks ago; remains unexposed on turf but overall record is 1-13 and others are more convincing.
runners.comment: Inconsistent 4yo who is 1-13 and others are more convincing

runners.name: Solar Biricz
runners.spotlight: Better signs for new stable when fourth with visor added at Redcar (5f, good to firm) in April but he finished tailed off at Thirsk (6f, good) three weeks ago; has something to prove again after that and the headgear is now discarded.
runners.comment: Has a patchy record and was tailed off at Thirsk last month; visor removed

runners.name: Roundhay Park
runners.spotlight: 13-time winner who is ideally suited by slow ground; his last success was off 2lb higher at Ripon (6f, soft) last summer but he's been well held in both runs this season; now drops back in trip but this veteran needs a major revival.
runners.comment: 13-time winner but he's been well held in two runs this season; needs to get back on track

runners.name: Run This Way
runners.spotlight: All eight wins have been on good or slower and last six have been over 6f; made it 2-2 in blinkers when beating 16 rivals at Thirsk last month and she ran well again when fourth in hat-trick bid at Nottingham (6f, good) on Wednesday; should be in the thick of things again and it would be a positive if rain arrives.
runners.comment: Creditable fourth in hat-trick bid at Nottingham on Wednesday; should go well again

runners.name: Hurstwood
runners.spotlight: Recorded his tenth turf win when scoring at Nottingham (6f, good to soft) in October; now 2lb lower than for that success but he's been short of spark in three runs this season and needs to raise his game.
runners.comment: On dangerous mark but he's been short of spark this season and needs to raise his game
runners.owner: Hard Held Partnership And Angus Racing

runners.name: Brave Empire
runners.spotlight: Very useful juvenile/3yo for Roger Varian but his last win was in February 2024 and he's not made an impact in seven runs this year; mark continues to fall but losing run is up to 20 and others are preferred.
runners.comment: His last win was in February 2024 and he's not made an impact this year; passed over
runners.owner: James Paul Services To Industry Ltd

races.race_name: British Stallion Studs EBF Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Jazz Queen
runners.spotlight: Mixed form in her three starts and was disappointing when favourite at Haydock (6f, good to soft) two weeks ago but she went close at Chester (5.5f, good) on her penultimate run; sets a fair standard on that form and she's a big player if she can bounce back.
runners.comment: Disappointing at Haydock latest but she sets a fair standard on her second at Chester

runners.name: Met Ball
runners.spotlight: April foal; second foal; half-sister to 7f winner Fashion People (including AW 2yo; RPR 92); dam 7f 2yo winner (88), sister to winners Sea Galaxy (1m2f) and Star Of Wins (1m2f/10.5f), out of German Listed-placed 6f 2yo winner; holds Group 2 entry at the Curragh later this month and she's an interesting newcomer.
runners.comment: Holds a Group 2 entry at the Curragh later this month and she's an interesting newcomer

runners.name: Ashina
runners.spotlight: 60,000euros foal, 200,000gns yearling; first foal; dam unraced sister to useful 1m3f/11.6f winner New Comedy, half-sister to 7f Listed winner Romantic Song and Oaks runner-up Dance Sequence, out of 1m Listed winner; plenty to like on paper and she represents an in-form yard.
runners.comment: 200,000gns yearling; interesting newcomer and she needs a close look in market

runners.name: Bayside View
runners.spotlight: March foal; 80,000euros yearling; closely related to 7f 2yo/1m2f Listed winner Jennifer Jane (RPR 105), half-sister to winners Surpass And Shine (5f; 80), Enkindle (Greek 5f-7f) and Black Angel (Polish 6f/7f inc 2yo); dam 5f-7f winner (including 2yo; 101); yard has had a 2yo debut winner (11-2) recently and she needs a close look in market.
runners.comment: 80,000euros yearling; yard has had a 2yo debut winner recently and she needs a close look

runners.name: Cash Cove
runners.spotlight: 36,000gns yearling; close fourth of six on Southwell debut (7f, AW) last month but she recorded an RPR of 44 in that ordinary race; this looks much tougher and she needs a major improvement dropped in trip.
runners.comment: Close fourth of six at Southwell but she recorded an RPR of 44 in that ordinary race

runners.name: Rhodes Runner
runners.spotlight: 75,000gns yearling; well-backed favourite at Salisbury (5f, good to firm) last month but she ran green before finishing a well-held fourth of six; should be more streetwise over this longer trip but she needs to leave that initial form behind.
runners.comment: Didn't live up to market expectations at Salisbury and she needs to leave that form behind

races.race_name: Dysons And TPI Taping And Jointing Ltd Handicap

runners.name: Naana's Sparkle
runners.spotlight: Reappeared after 208 days off with a win on his stable debut at Yarmouth (5f, good to soft) last month; up 4lb but he held off an odds-on rival last time and hit a personal best with that success; should be in the thick of things again and he won't mind what the weather does.
runners.comment: Won on stable debut at Yarmouth (5f) last month and that was a personal best; respected

runners.name: Master Of My Fate
runners.spotlight: Successful twice over 5f at Southwell this year; ran respectably back on turf at Nottingham (5f, good) in April but he was laboured in similar conditions at York two weeks ago; has bit to prove again after that and he's never won off a mark this high.
runners.comment: Looks weighted near best off this mark and he was in rear at York last time
runners.owner: Ms Janelle Cao, Ross Harmon & Partner

runners.name: Intervention
runners.spotlight: Won last season's Great St Wilfrid at Ripon (6f, good to firm) off a 9lb higher mark; some creditable efforts since but he's hard to predict and has struggled at Chester and York in his last two starts; comes with risks attached and he needs to bounce back on this drop back in trip.
runners.comment: On reduced mark but he's struggled in last two starts and needs to get back on track

runners.name: Blinky
runners.spotlight: Good fourth of 16 in the 3yo Dash at Epsom (5f, good) last June, but he's been in and out since and has been well held in both runs this season; on dangerous mark but losing run is now up to 12 and others are more convincing.
runners.comment: On dangerous mark but losing run is up to 12 and others are more convincing

runners.name: Toca Madera
runners.spotlight: His last win was in August 2024 but he went close off a reduced mark in a 16-runner handicap at York (5f, good) last month; back up 2lb in the weights but that was a bold bid last time and he's respected.
runners.comment: Back to form with a bold bid in a big field at York (5f, good) last month; respected
runners.owner: L Bond & Sheikh Aymen Jawad Al Hamandi

runners.name: Counsel
runners.spotlight: Six-time AW winner but he's 0-14 on turf and was only seventh of 17 back in this sphere at Thirsk (5f, good) last month; one of three runners for Mick Appleby and he has work to do back up in grade.
runners.comment: 0-14 on turf and was only seven of 17 back in this sphere at Thirsk (5f, good) last month

runners.name: Paddy's Day
runners.spotlight: Won six 5f handicaps on AW at Newcastle in 2025; now back on last winning mark but he's 1-24 on turf and that success was in a novice over four years ago; looks opposable back in this sphere after a short break.
runners.comment: Six wins at Newcastle last year but his sole turf win was in 2022; opposable

runners.name: Elegant Erin
runners.spotlight: Acts on good to firm and soft; ten-time winner but her last four turf wins have been at Beverley and she's been out of sorts in three runs this year; has something to prove and she needs a big turnaround.
runners.comment: Eight-time turf winner but he's been out of sorts this year; needs a big turnaround

runners.name: Baldomero
runners.spotlight: His turf wins have come on good or slower including heavy ground; well held in three runs this spring but he won three times on AW during the winter and was successful in this race last year; now 2lb lower than for his last win and has claims if he can rediscover his spark.
runners.comment: Won this race last year and has claims if he can rediscover his spark

runners.name: Squealer
runners.spotlight: Won off 10lb higher at York (5f, good) last June but disappointing since and has form figures of 690 this season; has a lot to prove and needs a major revival but stable is in good form and market strength would be interesting.
runners.comment: Won off 10lb higher at York last June but he's lost his way; stable is in good form

races.race_name: Solar Xpress Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Gaelic Approach
runners.spotlight: Two wins and two seconds this season in his four small-field handicaps; 4-9 when upped to 1m6f at Redcar on latest, well on top; raised 3lb but this should also be a far more competitive race.
runners.comment: Two wins and two seconds in his four small-field handicaps; 4-9 win when upped to 1m6f
runners.owner: Middleham Park Racing Xxiii & M Hulin

runners.name: Study Of Words
runners.spotlight: Marked improvement through three-race 2yo career over 1m/1m2f on soft/good; could hardly have scored more easily when 1-7 on final start in that campaign, which he did not live up to when 5-4 favourite with cheekpieces back over Newmarket's 1m2f (good to firm) in handicap on reappearance but his trainer has a major rethink with today's trip; can't be ruled out.
runners.comment: Third of five when 5-4 favourite for 1m2f handicap at Newmarket; big rethink on trip

runners.name: Siam Ruby
runners.spotlight: Made all in two small AW fields and ran creditably in 1m2f turf handicaps this season when pipped here and third of four at Salisbury; should stay beyond 1m2f but this is an extra 4f; 7lb claimer is recruited.
runners.comment: Can front-run; should stay beyond 1m2f but this is an extra 4f; 7lb claimer is recruited

runners.name: Lopeo
runners.spotlight: Showed staying potential with a 1m win on heavy ground in three-runner maiden at Goodwood last September, easily his best of three 2yo races; sent handicapping over 1m4f here (good to firm) on reappearance and kept on galloping for a good second of seven, so it looks like he will stay this trip; considered with an unchanged mark.
runners.comment: Kept on galloping for a good second here (1m4f) so it looks like he will stay this trip

runners.name: Killer Whale
