runners.spotlight: Improved in his three AW races, runner-up on last two occasions to short-priced Charlie Appleby-trained favourites over 1m and 1m4f; made most last time, keeping on strongly, and his dam was a 2m2f Cesarewitch winner, so this further step up in trip will surely suit him on handicap/turf debut.
runners.comment: Dam won 2m2f Cesarewitch; this step up in trip will surely suit him on handicap/turf debut

runners.name: Any Which Way
runners.spotlight: Always prominent to justify favouritism on handicap/seasonal debut at Nottingham (1m2f, good) in April; languished in rear, contrastingly, for most of the race at Windsor (11.4f, good) 19 days ago but started to pass rivals over 1f out and finished second of seven; 1m6f beckons strongly after that performance, so he could have more to give.
runners.comment: 1m6f beckons strongly after his Windsor second (11.4f) so he could have more to give

races.race_name: British Stallion Studs EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Flight Control
runners.spotlight: 200,000euros yearling who was sold by Coolmore for 8,000gns last October; 80-1 on debut here (6f, good to firm) in April but he came from off the pace to win in emphatic style; held in third on his return visit last month but he wasn't beaten far and that was a creditable effort under a penalty; now goes up in trip but he has plenty of stamina on dam's side and he's strongly respected.
runners.comment: Win and creditable third in two runs over 6f here this spring; respected upped in trip

runners.name: Shipbourne
runners.spotlight: By Frankel out of a 1,000 Guineas winner; odds-on favourite on Newbury debut (6.5f, soft) in October and he forged clear to win with lots in hand; well-held third under a penalty at Kempton in December but that was behind a smart prospect who finished third in the Craven (Group 3) this spring; sets the standard on that latest form and he's a big player on his return.
runners.comment: Won on Newbury debut and he ran into a smart prospect at Kempton last time; key player

runners.name: English Time
runners.spotlight: 260,000gns yearling; showed ability when in midfield on Newmarket debut last October and he found improvement when fourth of 15 on his Newbury reappearance (7f, good) last month; that was an eyecatching effort from off the pace and he should continue to progress with experience; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Reappeared with an eyecatching fourth at Newbury and that form puts him in the picture

runners.name: Race To The Edge
runners.spotlight: 135,000euros yearling; ran green at a big price on Newbury debut (7f, good) last month but he made some good late headway and was only beaten 4l in seventh of 15; ties in with English Time on that form and he looks a likely improver on his second start; dangerous.
runners.comment: Made late gains on Newbury debut last month and he ties in with English Time on that form

runners.name: Binmalk
runners.spotlight: Has a striking pedigree but he made a low-key start at a biggish price at Newbury (7f, good) last month; should know more this time but he needs to leave that debut form a long way behind.
runners.comment: Has a striking pedigree but he made a low-key start at Newbury (7f, good) last month

runners.name: Deadline
runners.spotlight: 30,000euros yearling; half-brother to four winners including Buthela (French Group-placed 1m; RPR 113), Toyotomi (French 5.5f-8.5f including Listed; 108) and Deemster (French 7f AW 2yo/1m turf Listed; 102); dam 6.5f-7.5f AW winner (including 2yo/Listed; 103); plenty to like on paper and he needs watching in market on debut.
runners.comment: 30,000euros yearling; plenty to like on paper and he needs watching in market on debut

runners.name: Mass For Kathleen
runners.spotlight: Outsider on recent Haydock debut (7f, soft) and she finished a well-held third of five, with an RPR in the 50s; opposable.
runners.comment: Outsider on recent Haydock debut (7f, soft) and she finished a well-held third of five

runners.name: Blue Icon
runners.spotlight: Gelded son of Space Blues; attracted some support into 14-1 here (6f, good) last month but he finished a well-held fifth of ten; should benefit from that experience but he needs major improvement upped in trip.
runners.comment: Well held on debut here last month and he needs major improvement upped in trip

runners.name: Lineofsuccession
runners.spotlight: 50-1 and she finished a well-held sixth of eight on Pontefract debut (6f, good to soft) last month; has a lot to find upped in trip and she's probably a longer-term prospect.
runners.comment: 50-1 and she finished a well-held sixth of eight on Pontefract debut (6f) last month

runners.name: Park Lane Penny
runners.spotlight: Has a good pedigree but she missed the break and finished tailed off at a big price on recent Redcar debut (1m, good to firm); can only watch.
runners.comment: Has good pedigree but she finished tailed off on recent Redcar debut (1m, good to firm)

runners.name: Brave New World
runners.spotlight: 220,000gns yearling; brother to two useful 7f winners, but he didn't show much at a big price on Kempton debut (7f, AW) in April and he's probably one for later on.
runners.comment: Cost 220,000gns as a yearling but he didn't show much at a big price on debut in April

races.race_name: Summer Saturday Series At Doncaster Racecourse Handicap

runners.name: Montezin
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo who won a Newcastle maiden (6f, AW) in November and was a creditable fourth of 22 at York (7f, good) last month, in just his second handicap; that was his first attempt at this trip and has each-way claims if he can build on that; cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 4yo who was fourth of 22 in a York handicap last month; not ruled out

runners.name: Arctic Dawn
runners.spotlight: Turf maiden winner who won two AW handicaps last autumn; his last success was off 7lb lower but he reappeared with a good third at Newmarket (7f, good) last month and he's still lightly raced on turf; in the mix.
runners.comment: Reappeared with a good third at Newmarket and he's still lightly raced on turf; in the mix

runners.name: Akkadian Thunder
runners.spotlight: His last two wins have been over C&D and latest was in this race last year; flopped when favourite at this track last month but he finished runner-up in a big field here (6f, good to soft) on his reappearance in March; looks interesting if he can recapture that form and it would be a bonus if rain arrives.
runners.comment: Flopped over 6f here last month but he won this race off 2lb lower last year; dangerous

runners.name: Desert Falcon
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who performed with great credit on all five starts last season, including a win here (6.5f) in September and a narrow defeat in the Ayr Gold Cup (6f); returned after wind surgery with a low-key effort at York last month but he may have need that run and has claims if he can get back near best; acts on most ground and four of his six wins have been at 7f.
runners.comment: May have needed his reappearance run and has claims on his best form last season

runners.name: Apotheosis
runners.spotlight: Dual turf winner (7f, good) who won on AW at Newcastle in December, but he's not easy to predict and was beaten 11l when sixth at Lingfield (7.6f, good to firm) last month; has bit to prove again after that and he's still 6lb higher than for his last win; regular hood is removed.
runners.comment: Triple 7f winner but he's not easy to predict and looks weighted near best off this mark

runners.name: Monsieur Bondy
runners.spotlight: Unexposed 4yo; won a Redcar maiden (7f, good to firm) and a Newcastle novice (7f, AW) towards the end of last summer but he was well held on handicap at Newcastle in October; still has potential but this is competitive on his return and he needs to find more progress.
runners.comment: Unexposed 4yo but this is competitive on his return and he needs to find more progress

runners.name: Galeron
runners.spotlight: Acts on good to firm and heavy; on a competitive mark on last year's best efforts but he's without a win since September 2022 and finished merely in mid-division on reappearance in the Lincoln here in March (unseated rider on the way to post); others are more persuasive.
runners.comment: On competitive mark but his last win was in 2022 and others are preferred

runners.name: New Image
runners.spotlight: Versatile sort who has won four times at up to 1m but his last success was in August 2024; reappeared with a close third off 2lb higher at Redcar (1m, good to firm) in April but he's not easy to predict and was tailed off at Thirsk three weeks ago; yard also runs Akkadian Thunder.
runners.comment: On dangerous mark but last win was in 2024 and he was tailed off at Thirsk last time

runners.name: Eminency
runners.spotlight: Kept fairly busy in the last 12 months and he supplemented a 6f Windsor win (good) in September by scoring over 7f at Yarmouth (good to soft) in October and at Chelmsford (AW) in December; has held his form with some solid efforts this spring and he could make a serious impact if this sets up for his closing style.
runners.comment: Reliable type who has been running well this spring and has claims if gets some luck

runners.name: Khafiz
runners.spotlight: Won novice events at Kempton (7f, AW) and over C&D (good) for Roger Varian last summer but has mixed record in handicaps since; made a promising stable debut when fifth at Haydock in April but he was a long way below that form in another Class 2 event at Ascot last month; now drops back in grade but regular hood is removed and he comes with risks attached.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 4yo but he has mixed record in handicaps and was beaten 15l at Ascot latest

runners.name: Spell Master
runners.spotlight: Useful juvenile who ended his 3yo campaign with a win in a Newcastle handicap (7f, AW) in October; been gelded and left Andrew Balding for 150,000gns since; remains feasibly treated off only 3lb higher than last time and he needs a close look on return for new yard.
runners.comment: Changed hands for 150,000gns after his AW win in October; needs close look on stable debut

races.race_name: attheraces.com Handicap

runners.name: Thapa Vc
runners.spotlight: His 13 wins include one over C&D; on a good mark and his last couple of efforts suggest he may be ready to take advantage, meeting all sorts of trouble in the straight but still beaten only 2l into fourth at Wolverhampton on Monday; gets the vote.
runners.comment: On good mark and has caught eye recently, particularly on AW earlier in week; gets vote

runners.name: Bizarre Law
runners.spotlight: Edged out a next-time-out scorer (pair 4l clear) when coming from well off the pace to get up late over C&D (good to soft) three weeks ago; he's been an infrequent winner over the years but he retains handicapping scope after a 5lb rise.
runners.comment: Only 4-41 overall but form of last month's C&D win boosted by second going in since

runners.name: Highfield Sunshine
runners.spotlight: Below best at the end of last season and also well held on 7f Catterick reappearance, taking her record to 0-13; a subsequent wind operation needs to have had a positive effect.
runners.comment: Well held on return, taking record to 0-13; a recent wind op needs to make a difference

runners.name: Age Of Baroque
runners.spotlight: 7f winner at Southwell for Marco Botti last summer off 5lb higher; patchy for this yard since, although 9.5f at Wolverhampton seemed to stretch his stamina last time and this test is more suitable.
runners.comment: Patchy form for this yard but failed to stay 9.5f latest and is 5lb below his win mark

runners.name: Mayo County
runners.spotlight: Added to her good record over 7f at Redcar when comfortably seeing off ten rivals on good to firm 12 days ago; a 6lb rise shouldn't prevent a prominent showing if turning up in a similar mood.
runners.comment: Comfortable winner over 7f at Redcar latest and should go well again

runners.name: Amelia's Joy
runners.spotlight: Dual winner over 7f at Wolverhampton this year and did enough to think she's as effective on turf when fourth of eight over 7f at Catterick 16 days ago.
runners.comment: Dual AW winner this year and latest Catterick fourth shows she acts on turf

runners.name: Ziggy's Angel
runners.spotlight: Made a successful handicap debut at Newcastle (7f, AW) last September but has suffered heavy defeats back on turf the last twice; first-time cheekpieces need to make a difference.
runners.comment: 4lb below mark he defied on AW last autumn but well held on turf last 2 runs; headgear on

runners.name: Finn Ironside
runners.spotlight: C&D winner on good to soft last year and another solid C&D effort when third of 16 on good to firm six weeks ago; successful off 1lb higher on AW last year; likely to be in the thick of things.
runners.comment: C&D winner last year and latest C&D third of 16 suggests he may be ready to strike again

runners.name: Magic Music
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo who left behind a disappointing reappearance effort over 6f at Leicester when third of ten over C&D (good to firm) five weeks ago; a low-mileage profile provides hope that she can take another step forward today; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Lightly raced and latest C&D third was promising; enters calculations

runners.name: Singoura
runners.spotlight: 0-8 on turf but her last three runs (all 7f on good/good to firm) show that she can be competitive from her mark.
runners.comment: 0-8 on turf but her last three efforts show she can be competitive off her mark

runners.name: Palmarian
runners.spotlight: Ex-Gosden maiden who has yet to seriously threaten for Ruth Carr but his mark is dropping and it wouldn't be a surprise were he to pop up soon.
runners.comment: Yet to threaten for Ruth Carr but dangerous to discount from his reduced mark
runners.owner: MDP Racing And Mrs R Carr

races.race_name: Ladbrokes 'Bet #5 Get #30 New Customer Offer' Novice Hurdle

runners.name: Switch From Diesel
runners.spotlight: Very useful bumper performer, 3-12 in that sphere, second to Bambino Fever at Leopardstown last year; Flat maiden winner over 1m4f (heavy); aimed high in five starts over hurdles, second in Listed race at this track in December, respectable eighth in Grade 1 company at Leopardstown; easy winner of maiden hurdle over C&D 24 days ago; has class, is ground-versatile and sets a good standard.
runners.comment: Smart bumper form, Flat winner; maiden hurdle winner over C&D lto; sets a good standard

runners.name: Playtime
runners.spotlight: Good winner of a Leopardstown maiden hurdle (2m, soft) in March; okay runs in handicaps last twicel beaten 9.25l into third at Tramore lto; vulnerable to a few here but won't mind soft ground.
runners.comment: L'town maiden hurdle winner; found limits in h'cap company last twice; won't mind soft

runners.name: Magico
runners.spotlight: Three-time Flat winner in Britain (1m-1m3f); made promising start to hurdling career on stable debut after 11 months off when landing 2m1f maiden at Killarney 27 days ago, beating Tyson Fury by 4l; jumping was very novicey and needs to improve; form of that race has a solid look to it and he's a leading contender; wouldn't want much rain based on Flat form.
runners.comment: Made a promising start over hurdles at Kilbeggan lto; jumping novicey; obvious contender

runners.name: The First Turn
runners.spotlight: Thurles bumper winner on debut in October; hasn't threatened to go on from that since in three starts since, twice over hurdles; pulled up in hurdle race at Punchestown 38 days ago; needs plenty more and best watched.
runners.comment: Thurles bumper winner in Oct; not replicated that form in two hurdle starts; big ask

runners.name: Saporetti
runners.spotlight: Flat winner in France over 1m4f on heavy in November; reasonable hurdles debut when beaten 22l into fifth in maiden hurdle at Cork 79 days ago; likely out of depth here at this stage of his career.
runners.comment: Flat winner in France (1m4f, heavy); reasonable hurdles debut; out of depth here

races.race_name: Ladbrokes 'That's Ladisfaction' Handicap Chase

runners.name: Birdie Or Bust
runners.spotlight: 2-11 over fences; rated 13lb higher over hurdles; ran well when second in Grade 3 novice here in October (War Correspondent third); has disappointed at Fairyhouse and when pulled up over C&D since returning from a break; needs to leave those efforts behind.
runners.comment: Rated 13lb higher over hurdles, poor efforts last twice, on nice mark but revival needed

runners.name: Gaelic Arc
runners.spotlight: 2-30 over fences, both wins coming at Kilbeggan; is 5lb below his latest win but he's been out of sorts for a while and not an ideal preparation for this when falling at the fifth fence at Kilbeggan just six days ago; fourth in this last year but others make more appeal.
runners.comment: Both chase wins at Kilbeggan, out of form, fell six days ago, others appeal more
runners.owner: Mark R D Fleming / T Westropp Bennett

runners.name: Crescent Moon
runners.spotlight: Hurdles winner is 0-7 over fences and yet to trouble the judge; step in the right direction in a mares' chase at Killarney (2m5f, gd-yld) last time after successive pulled up efforts but more needed down in trip back in handicap company.
runners.comment: 0-7 over fences, step in right direction latest but more needed back in h'cap company

runners.name: Stuntman Steve
runners.spotlight: 2-12 over hurdles and rated 8lb higher in that sphere; hasn't looked like exploiting lower chase mark in recent starts; valid excuse at Tramore latest (mistake at third, pulled up after stirrup broke); capable of better than that and can't discount, especially if the rain misses.
runners.comment: Rated 8lb higher over hurdles, excuse when pulled up at Tramore, can't discount

runners.name: Be Fierce
runners.spotlight: Course winner over hurdles on testing ground; 1-6 over fences, the win coming on heavy ground at Tramore; not a bad effort back there on latest (15.5f, good) when a 14.5l fourth of 14 and eased 2lb; could go well if the forecast rain materialises.
runners.comment: Course winner over hurdles, best form on testing ground, could go well if rain arrives

runners.name: Indiana Jones
runners.spotlight: Back from a long spell in the doldrums with improved efforts in hot handicap chases at Aintree (2m5f, gd-sft) and over C&D (yielding) the last twice; big chance now dropped in grade with talented claimer taking off a handy 7lb.
runners.comment: Back to form the last twice, drops in grade, rider takes off handy 7lb, big chance

runners.name: Hashtag Pretender
runners.spotlight: 1-9 over hurdles; promise in both chase starts; 8.5l second in Killarney beginners' (2m1f, gd-yld) and when 5l behind Birdie Or Bust in a novice at Listowel (17.5f, gd-yld) last September; worse off at weights with that rival and not seen since but can't discount on handicap chase debut in new headgear combination.
runners.comment: H'cap chase debutant, promise in both chase starts, new headgear, can't discount

runners.name: Park That
runners.spotlight: Winning hurdler is 0-5 over fences but has run well in defeat at times; fine second behind subsequent Ayr premier handicap winner Moudan at Navan on penultimate (2m1f, sft-hvy); 20.5f may have stretched at Kilbeggan latest; drops back in trip now but would ideally prefer softer ground.
runners.comment: Fine Navan second on penultimate franked, trip too far latest, would prefer softer ground

runners.name: War Correspondent
runners.spotlight: Not the easiest to win with but often runs well in defeat; beaten 7.75l off 3lb higher on his last start in this sphere at Wexford on penultimate; 9l fourth in claiming hurdle back there on latest; best chase effort came when a 6l third in a Grade 3 novice here (18.5f, gd-yld) in October (Birdie Or Bust second); big chance on that form and has won here over hurdles.
runners.comment: Fine third in Grade 3 novice chase here in October, big chance if replicates that form

runners.name: Listentillitellyea
runners.spotlight: Has won over hurdles but is 0-9 over fences; ground given as possible excuse for 45l defeat at Ballinrobe (2m1f, good) on return from 295-day break on latest; forecast rain a plus and has run respectably here before but others hold more pressing claims.
runners.comment: 0-9 over fences, good ground not ideal latest, rain a plus but others appeal more

races.race_name: Ladbrokes 'Insure Your Acca On Football' Beginners Chase

runners.name: Da Capo Glory
runners.spotlight: Talented hurdler rated 132 in that sphere; placed in three of four starts over fences, including over C&D (yld-sft) in December when 16.5l third behind Argento Boy; another decent effort when a 9l second behind Butch Cassidy in a Killarney novice four weeks ago; has won here over timber; big chance.
runners.comment: Promise last three chase starts inc' here, good second at Killarney latest, big chance

runners.name: Quantum Quest
runners.spotlight: 2-9 over hurdles and rated 129; shaped well before tiring late on in grade 3 handicap hurdle at Aintree (24.5f, gd-sft) after break on latest; sharper now and makes chase debut with yard in hot form; dam related to chase winners; could go well.
runners.comment: 129 rated hurdler, sharper for latest, dam related to chase winners, could go well

runners.name: Kainsbourg
runners.spotlight: 2-2 in French bumpers; 2-10 and rated 130 over hurdles; fine second in Grade 2 novice hurdle at Chepstow (19.5f, good) in October; tailed off in rated hurdle when last seen at Fairyhouse in November; that was too bad to be true and his dam won over fences so could improve for switch to chasing but stamina to prove for this trip.
runners.comment: 2-10 and rated 130 over hurdles, dam a chase winner, stamina to prove at this trip

runners.name: Fillyoureye
runners.spotlight: Chase debutant; rated 123 over timber; won maiden hurdle here in February (2m7f, sft-hvy); poor efforts in handicap hurdles the last twice; related to useful chase winners and not discounted for top yard.
runners.comment: Chase debutant, course winner over hurdles, bred for this game, can't discount
runners.owner: Mrs N Flynn & John Joseph Flynn

runners.name: Game On
runners.spotlight: 1-4 over hurdles and rated 113; pulled up on chase debut at Kilbeggan (20.5f, good) last June when 6-1 (suffered an overreach to left fore heel); beaten 20l on return at Limerick last month when 7-2; capable of better but a bit to prove for now.
runners.comment: 113 rated hurdler, no impact first two chase starts, capable of better but risky for now

runners.name: Vicar Street
runners.spotlight: Turned over at short odds in Galway bumper on debut in August 2024; scored on hurdles debut on next start at Roscommon (20.5f, good) in July 2025; runner up in listed novice at Galway Festival later that month; off since; dam won over fences and market can guide on chase debut after a 312-day absence.
runners.comment: Placed in listed novice h'dle when last seen, dam chase winner, lacks a run, check market
runners.owner: John P McManus & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Wendrock
runners.spotlight: No joy since Leopardstown maiden hurdle win in December 2024; poor form in that sphere last season and not an obvious candidate for chasing on breeding; others appeal more.
runners.comment: No win over hurdles since Dec' 2024, poor form last season, not bred for chasing

runners.name: Mister Frostie
runners.spotlight: Point-to-point winner; modest maiden over hurdles rated 103; rated 95 after three poor efforts over fences (pulled up, beaten 52l and pulled up); hard to fancy in this company.
runners.comment: Point winner, rated 95 after three chase runs (P7P), hard to fancy

races.race_name: Ladbrokes 'Get More With Racing Bet Builder' Handicap Chase

runners.name: Aclass
runners.spotlight: Winner of Tramore beginners chase (2m5f, soft); bounced back after a couple of underwhelming runs when beaten a neck off 2lb lower mark at Tramore two starts ago; probably did too much too soon at same track lto; one of the more likely contenders; stays this trip.
runners.comment: Tramore beginners chase winner; two good efforts at Tramore last twice; chance up in trip

runners.name: Sammy Smart
runners.spotlight: Both career wins came at this track, including off 5lb lower over fences in December over 2m4f; couple of good placed efforts this year, most notably when beaten 4l into third at Tramore in April (Aclass in second); let down by jumping when well-held over shorter trip at Ballinrobe 12 days ago; could figure back up in trip with 5lb claimer taking over.
runners.comment: Two-time course winner; good run at Tramore; jumping mistakes lto; step up in trip a plus

runners.name: Il Silenzio
runners.spotlight: Improved for being sent chasing last summer and landed a Roscommon handicap on his third start over fences when last seen 305 days ago, travelling powerfully before beating subsequent winner Hit The Frame by 2.75l; unexposed for an 8yo and big chance off 6lb higher mark if ready to go after absence.
runners.comment: Landed a Roscommon h'cap well on third chase run; absent 305 days; big chance if ready

runners.name: North Of Nashville
runners.spotlight: Two-time handicap chase winner by wide margins, both coming at Wexford, including last time when scoring by an easy 9l off 8lb lower mark; can go well again but didn't back up a similarly comprehensive win in 2023 so not guaranteed to reproduce that effort.
runners.comment: Won Wexford h'cap by 9l lto; can go well again off 8lb higher but not sure to back it up

runners.name: A Year Older
runners.spotlight: Looked very limited until springing a 40-1 surprise at Fairyhouse over hurdles (3m2f, yielding) in January, form which has worked out well; fell four out over fences next time but made no mistake in scoring at Limerick 16 days ago, beating Cullenwaine by 0.75l; 5lb rise fine and he should relish this extra distance.
runners.comment: Been in fine form of late; landed h'cap chase 16 days ago; will enjoy this trip; big shout

runners.name: Tiffiny's Girl
runners.spotlight: Couple of fair efforts in maiden hurdles last year, not out of the first three in four point-to-points this year; landed a maiden hunters chase at Cork by 12l and backed up that run when second to Muskerry Rock at Wexford 30 days ago; on a fair mark on handicap chase debut and open to more improvement than most.
runners.comment: Fair efforts in maiden hurdles, point-to-points and hunter chases; on a fair mark in h'cap

runners.name: Bodhisattva
runners.spotlight: Strong stayer but winless in over three years and 1-24 over fences; positive run when fourth at Haydock two starts ago but didn't show much over the banks lto; mark continues to slide and shouldn't be discounted.
runners.comment: Winless in over three years but positive run at Haydock two starts ago; mark sliding

runners.name: Le Grand Vert
runners.spotlight: Three-time chase winner, twice over this distance at Ayr on soft; pulled up on last chase run at Down Royal and below-par hurdles at Sligo 25 days ago; put two decent runs back-to-back prior to Down Royal effort and chance if bouncing back; enjoys soft ground.
runners.comment: Won twice over this distance at Ayr on soft, latest in Feb; below form last twice; chance

runners.name: Pourquoi Poi
runners.spotlight: One win from seven starts coming at this track in a handicap hurdle (2m4f, good) in October off 87; backed that up when fifth at Thurles before fading tamely at Kilbeggan; okay debut over fences when beaten 10l into sixth at Downpatrick 15 days ago; bred to enjoy this step up in trip and an interesting handicap debutant.
runners.comment: Lightly-raced course winner over hurdles; okay chase debut lto and bred to enjoy this trip

runners.name: Seventy Eight Team
runners.spotlight: A maiden in 18 starts, four over fences, but put in three fair efforts over fences last year, most notably when second to Hit The Frame at Wexford, before being pulled up at this track over hurdles 177 days ago; 5lb out of the handicap off basement mark here but could go well.
runners.comment: Maiden in 18 starts; 5lb out of the handicap here but a couple of fair efforts last year

runners.name: Hascoeur Clermont
runners.spotlight: Two-time chase winner, winless since November 2023; had been running poorly but ran better when beaten 10l into fourth at Limerick 16 days ago in first-time cheekpieces having been hampered two out; they're left on now and he's on a nice mark if he can build on that.
runners.comment: Improved on prior poor efforts lto in first-time cp; chance if building on that

runners.name: Difficult Decision
runners.spotlight: Dual hurdle and chase winner who has won off higher marks in the past; not shown enough this year in recent months in either sphere to suggest a revival is imminent.
runners.comment: Won off higher marks in the past but no suggestion of late that a revival is imminent

runners.name: Reve Inoui
runners.spotlight: Point winner who was struggling over hurdles when returning from a long absence but has fared much better in two starts in handicap chases, beaten 10l into fourth over 3m2f at Down Royal 55 days ago; drop in trip sure to suit and chance.
runners.comment: Shaped well last twice over fences; drop in trip sure to suit; each-way chance

runners.name: Mistyburn
runners.spotlight: Improved for switch to handicap company last twice over hurdles, finishing third at Cork before scoring at Kilbeggan (3m, good) 43 days ago, beating Plain Or Battered by 1.25l; races off a 3lb higher chase mark here and a contender for last year's winning yard.
runners.comment: Won h'cap hurdle at Kilbeggan lto; 3lb higher, chance for last year's winning yard

runners.name: Secrecies Of Stone
runners.spotlight: Won over extended 2m5f at Leopardstown last year off 1lb lower, was set to back that up next time at Fairyhouse when unseating two out; not much to recommend in next three starts but fared a bit better over longer trip at Down Royal 33 days ago when fourth; dropped another 2lb and needs more.
runners.comment: Put two good runs together last year; below form since but better run lto; needs more

runners.name: Noble Talent
runners.spotlight: Has been struggling for over a year; three runs this year, pulling up twice and being well held over hurdles at Cork; slipping in the weights but little other reasons to recommend him back over fences up in trip in first-time blinkers.
runners.comment: Been struggling for over a year, only completed twice in last six runs; hard to fancy

runners.name: Justabitofyours
runners.spotlight: Standout form in 14 starts came when beaten 1.75l by Je T'ai Porte at Limerick a year ago; not threatened to rediscover that run since and hasn't completed in last three starts; up in trip.
runners.comment: Standout run at Limerick last year; downhill since and hasn't completed in last three runs

races.race_name: Ladbrokes 'Big Daily Boosts On Racing' Maiden Hurdle (Div I)

runners.name: Cailin Deas
runners.spotlight: Useful in bumpers, scoring at tenth attempt at Kilbeggan in August, beating Red Oak by 1.25l; ran to a similar level on hurdles debut at Thurles; returned from break on stable debut to be beaten 8.5l into third by Mr Master Mac at Downpatrick 15 days ago, lack of a recent run telling after going for home early; will improve and drop in trip should suit; each-way chance in first-time hood.
runners.comment: Useful in bumpers; shaped nicely over hurdles after a break lto; drop in trip will suit

runners.name: Firebrand
runners.spotlight: Flat maiden winner; 0-9 over hurdles; best effort over obstacles when beaten 3.75l behind subsequent Grade 1 winner Eachtotheirown at Galway in October; ran to similar level when 5.5l behind Mullaghgloss at Thurles 198 days ago; rated 110 and has experience edge under claimer.
runners.comment: Solid efforts last twice; rated 110; experience edge with nine runs over hurdles

runners.name: Rosie's Wings
runners.spotlight: Decent bumper form and improved a bit on hurdles debut after lay-off to be beaten 1l into third by Figero at Kilbeggan 22 days ago for new stable, running on well; form of that can be questioned but he should step forward for that.
runners.comment: Decent bumper runs; improved a bit on hurdles after lay-off when third; should improve

runners.name: Ludus Magnus
runners.spotlight: Couple of fair efforts in bumpers; ran well on hurdles debut when beaten 17.5l by Hey Now at Sligo 25 days ago; shaped as if in need of further so drop in trip not ideal.
runners.comment: Fair bumper performer; ran well on hurdles debut at Sligo but may need further

runners.name: The Weighbridge
runners.spotlight: Placed in one of two points; sent off a huge price in tough opening assignment at Ballinrobe 12 days ago and was beaten 30l into seventh by Daddy Long Legs in conditions hurdle; these are much calmer waters but still needs plenty more.
runners.comment: Tough assignment at Ballinrobe last time when beaten 30l; needs plenty more

runners.name: Ferrybank
runners.spotlight: Five-time winner on the Flat, below form on last two runs in that sphere, last seen finishing tenth of 11 over extended 1m2f at Dundalk off a mark of 63; stays 1m; off 113 days; late comer to hurdles and makes debut.
runners.comment: Five-time winner on the Flat; below-par last twice; stays 1m; makes hurdles debut

runners.name: My Poetic Queen
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to winners Cotteemcavennigoal (2m hurdle) and Letters To Juliet (2m1f hurdle); dam point/2m6f hurdle winner, closely related to useful winners Dosco (2m1f-3m hurdle/chase) and Oscargo (3m hurdle/chase); market can guide on debut.
runners.comment: Poet's Word mare; half-sister to two winners (2m hurdle to 2m1f); market can guide

runners.name: Shing Shilla
runners.spotlight: Low-level handicapper and a 16-race maiden; rated 75 and likely to struggle here.
runners.comment: Low-level handicapper and a 16-race maiden; rated 75 and likely to struggle here

runners.name: Pixie Hill
runners.spotlight: Pulled up on sole point outing and tailed off on hurdles debut at Limerick nine days ago.
runners.comment: Pulled up on sole point outing and tailed off on hurdles debut at Limerick nine days ago

runners.name: Easter Bonnet
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to six winners, including Chart Topper and Pink In The Park for same yard; sent off short odds on bumper debut at this track in October, won cosily by 6.5l; reportedly lost action when pulled up in Listed contest at Navan 202 days ago; should bounce back after a break; form of debut win very questionable but the one to beat on hurdles debut nonetheless; yard has won last three runnings.
runners.comment: Well-bred bumper winner from top yard; lost action when PU lto; one to beat on hurdles bow

runners.name: Inver Bay
runners.spotlight: Has only beaten a handful of rivals in seven starts, one of them over hurdles; off 192 days and no reason to suggest she'll be in the mix.
runners.comment: Only beaten a handful of rivals in seven starts; off 192 days; up against it

runners.name: Matcho d'Agrostis
runners.spotlight: Was making little impression on both hurdle outings prior to falling; hard to make a case for.
runners.comment: Was making little impression on both hurdle outings before falling; hard to make case for

runners.name: Finns First
runners.spotlight: Finished tenth in a pair of maiden hurdles this year and looks one for handicaps.
runners.comment: Finished tenth in a pair of maiden hurdles this year and looks one for handicaps

runners.name: Glendaars Crystal
runners.spotlight: Finished seventh in a good maiden hurdle on debut at this track 24 days ago but was beaten out of sight; needs a lot more.
runners.comment: Beaten out of sight in good maiden hurdle on debut; needs plenty more

runners.name: Hardlee Trouble
runners.spotlight: Couldn't land a blow when tailed off on debut over hurdles at Sligo 25 days ago; unlikely to be a threat.
runners.comment: Couldn't land a blow tailed off on debut over hurdles at Sligo; unlikely to be a threat

runners.name: Spellitoutforme
runners.spotlight: Decent pedigree, dam sister to 2m6f hurdle/chase winner and closely related to smart Royal Rendezvous; tailed off on debut at Punchestown 24 days ago; more of long-term project.
runners.comment: Tailed off on debut at Punchestown 24 days ago; more of long-term project

runners.name: Superficial
runners.spotlight: Sole win in eight Flat starts came last time out in 1m4f Roscommon handicap 19 days ago, staying on to beat subsequent winner Obscenity by 2.75l; had two prior hurdle runs at backend of last year and didn't show a huge aptitude but is in better form now and has been gelded; rated 80 on the Flat and could go well.
runners.comment: Didn't show much in two hurdle runs last year but good Flat win lto and has been gelded

runners.name: Urinmydreams
runners.spotlight: Nothing of note in a bumper and two maiden hurdles; hard to see featuring in the finish.
runners.comment: Nothing of note in a bumper and two maiden hurdles; hard to see featuring in the finish

runners.name: Drumadeise
runners.spotlight: Sent off three-figure price on hurdles debut at Limerick nine days ago and ran accordingly; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Sent off three-figure price on hurdles debut at Limerick, ran accordingly; hard to fancy

runners.name: Evangelic
runners.spotlight: Modest on the Flat in seven starts and didn't show anything in two recent hurdle starts to suggest he's up to winning a maiden.
runners.comment: Modest on the Flat; nothing to suggest he can win a maiden in two hurdle starts

runners.name: Bethpage Theatre
runners.spotlight: Fell halfway on hurdles debut at Cork and tailed off ninth at Listowel last time; likely to struggle.
runners.comment: Fell on hurdles debut at Cork; tailed off ninth at Listowel lto; unlikely to figure

runners.name: Jukebox Jolene
runners.spotlight: Made some late progress when beaten 27l in maiden hurdle at Down Royal on debut 217 days ago; unlikely she will find enough improvement to threaten main players here.
runners.comment: Reasonable debut when beaten 27l in maiden hurdle 217 days ago; unlikely to figure

races.race_name: Ladbrokes 'Big Daily Boosts On Racing' Maiden Hurdle (Div II)

runners.name: Livio
runners.spotlight: Won a Grade 3 AQPS Flat race at Lyon-Parilly (1m4f, very soft) in September 2024; encouraging 2.25l third of 12 in maiden hurdle at Killarney (2m4f, gd-yld) last month after mammoth layoff; ought to be sharper now and leading claims dropped in trip for top yard.
runners.comment: Gr 3 AQPS winner in France in 2024, sharper for Killarney comeback third, leading claims

runners.name: Alaskan View
runners.spotlight: Tramore bumper winner failed to make much of an impression in a maiden hurdle back there (2m, good) a week ago when 33-1; much more needed.
runners.comment: Tramore bumper winner, well beaten in maiden hurdle there a week ago, more needed

runners.name: Lizzie Twigg
runners.spotlight: Scored in a Down Royal mares' bumper on debut (17.5f, yielding) in March; came up short but not disgraced when a 12l fifth in a 2m Listed mares' bumper at Fairyhouse in April; worth a market check on hurdles debut for top yard.
runners.comment: Down Royal bumper winner, not disgraced in Listed bumper latest, top yard, check market

runners.name: Sestini
runners.spotlight: Modest Flat winner in France made a very pleasing stable and hurdling debut when a 3.75l third of 14 at Cork (17.5f, good) when a 33-1 shot; the runner up from that race won a maiden hurdle by 15l next time and the winner was placed in Listed company on the Flat; should come on for that first run for six months; interesting.
runners.comment: Form of Cork stable debut third boosted by the runner up next time, interesting runner

runners.name: Little Ginger
runners.spotlight: Built on promise shown on second bumper start at Limerick (19.5f, sft-hvy) to score in 18.5f (good) bumper at Kilbeggan 22 days ago; the third from that race has since finished third in a Killarney maiden hurdle; bred to make a jumper; player.
runners.comment: Won Kilbeggan bumper latest, bred to make a jumper, big player on hurdles' debut

runners.name: Coolishael
runners.spotlight: Placed in one of two point-to-points; promising start to life under Rules with a 3.75l third of 13 in a bumper here (2m, gd-yld) 24 days ago; half-brother to a winning hurdler and should go well.
runners.comment: Placed in a point and on rules debut in a bumper here, should go well on hurdles debut

runners.name: Million Dollar Man
runners.spotlight: Pulled up in sole point-to-point; outran odds of 200-1 when weakening late on into a 12l sixth of 17 on hurdles debut at Clonmel (19.5f, gd-yld) behind now 120 rated rival; this shorter trip should suit on that evidence; could go well.
runners.comment: Outran odds of 200-1 on hurdles debut at Clonmel (19.5f), drop in trip a plus, go well

runners.name: Jet To Glory
runners.spotlight: Runner up in four runner mares' maiden point-to-point at Durrow (soft); pulled up previously on good ground at Necarne; likely best watched on Rules' debut.
runners.comment: Runner up in mares' maiden point at Durrow, likely best watched on Rules' debut
runners.owner: Claire Wylie & Mrs Orla Cleary-O'Kane & Mark McGuckin

runners.name: Navy Waters
runners.spotlight: Useful two-time Flat winner rated 85 in that sphere; ground versatile and not beaten far when fifth in the Cork Derby on latest; dam won over hurdles so could take to this game; could go well.
runners.comment: Useful on the Flat and rated 85, dam won over hurdles, could go well

runners.name: Dorie Miller
runners.spotlight: Showed promise in a Fairyhouse bumper for Noel Meade but yet to show much since joining this yard; 66-1 and beaten when unseating at the last at Kilbeggan on latest; cheekpieces now tried.
runners.comment: Hasn't shown much for this yard, beaten when unseating latest, cheekpieces tried

runners.name: Scotty Joe
runners.spotlight: Point-to-point winner sent off at 11-1 for hurdles debut at Kilbeggan (18.5f, good) and stayed on for a 15l sixth of 16; jockey reported he was one paced and jumped slowly so this drop in trip may not suit.
runners.comment: Stayed on for a 15l sixth over 18.5f at Kilbeggan, not sure this drop in trip will suit

runners.name: Jodoro
runners.spotlight: Showed ability in two bumpers last year; pulled up when 150-1 on hurdling debut at Down Royal (2m1f, yld-sft) in January, however, and beaten 22l in a Kilbeggan bumper on latest in April; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Definite ability in two bumpers, pulled up when 150-1 on hurdles debut, others

runners.name: Rosie Brown
runners.spotlight: No impact in three bumper starts; Sligo third on penultimate was a remote one; best watched on hurdling debut.
runners.comment: Modest form in three bumper starts, best watched on hurdles debut

runners.name: Glendaars Secret
runners.spotlight: Not disgraced at long odds in maiden hurdles at Down Royal and Downpatrick but will likely be seen to better effect when sent handicapping.
runners.comment: Not disgraced at big prices in first two maidens, qualifies for handicaps after this

runners.name: Undertheinfluence
runners.spotlight: Not a bad introduction when midfield in a Downpatrick maiden hurdle two weeks ago (2m3f, gd-yld); this drop in trip should suit but more needed.
runners.comment: Midfield over 2m3f on hurdles debut at Downpatrick, drop in trip a plus, more needed

runners.name: Cool As A Cat
runners.spotlight: Unseated early on debut at Fairyhouse; beaten 81l over C&D on latest; may need more time.
runners.comment: Unseated early at Fairyhouse and beaten 91l over C&D latest, may need more time

runners.name: Mount Mandela
runners.spotlight: 0.5l second in a 2m Tramore maiden hurdle (yielding) for Philip Rothwell in October 2024; lightly raced since and beaten 19.5l when third of 7 on second start for this yard in a Down Royal maiden hurdle last October; might need this run after 218 days off.
runners.comment: Close second in Tramore maiden hurdle for former yard in 2024, might need this after break

runners.name: Rattledelatch
runners.spotlight: Unlikely winner based on moderate efforts in a bumper here and maiden hurdles at Gowran and Wexford; hood replaces cheekpieces worn last time.
runners.comment: Unlikely winner based on efforts in a bumper and two maiden hurdles, hooded
runners.owner: John Joseph Flynn & Conor Lannen & Mrs Miriam Queally

runners.name: Cometothinkofit
runners.spotlight: Heavy defeats at huge odds in a trio of maiden hurdles at 15.5f-2m4f; would be a surprise winner.
runners.comment: Heavy defeats at huge odds in first three maiden hurdles, would be a surprise winner

runners.name: Anam Geal
runners.spotlight: Beat just one rival home on debut in a Tipperary bumper in July of last year and off since; can only be watched for now.
runners.comment: Beat just one rival home on debut at Tipperary last July, off since, watch

runners.name: Ancient Myth
runners.spotlight: Flat winner has been soundly beaten at long odds in maiden hurdles at Killarney and Ballinrobe; will do better when handicapping.
runners.comment: Flat winner, no impact at big odds in two previous maiden hurdles, one for handicaps

runners.name: Good Thoughts
runners.spotlight: Fell three out on hurdles debut at Fairyhouse in November; 125-1 and beaten 23l over this C&D later that month; off since and can only be watched for now in first-time hood unless the market speaks.
runners.comment: Fell and beaten 23l in first two starts over hurdles, hood goes on, watch unless backed

runners.name: Tiffany Mae
runners.spotlight: Modest Flat maiden and no sign of winning potential in first two starts over hurdles; new yard now; look elsewhere.
runners.comment: Modest Flat maiden, poor first two efforts over hurdles, new yard now, look elsewhere

runners.name: Glengarriff
runners.spotlight: Modest Flat maiden didn't make much of an impression when 250-1 for his hurdling debut at Thurles; others preferred.
runners.comment: Modest Flat maiden, no impression at 250-1 on hurdles debut, others preferred

races.race_name: Ladbrokes 'Get More With Ladbucks' Maiden Hurdle

runners.name: Angelo Dundee
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner at this track on debut three years ago; ran with credit in four winners' bumpers since despite a stop-start career; promising hurdles debut for new yard when beaten 6l at Down Royal (2m1f, good) 33 days ago, still signs of greenness after nearly a year off; could improve a lot and a leading contender with step up in trip sure to suit.
runners.comment: Very useful in bumpers; good hurdles debut 33 days ago; should improve plenty; big chance

runners.name: Brother Walfrid
runners.spotlight: Very promising debut in Fairyhouse maiden hurdle when 3.75l second to Colcannon in April; didn't reproduce that switched to a bumper 11 days ago at Ballinrobe when beaten favourite into fourth, looking tapped for toe; step up in trip back over hurdles will suit and he holds a leading chance.
runners.comment: Promising hurdles debut; below-par bumper run lto; step up in trip over hurdles will suit

runners.name: Frankie's Freebie
runners.spotlight: Point winner who ran some nice races when placed in maiden hurdles behind Zanoosh, Rebel Budz and Fillyoureye; below-par the last twice, however, including in handicap company at Fairyhouse 62 days ago; not finishing out his races and needs to bounce back.
runners.comment: Point winner with some good placed form but needs to bounce back after two poor runs

runners.name: Petit Secret
runners.spotlight: Made winning debut in Fairyhouse bumper in December 2023, beating the smart Bleu De Vassy; returned from long absence on hurdles debut when beaten 9.5l by Playtime at Leopardstown in March; too keen and a bad mistake when tailed off at Fairyhouse 63 days ago; tongue-tie goes on now and one not to give up on for top yard.
runners.comment: Bumper winner; returned from long absence with good hurdle run at Leop; best ignore latest

runners.name: Marble Rile
runners.spotlight: Westerner mare; half-sister to point winner Kings Cave; dam bumper winner, half-sister to 2m chase winner Cango, out of unraced sister to smart 2m3f-2m6f hurdle/chase winner El Vaquero; wears hood on debut.
runners.comment: Westerner mare; half-sister to point winner; dam bumper winner; wears hood on debut

runners.name: Backinbusiness
runners.spotlight: Placed in point; encouraging debut over hurdles when beaten 3.5l into fourth at Kilbeggan 22 days ago; will improve and extra furlong likely to suit on pedigree.
runners.comment: Encouraging debut, fourth in maiden hurdle 22 days ago; will improve; extra trip to suit

runners.name: Misery Hill
runners.spotlight: Well-held on both starts thus far over 2m at Kilbeggan and extended 2m6f at Ballinrobe; unlikely to be winning a maiden based on that.
runners.comment: Unlikely to be winning a maiden based on his two starts thus far

runners.name: Phoenix Lights
runners.spotlight: Fascinating Rock gelding, cost 9,000euros as a 3yo; half-brother to winners Dolphin Square (useful 2m2f-3m2f hurdle/chase) and Game Of War (2m-2m2f hurdle/chase); dam point winner, half-sister to winners Foxspur (2m4f hurdle) and Its Ironic (point/bumper).
runners.comment: Cost 9,000 euros as a 3yo; half-brother to two winners (2m2f-3m2f hurdle/chase); debut

runners.name: Ol Parson
runners.spotlight: Ol'Man River gelding; half-brother to winners Ballygrifincottage (useful 2m6f chase/2m7f hurdle) and Brown Boots (3m hurdle/chase); dam point winner, closely related to 2m5f-2m7f hurdle chase and multiple point winner Patricktom Boru; second and fourth in latest points; makes rules debut.
runners.comment: Half-brother to two winners (2m6f -3m); second and fourth in completed points

runners.name: Rosenborg
runners.spotlight: Muhtathir gelding, half-brother to winners Castelfort (2m hurdle) and Mare Sol (French 2m1f chase); dam unplaced 12.5f Flat/2m1f hurdles in France, half-sister to French Listed-placed 2m1f-2m3f hurdle/chase winner Bal Celtique; finished third, beaten 11l in latest point in April; makes rules debut
runners.comment: Half-brother to two winners (2m hurdle-2m1f chase); third on sole completed point; debut

runners.name: Siofra Beag
runners.spotlight: Very poor form in five starts on the Flat; similar standard when sent hurdling when pulled up at this track 24 days ago.
runners.comment: Very poor form in five starts on the Flat; similar story on hurdles debut here 24 days ago

runners.name: Ri Ra
runners.spotlight: Big prices and nothing of note in pair of bumpers and maiden hurdles; hard to recommend.
runners.comment: Big prices and nothing of note in pair of bumpers and maiden hurdles; hard to recommend

runners.name: Forlio
runners.spotlight: Dual Flat winner, rated 62; ran well when beaten 23l into tenth by Gomez Addams on hurdles bow at Listowel seven days ago; jumping needs to improve and he has a future in this discipline, but probably in handicaps.
runners.comment: Dual Flat winner; ran well on hurdles debut lto but more one for handicaps

runners.name: Little Big John
runners.spotlight: Pulled up and tailed off at Fairyhouse and Clonmel on first two starts; hood and tongue-tie reached for.
runners.comment: Pulled up and tailed off on first two starts; hood and tongue-tie on now

runners.name: Switched On
runners.spotlight: Decent pedigree, out of Irish Oaks second Roses For The Lady; beaten out of sight on debut in heavy ground bumper at Limerick 200 days ago; makes hurdles debut.
runners.comment: Beaten out of sight in heavy ground bumper at Limerick 200 days ago; hurdles debut

runners.name: Minella Jackpot
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in point before 30l into 16th on debut in a good bumper here a year ago, unable to sustain challenge; pulled up in point since in November and returns after a break.
runners.comment: Well-held on debut in a good bumper a year ago; pulled up in point in Nov; back from break

runners.name: Vangaway
runners.spotlight: Hasn't shown anything in two starts over hurdles, finishing last of the finishers both times; off 373 days.
runners.comment: Hasn't shown anything in two starts over hurdles; off 373 days

races.race_name: Castlestone Holiday Homes (Pro/Am) INH Flat Race

runners.name: Returning Officer
runners.spotlight: Form of debut Navan sixth in February 2025 has worked out well; failed to build on that when a 7.75l fifth of 11 in a lesser race at Kilbeggan last month but may have needed that after a 460-day absence; better expected now.
runners.comment: Form of debut sixth strong, may have needed latest after long layoff, better expected now

runners.name: Working Class Hero
runners.spotlight: Placed in a point-to-point; excellent efforts in defeat in both starts over C&D; stayed on to be a 12l runner up on Rules' debut (yielding) and beaten a head on good to yielding 24 days ago when ridden more positively; leading form claims.
runners.comment: Runner up both starts over C&D, beaten a head on latest, leading form claims

runners.name: She's A Divil
runners.spotlight: Vadamos filly; unraced dam closely related to bumper/2m hurdle winner Frontline, half-sister to 1m6f Flat and smart 2m-2m2f hurdle/chase winner Castlegrace Paddy, out of 2m Flat/2m hurdle winner; some appeal on paper and worth checking in the market.
runners.comment: Vadamos filly, dam closely related to bumper winner, worth checking in market

runners.name: Dee Bee Zed
runners.spotlight: Gleneagles gelding; closely related to 1m1f-1m4f Flat (inc AW)/Australian 2m hurdle winner Impulsar, half-brother to Listed-placed 7f/1m1f winner Silver Legend; dam unraced half-sister to 1m Group 2 winner; nicely bred but yard 0-7 in bumpers last five seasons.
runners.comment: Gleneagles gelding, dam half-sister to 1m Gr' 2 winner, yard 0-7 in bumpers last 5 seasons

runners.name: Ealu Tapa
runners.spotlight: Homebred Getaway gelding; dam placed 1m5f, sister to yard's high class jumper Peregrine Run and Takeyourcapoff (1m6f Flat and useful 2m-2m4f hurdle), out of bumper winner; likely type on paper worth a market check.
runners.comment: Getaway gelding, dam sister to yard's classy jumper Peregrine Run, worth a market check

runners.name: Farney Bridge
runners.spotlight: Casamento gelding; dam unraced half-sister to winners Absolutely Doyen (useful 2m4f-3m1f hurdle) and Highway Jewel (3m1f chase), out of unraced half-sister to bumper winner; may need further than this in time.
runners.comment: Casamento gelding, dam unraced, may need further than this in time

runners.name: Go Loud
runners.spotlight: Failed to trouble the judge in four starts in point-to-points; likely best watched.
runners.comment: 0-4 in points and failed to trouble the judge, best watched

runners.name: Hurricane Leaf
runners.spotlight: Not disgraced on debut in a Listowel bumper when a 15l seventh of 10; didn't build on that at Sligo last time; much more needed.
runners.comment: Not disgraced on debut at Listowel, didn't build on that on Sligo last time

runners.name: It's Buster's Way
runners.spotlight: Doyen gelding; E21,000 foal; dam unraced half-sister to winners Kalashnikov (high-class 2m-2m4f hurdle/chase), Kalane (useful 2m-2m4f hurdle/chase) and Holdbacktheriver (2m6f-3m2f hurdle/chase), from family of Kicking King; check the market.
runners.comment: Doyen gelding, dam half-sister to high class jumper Kalashnikov, check market

runners.name: Lauro James
runners.spotlight: Lauro gelding pulled up in both point-to-points; can only be watched on Rules' debut.
runners.comment: Lauro gelding, pulled up in two points, can only be watched on Rules' debut

runners.name: Sir Vinny
runners.spotlight: Walk In The Park gelding; dam unraced sister to 9.4f winner Aiseolas, half-sister to useful 7f 2yo/1m winner Home School; worth checking in the market for in-form yard.
runners.comment: Walk In The Park gelding, dam unraced, yard in good form, market can guide

runners.name: Union Boy
runners.spotlight: Workforce gelding; dam point/2m5f hurdle winner, out of half-sister to bumper winning dam of useful 2m/2m4f hurdle/chase winner Cruising Katie, is granddaughter of useful 2m hurdle winner; market check advised.
runners.comment: Workforce gelding, dam point/2m5f winner, market check advised

runners.name: Woodie Wiley
runners.spotlight: El Salvador gelding pulled up in point-to-points at Dromahane and Ballindenisk just over a year apart; can only watch on Rules' debut.
runners.comment: Pulled up in both points just over a year apart, can only watch on Rules' debut

runners.name: Dieux Diligence
runners.spotlight: Saddex gelding; dam placed 1m4f Flat (RPR 74)/2m hurdle winner, half-sister to French winners Hillflower (2m-2m3f hurdles) and Yo Minfoutaie (2m1f hurdle); looks yard's main hope on rider arrangements.
runners.comment: Saddex gelding, dam 2m hurdle winner, looks yard's main hope on rider arrangements

runners.name: Iron Sword
runners.spotlight: For Fun gelding; dam ran three times in France, best effort at 1m4f (RPR 78), half-sister to winners Celtic Art (useful 1m-1m4f Flat inc 2yo; 2m/2m3f hurdle) and Warm Love (French 9.7f-1m3f); yard has success in this sphere; check market.
runners.comment: For Fun gelding, dam modest on Flat in France, respected source, check market

runners.name: Seamy Appletart
runners.spotlight: Blue Bresil gelding; dam placed 2m3f hurdle, sister to useful 2m4f/2m5f hurdle winner Atlantic Fairy; may need further than this in time.
runners.comment: Blue Bresil gelding, dam sister to useful 2m4f/2m5f winner, may need further in time

runners.name: Which Flannerys
runners.spotlight: Order Of St George gelding; closely related to 2m1f hurdle winner Custom Of The Sea, half-brother to 1m5f/2m Flat/2m-2m4f hurdle winner An Bradan Feasa; dam unraced half-sister to high-class jumper Kalashnikov; looks yard's second string on jockey bookings but still worth a market check.
runners.comment: Order Of St George gelding, related to jumps and Flat winners, market can guide

runners.name: An Bhoireann
runners.spotlight: No impact in two point-to-points; triple figure odds and beaten 82l and 25l in first two bumpers; tongue tie goes on now.
runners.comment: Triple figure odds and beaten 82l and 25l in first two bumpers, tongue tie on

runners.name: Noble Trix
runners.spotlight: Tailed off when last of 12 in a Down Royal bumper on debut last month; unlikely.
runners.comment: Tailed off in Down Royal bumper on debut last month, unlikely

runners.name: Harbour Man
runners.spotlight: 150-1 and tailed off on debut at Fairyhouse in November; seems safe to rule out.
runners.comment: 150-1 and tailed off on debut at Fairyhouse in November; seems safe to rule out

races.race_name: FBC Manby Bowdler Mares' Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier) (GBB Race)

runners.name: She Is For Me Boys
runners.spotlight: 1-6 over fences; returned to form of late and although a beaten favourite when runner-up at Fakenham (2m5f, good) last month, that was still a creditable effort; tongue-tie now added; the one to beat.
runners.comment: Returned to form lately; solid second at Fakenham last month and looks the one to beat.

runners.name: Laffer Curve
runners.spotlight: Showed promise in maiden/novice hurdles before running respectably when third on handicap debut at Huntingdon (2m4f, good) in March; finished weakly over a slightly longer trip at Newton Abbot (2m5f, good) the following month but has since undergone wind surgery; now switches to fences from what looks a workable mark (eased 2lb) and commands respect.
runners.comment: Promising hurdler; wind surgery since latest run and respected on chase debut.

runners.name: Charity Barnum
runners.spotlight: Irish raider; 13-race maiden whose best effort came over hurdles when a close second at Tramore (2m5f, good) last August, but three attempts over fences have seen her pulled up twice and tailed off once; Sean Bowen booked and, while a return to better ground and handicap company may help, she still has plenty to prove.
runners.comment: Irish raider; 13-race maiden who has struggled over fences but the better ground may help.

runners.name: Miss Denver
runners.spotlight: Well-bred mare; kept on for a respectable third on handicap hurdle debut at Newton Abbot (2m1f, good) last month and shaped reasonably well on chase debut at Stratford (2m1f, good to firm) where she jumped soundly; pedigree suggests this longer trip should suit and a first-time hood may also eke out a little more, now eased 2lb.
runners.comment: Fair third on handicap debut; shaped okay on chase bow; hood is added and now 2lb lower.

races.race_name: CopyBet Supporting UK Horse Racing Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier)

runners.name: The Flying Poet
runners.spotlight: Broke his duck over hurdles after a few near misses and made a winning start to fences at Perth (3m) on his return from a break last month, albeit narrowly with the second making a costly error at the last; lacked fluency there but kept on well and, although a 6lb higher mark asks a question, another bold bid can be expected.
runners.comment: Scored over hurdles after some near misses then won chase debut; respected off 6lb higher.

runners.name: Redbridge Rambler
runners.spotlight: Showed the benefit of April's reappearance when back up to a more suitable stamina test off a reduced mark and winning at Warwick (3m, good) the following month; back up 3lb but Freddie Keighley can claim his full allowance; a big player if repeating the effort.
runners.comment: Improved on reappearance to win at Warwick; up 3lb but F Keighley claims 5lb; big chance.

runners.name: Gata Ban
runners.spotlight: 0-7 over fences but ran big race in defeat when second at Fontwell (3m2f, good) last month, looking the likely winner until collared on run-in; one of two for the stable and a player off same mark again today.
runners.comment: 0-7 over fences but a close second at Fontwell latest, caught late on; leading player.

runners.name: Hall Lane
runners.spotlight: 1-13 over fences but has been shaping as though his turn is near again after finishing runner-up on his last three starts, all around this trip on good ground when ridden by Jack Hogan; likely to be in the mix again off an unchanged mark.
runners.comment: 1-13 over fences, but shaping as though his turn in near after three consecutive seconds.

runners.name: Jullou De Grissay
runners.spotlight: On the same mark as when winning over hurdles (2m7f, good) last summer, ridden by James King; shaped as though the run was needed in March on return from an absence, before finishing a well-beaten second of three on chase debut the following month; has since undergone wind surgery and retains potential over fences.
runners.comment: Same mark as hurdle win; chase debut second and wind surgery since; retains potential.
runners.owner: The Galloping Dentist At 45 The Green

runners.name: Est Illic
runners.spotlight: Point winner in April but his rules form is modest; although placed under Jess Stewart on both recent runs in hunter chases, he's been well held; needs to find plenty more back in handicap company.
runners.comment: Point winner in April but modest rules form; needs big step up.

runners.name: Captain Boudet
runners.spotlight: Won over 2m3f at Exeter off 1lb lower in March 2025; inconsistent in five starts last season for Jeremy Scott, signing off with a fair second back at Exeter two and a half months ago; has changed hands since and goes back up in trip on stable debut with a visor added; on a good mark if able to put his best foot forward.
runners.comment: Won at Exeter off 1lb lower but inconsistent since; new stable; visor on; back up in trip.

runners.name: Axel Bleue
runners.spotlight: Point and dual bumper winner in 2025; runner-up in two 2m7f novice hurdles in October but pulled up on handicap debut the following month and has offered little in two runs this spring since a break, including when beaten 24l on last month's chase debut; probably on a workable mark but needs the cheekpieces to have a positive effect.
runners.comment: Point winner; modest since hurdles promise; cheekpieces on but he needs a major step up.

runners.name: Jack To Bat
runners.spotlight: £130,000 buy after runner-up in an Irish point in 2023 and showed promise over hurdles, but his two efforts since switched to fences/handicaps this year have been disappointing; wore this headgear when a remote fourth of four at Wetherby (3m, good to soft) in March and has since undergone wind surgery.
runners.comment: £130,000 buy; promising hurdler but disappointing over fences so far; wind surgery since.
runners.owner: Martyn & Elaine Booth & The Maiden Over

runners.name: Stay Hopeful
runners.spotlight: Irish point winner who has yet to show any worthwhile form over hurdles (2m-2m4f) on varying ground, including since switched to handicaps on his last two starts and fitted with cheekpieces; not entirely dismissed on this switch to fences off a basement mark but he needs to step up.
runners.comment: Irish point winner who has yet to show much over hurdles; makes his chase debut.

runners.name: Jongleur d'Etoiles
runners.spotlight: Multiple hurdles winner at up to 3m2f, including on a sound surface; 0-6 over fences but went very close at Doncaster (3m2f, good to soft) in March having looked the likely winner; leading contender from an unchanged mark, if repeating that effort.
runners.comment: Multiple hurdle winner; went close over fences at Doncaster latest; contender again.

runners.name: Atlanta Brave
runners.spotlight: Hasn't won for three years and struggles for consistency; made an encouraging return when third at Hereford (3m1f, good) in March but couldn't back it up when well beaten at Bangor in refitted cheekpieces last time; blinkers now replace them but others appeal more.
runners.comment: No win for three years; third on return but poor since; blinkers on but others preferred.

races.race_name: Law Without The Horsing Around Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier)

runners.name: Jiair Madrik
runners.spotlight: Took advantage of a declining mark to open his account over fences at Uttoxeter (2m5f, good) three weeks ago, on his second start following wind surgery; there should be more to come with that win under his belt and he may well follow up.
runners.comment: Took advantage of a declining mark to win over fences at Uttoxeter three weeks ago.

runners.name: Majestic Moment
runners.spotlight: 3-7 over fences and remains in form following his second at Fontwell (2m3f, good) 13 days ago; may not have finished progressing yet and entitled to be on the shortlist.
runners.comment: 3-7 over fences and remains in form following his second at Fontwell 13 days ago.

runners.name: Culligran
runners.spotlight: Scored on her second chase start at Newton Abbot (2m5f, good) last June for John O'Shea; absent since last July and makes stable debut; best watched unless the market speaks in her favour.
runners.comment: Won on her second chase start at Newton Abbot last June; absent since last July.

runners.name: Noble Recall
runners.spotlight: Improved to win off lowly hurdle mark at Southwell (2m4f, good) in May last year; made satisfactory chase debut when fourth over C&D (good) last September but has been off since; this looks a tough assignment on his return.
runners.comment: Made satisfactory chase debut when fourth over C&D last September but has been off since.

runners.name: Holy Legend
runners.spotlight: Opened his account over fences with an emphatic win at Fontwell (2m2f, good) last month; will find it tougher off 10lb higher but should be kept onside.
runners.comment: Opened his account over fences with an emphatic win at Fontwell last month.

runners.name: Applicationofcash
runners.spotlight: Responsible for both of his new trainer's wins, the latest at this track (2m, good) 15 days ago; didn't appear to see his race out when tried over today's distance at Wincanton in April but he is clearly in good order.
runners.comment: Responsible for both of his new trainer's wins, the latest at this track 15 days ago.

runners.name: Pooroldmackley
runners.spotlight: Scored over fences in April 2025 on heavy, and has won twice over hurdles since but has lost his form in that sphere; now returns to fences; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Has lost his way over hurdles; hard to fancy on return to chasing

runners.name: The Moonlight Man
runners.spotlight: Point winner but has been pulled up on five of his seven starts under rules; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Point winner but has been pulled up on five of his seven starts under rules.

runners.name: Song Of Earth
runners.spotlight: Sole success over fences was when trained in Ireland in April 2022; plenty of water under the bridge since and was well below form on all three starts last term; easy to oppose.
runners.comment: Sole success over fences was when trained in Ireland in April 2022; poor since.
runners.owner: Mrs J A Benson & Miss S N Benson

races.race_name: Manby Not Mandy Open National Hunt Flat Race (Category 1 Elimination) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Redbarn
runners.spotlight: 50,000euros foal, 30,000euros 3yo, £25,000 Doncaster May 26; fourth foal; dam unraced sister to high-class French staying chaser Rubi Ball; a beaten favourite in a couple of Irish points; she looks likely to show significant improvement in the hands of champion trainer on rules debut.
runners.comment: Beaten favourite in a couple of Irish points; looks likely to show big improvement.

runners.name: Ultimately
runners.spotlight: Beaten 27l on debut over C&D (good; 80-1) 15 days ago and may do better when he tackles hurdles.
runners.comment: Beaten 27l on debut over C&D 15 days ago and may do better when he tackles hurdles.

runners.name: Giant's Way
runners.spotlight: 58,000euros foal, 140,000euros 3yo; eighth foal; closely related to high-class 2m hurdler Labaik, half-brother to winners Danza De La Barre (French 7f-9.5f; RPR 72) and Aiguille Rouge (2m2f hurdle); dam French 5.5f winner (100), out of German 7f Listed winner; yard in decent nick at present and he is one to be interested in; tongue-tie fitted for debut.
runners.comment: Closely related to high-class 2m hurdler Labaik; yard in decent nick; interesting

runners.name: Litaque
runners.spotlight: 10,000euros 3yo, £5,000 Doncaster May 25; eighth foal; half-brother to winners Grozni (bumper and useful 2m4f-4m hurdle/chase) and Donskoye (French 2m6f chase); dam unraced half-sister to smart 2m1f-3m hurdle/chase winner Val De Ferbet; landed maiden point with the minimum of fuss 15 days ago, and could be hard to beat on this rules debut.
runners.comment: Landed maiden point with the minimum of fuss 15 days ago, and could be hard to beat.

runners.name: Delusionofgrandeur
runners.spotlight: 5,700euros foal, 13,000euros 3yo; third foal; dam 2m hurdle winner, half-sister to useful 2m-2m4f hurdle/chase Golden Hoof; beaten in all six maiden Irish points but in good hands and likely to show improvement under rules.
runners.comment: Beaten in all six maiden Irish points but in good hands and likely to show improvement.

runners.name: Emerald Tir
runners.spotlight: £16,000 3yo; sixth foal; dam unraced sister to high-class 2m-3m1f hurdle/chase winner Menorah, out of 2m4f-2m6f hurdle/chase winning half-sister to thorough staying jumper Jet Boys; may need the run on debut.
runners.comment: Out of unraced sister to high-class jumper Menorah; may need the run on debut

runners.name: Master Tim
runners.spotlight: Second foal; dam placed in bumpers, sister to point winners Beyondthestorm and Coningbeg, half-sister to 3m1f/3m2f chase winner Kimora, out of unraced sister to bumper winner Culmore Native, half-sister to bumper/2m-2m4f hurdle winner Finns Cross; beaten 17l in a point in April; makes rules debut for yard that has occasional bumper success.
runners.comment: Beaten 17l in a point in April; makes rules debut; yard has occasional bumper success.

runners.name: Storm Dudley
runners.spotlight: Second foal; dam 1m-1m4f maiden (RPR 65), closely related to useful 7f-1m4f winner Cay Dancer, half-sister to 1m2f Listed winner Dynamic, out of French 1m/1m1f winning half-sister to 1m Group 1 winner Balisada; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Out of a half-sister to a Listed Flat winner but others appeal more

runners.name: C'Est Pour Moi
runners.spotlight: Tailed off on debut at Worcester (2m, good; 8-1) 15 days ago with hood fitted (absent today); likely capable of better but big improvement required.
runners.comment: Tailed off on debut at Worcester 15 days ago.

races.race_name: #Bepartofit Novices' Hurdle (Arc Summer Novices' Brush Hurdle Series Qualifier) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Pioneer Pete
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark over hurdles at the fifth attempt in a Wetherby maiden (2m, good) in March; surpassed that with a cracking second in a big-field handicap at Punchestown (2m, yielding) in May and should be up to defying a 7lb penalty in this company.
runners.comment: Won Wetherby maiden in March; second in a big-field handicap at Punchestown in May.

runners.name: Miss Kingston
runners.spotlight: 0-6 when trained in Ireland; sold for 22,000gns to join current yard in January, and bolted up on her British debut in a Chepstow maiden (2m, good) in April; needs to be taken seriously even with a 7lb penalty.
runners.comment: Bolted up on her British debut in a Chepstow maiden in April.

runners.name: Molten Sea
runners.spotlight: Won three on the Flat for William Haggas; sold for 20,000gns to join this yard last October and scored on her second hurdles start at Kempton (2m good) in March; another good run when runner-up at Chepstow (2m, good to soft) the following month and should run her race.
runners.comment: Won on her second hurdles start at Kempton in March; another good run when second since.

runners.name: Mayo
runners.spotlight: Best effort in third of three bumpers when third of four at Hereford (2m, good to firm) last month on stable debut; hurdles debut and needs to show more.
runners.comment: Best effort in third of three bumpers when third at Hereford last month on stable debut.

runners.name: Little Lady Rock
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in three bumpers last summer when trained by Ryan Potter; has changed yards since and best watched on her hurdles debut.
runners.comment: Runner-up in three bumpers last summer when trained by Ryan Potter.

runners.name: Solo Eclipse
runners.spotlight: Made a promising start when runner-up in a bumper at Huntingdon (2m, soft) in February but hasn't progressed on good ground in a similar contest or on hurdles debut at Uttoxeter three weeks ago; may need soft to see him in a better light.
runners.comment: Runner-up in a bumper at Huntingdon in February but hasn't progressed since.
runners.owner: DL Adams, JA Adams & G McPherson

runners.name: Fine Fellow
runners.spotlight: Beaten 26l on hurdles debut at Warwick (2m, good; 25-1) four weeks ago; should do better once he gets a handicap mark.
runners.comment: Beaten 26l on hurdles debut at Warwick four weeks ago.

runners.name: Thatsnotmyname
runners.spotlight: Has shown only modest form in a bumper and on hurdles debut over C&D 15 days ago; easy to dismiss.
runners.comment: Has shown only modest form in a bumper and on hurdles debut over C&D 15 days ago.

races.race_name: Best Scrap Metal Prices CRS Malvern Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Castle Ivers
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner who made it two out of three over hurdles at Perth (2m, good to soft) when making all; looks sure to be suited by this step up in trip and holds good claims.
runners.comment: Bumper winner who made it two out of three over hurdles at Perth when making all.

runners.name: Oakley
runners.spotlight: Both wins over hurdles have came at Hereford over 2m3f on good; ran up to his best when runner-up at Fontwell (2m3f, good) last month; he needs to be held up to get the best out of him; this is a step up in class and he needs to improve further.
runners.comment: Both wins over hurdles have came at Hereford; runner-up at Fontwell last month.

runners.name: Trust House
runners.spotlight: Progressive in the spring when winning at Market Rasen and Ffos Las over around 2m; no disgrace in finishing runner-up to stablemate Castle Ivers at Perth (2m, good to soft) last time; Sean Bowen prefers Castle Ivers on this occasion but still a contender up in trip.
runners.comment: Progressive in the spring when winning at Market Rasen and Ffos Las over around 2m.

runners.name: Speed Davis
runners.spotlight: Completed a double, five months apart, when successful at Bangor (2m7f, good) in April; this is tougher off 7lb higher but may have more to offer in search of a three-timer.
runners.comment: Completed a double, five months apart, when successful at Bangor in April.

runners.name: Continuance
runners.spotlight: Has three wins over hurdles at around 2m; may have needed the run on his first start since January when fourth of eight at Warwick (2m, good) last month and this step up in trip should suit.
runners.comment: May have needed the run on first start since January when fourth at Warwick last month.

runners.name: Theatre Tom
runners.spotlight: Beaten in three points and in four novice/maiden hurdles; returns from a lengthy absence and makes handicap debut; likely to need the run; trainer also runs Castle Ivers and Trust House.
runners.comment: Beaten in three points and in four novice/maiden hurdles; returns from a lengthy absence.

runners.name: Bertie B
runners.spotlight: Has two wins over hurdles but was below his best in his last three starts, in 2025, and is still higher than his last winning mark; returns from a 411-day absence and may need to come down in the weights.
runners.comment: Has two wins over hurdles but was below his best in his last three starts, in 2025.

races.race_name: CopyBet Overnight Best Odds Guaranteed Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Pike Road
runners.spotlight: Dual 2m7f hurdle winner last year, latest success at Uttoxeter (soft) in November off 2lb lower; followed that with a solid effort in a Class 2 at Cheltenham; form has dipped since but recent return may have been needed and he's worth considering with that run behind him.
runners.comment: Dual hurdle winner; form dipped of late but not out of it if improved for comeback run.

runners.name: Impecunious
runners.spotlight: Two wins for Chris Down last year, latest at Newton Abbot (3m2f, good) off 3lb lower; well-held fourth of seven on stable/seasonal debut at Worcester (2m5f) but ran well for a long way; worth considering with that run behind him.
runners.comment: Should be sharper for last month's return on stable debut and he's worth considering.

runners.name: Eaton Anne
runners.spotlight: Dual hurdle winner who went close to breaking his duck over fences off this mark at Hexham (3m, good to soft) last month, with a tongue-tie added to refitted cheekpieces following a second wind operation; merits serious consideration back over hurdles with the stable in good form.
runners.comment: Reverts to hurdles after going close over fences latest; key player for in-form stable.

runners.name: Fairlawn Skipper
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner last year; yet to add to that in five hurdle starts but has shown ability, notably when third at Newcastle (2m7f, good) in October; below-par effort in November and well held on last month's reappearance at Perth (3m2f, good to soft); may come on from that but others appeal more.
runners.comment: Well held on last month's return; may come on from that but others appeal more.

runners.name: The Long Walk
