runners.spotlight: Mixed form last season; peak efforts included third at Market Rasen (2m2f) on Boxing Day and second off this mark at Hereford in April latest; not guaranteed to back that up but he's in the mix if repeating that effort with cheekpieces now added.
runners.comment: Runner-up latest and holds solid claims if backing that up with cheekpieces now added.

runners.name: Gwash
runners.spotlight: Yet to add to his sole win at Warwick (2m3f, good) over two years ago and recent evidence doesn't suggest a breakthrough is imminent; not dismissed completely but needs a revival.
runners.comment: Sole win came over two years ago and, though not entirely dismissed, a revival is needed.

runners.name: Lacrima
runners.spotlight: Completed a hat-trick over fences in France last autumn, all around 2m2f on deep ground, but has shown little in three handicap chase runs for this stable this year; reverts to hurdles off a lower mark and the step up in trip needs to spark a turnaround.
runners.comment: Switches to hurdles off a lower mark but the step up in trip needs to spark a turnaround.

runners.name: Ebony Warrior
runners.spotlight: Dual hurdle winner (2m-2m5f, good) but yet to win in handicap company and was out of sorts in a light campaign last season; shaped as though the run may have been needed on last month's reappearance but remains with something to prove on recent evidence despite a further ease in the weights.
runners.comment: Dual hurdle winner but something to prove on recent evidence despite a reduced mark.
runners.owner: DL Adams, JA Adams & G McPherson

runners.name: Peter's Last Deal
runners.spotlight: Yet to build on November's promising hurdle debut when third at Hereford (2m3f, good), with progress interrupted by absences and he was always out the back when tailed off on his comeback run 28 days ago; probably capable of better in time but still best watched on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Makes handicap debut; likely capable of better in time but probably best watched for now.

runners.name: Balkardy
runners.spotlight: 7-39, with three wins over hurdles, but he's hard to predict and was badly out of sorts in the autumn; unseated early on last month's return and reverts to hurdles off his last winning mark, but it's difficult to know what to expect.
runners.comment: Back over hurdles off his last winning mark, but it's difficult to know what to expect.

runners.name: Prince De Juilley
runners.spotlight: Came back to form in the autumn and gained a deserved success at Stratford (2m6f) in October, following that with two solid placed efforts off higher marks, latest at Hereford (3m2f) in November off 2lb lower and all on good ground; has a career-high mark to defy but is reliable and has gone well off a similar break before.
runners.comment: On a career-high mark and has an absence to overcome but he's reliable and can figure.

races.race_name: Michael Maine Memorial Cup Handicap

runners.name: Tomarlo
runners.spotlight: A fair ex-Irish 6f winner; offered little in three runs for Barry Brennan early in 2026 but much more positive signs for new yard with fourth of eight in 5f Wolverhampton handicap last month, keeping on late after tardy start; a bold showing is on the cards eased 1lb.
runners.comment: Ex-Irish 6f winner; encouraging Wolverhampton fourth for new yard latest; big shout here

runners.name: Son Of Astar
runners.spotlight: A three-time 5f winner in 2025; yet to hit top form this season though, going without headgear after three months off when last of 12 in 6f handicap here 16 days ago; blinkers return but he needs to get back on track.
runners.comment: Last of 12 in 6f handicap here 16 days ago; blinkers are now reapplied

runners.name: Some Nightmare
runners.spotlight: A three-time C&D scorer (twice last summer) and this veteran got back on track with third of seven in 5.5f Bath handicap 25 days ago; a likely player eased 1lb returned here.
runners.comment: A three-time C&D scorer; back on track with Bath third latest and likely player eased 1lb
runners.owner: Mrs S M Maine & Roger Gould & Partner

runners.name: So Smart
runners.spotlight: C&D winner who arrives in decent nick, leading long way when third of five over C&D eight days ago; must enter calculations off the same mark.
runners.comment: C&D winner who arrives in decent nick; must enter calculations

runners.name: Macarone
runners.spotlight: A fair 6f scorer for Ivan Furtado in 2025; changed hands for 2,000gns before coming in just tenth for new yard in 6f handicap here 16 days ago; needs to take a big step forward.
runners.comment: A fair 6f scorer for Ivan Furtado; tenth for new yard here 16 days ago; needs lots more

runners.name: Hidden Verse
runners.spotlight: Scored twice last summer (including over C&D) and comes here on the back of a solid fifth in 5.5f Bath handicap 25 days ago; not out of things from an easing mark.
runners.comment: C&D winner; solid fifth at Bath 25 days ago; not out of things from an easing mark

runners.name: Call Time
runners.spotlight: Doubled his career tally over C&D in September; off five months and shaped as if in need of the run when a fading tenth of 13 in 5f Bath handicap on his return in April; can take a step forward.
runners.comment: C&D winner; fading tenth at Bath on his return in April; can take a step forward
runners.owner: The 19th Hole Syndicate & J S Moore

runners.name: Fletcher's Flight
runners.spotlight: On a long losing run and he went too freely in first-time cheekpieces for his new yard when a below-par fourth of eight in 5f Lingfield handicap 18 days ago; tongue tied now with more required.
runners.comment: On a long losing run and below-par Lingfield fourth latest; tongue tied with more needed

runners.name: Pearl's Edge
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 6yo who ended 2025 well below par; back from seven months off with plenty more required.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 6yo who ended 2025 well below par; back from seven months off here

races.race_name: LSL Racing Horse Sales Syndication Leasing/EBF Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive)

runners.name: Resemblance
runners.spotlight: Bayside Boy filly who took a big step forward from her debut when a clear second of nine in 6f Haydock maiden 15 days ago; she holds very good form claims.
runners.comment: Big step forward when clear second at Haydock 15 days ago; holds very good form claims

runners.name: Miss Moneypit
runners.spotlight: Without Parole filly; built on debut promise with keeping-on fourth of 12 in 5f Ascot novice last month; she looks to have more to offer over 6f so merits serious consideration.
runners.comment: Built on debut with Ascot fourth; more to offer at 6f and merits serious consideration

runners.name: Clueisinthetitle
runners.spotlight: 22,000gns yearling; half-sister to 7f/1m winner Lambournghini (RPR 54); dam Listed-placed 5f 2yo winner (RPR 93), sister to Listed-placed 6f 2yo winner Pastoral Girl; yard's juveniles are going well so this Ubettabelieveit filly is very much one to consider.
runners.comment: Yard's juveniles are going well so this Ubettabelieveit filly is very much one to consider

runners.name: My Maria
runners.spotlight: 9-2, shaped with encouragement when a debut fourth of nine in novice at Salisbury (5f, good) two weeks ago, keeping on late having been hampered over 2f out; this daughter of Persian Force seems sure to take a step forward.
runners.comment: Encouraging debut fourth at Salisbury; seems sure to take step forward

runners.name: Angels Lane
runners.spotlight: 27,000gns yearling; Harry Angel filly; half-sister to 5f/5.5f winner Supersonic Man (Australia; RPR 88); dam unraced sister to 7.3f winner One Kiss, half-sister to 6f Group 3 winner Jimmy Styles; the market can guide for this debutante.
runners.comment: Harry Angel filly; the market can guide for this debutante

runners.name: Chilli
runners.spotlight: Harry Angel filly; dam 8.6f 2yo winner (RPR 77), sister to 1m5f winner Isaiah; the betting can prove an accurate indicator.
runners.comment: Harry Angel filly; the betting can prove an accurate indicator

runners.name: Big Hitter
runners.spotlight: 65,000euros yearling; dam 6f 2yo winner (Group 2; RPR 100), half-sister to winners Oliver Hardy (1m2f/1m4f) and Dajla (Spanish 1m-1m3f), out of a maiden sister to 6f/7f 2yo Group 2 winner Strategic Prince; this Starspangledbanner filly needs considering, especially if the market vibes are positive.
runners.comment: This Starspangledbanner filly needs considering, especially if market vibes are positive

runners.name: Secret Rival
runners.spotlight: 60,000gns yearling; dam 5f 2yo winner (RPR 85), half-sister to 5f winners Orlaith (Listed) and Tammy Wynette, out of an unraced half-sister to 7f 2yo Group 1 winner Toormore; this Ardad filly appeals on paper and must enter calculations for good yard.
runners.comment: This Ardad filly appeals on paper and must enter calculations for good yard

runners.name: Penny Capri
runners.spotlight: 11-1 and hooded, hinted at promise with sixth of ten in maiden at Chester (5.5f, good) on her first run 30 days ago, leading until 1f out; this Minzaal filly can build on it here.
runners.comment: Hinted at promise with debut sixth at Chester; this Minzaal filly can build on it here

races.race_name: Capital Windscreens Supporting Breast Cancer Handicap

runners.name: Poetry Of Time
runners.spotlight: In the frame all four starts as a 2yo; gelded and comfortably off the mark in 7f Salisbury novice on his return last month; this son of Ulysses has more to offer now handicapping; major player.
runners.comment: Comfortably off mark at Salisbury on return; more to offer on h'cap debut; major player

runners.name: Mighty Vega
runners.spotlight: Enjoyed a very good winter on the AW and he gained a first turf win in 7f Lingfield handicap 11 days ago; up 3lb but this consistent sort can go well again.
runners.comment: First turf win at Lingfield 11 days ago; up 3lb but consistent and can go well again

runners.name: Lyra Lea
runners.spotlight: A fair 7f winner as 2yo; resumed from seven months off with a good fourth of seven in 8.5f handicap at Wolverhampton 19 days ago; she's very much one to consider eased 1lb.
runners.comment: Good fourth at Wolverhampton on recent return; very much one to consider eased 1lb

runners.name: Ebn Sabt
runners.spotlight: Gained a first win at Lingfield (1m) in February but he ended his time with William Muir and Chris Grassick with a below-par seventh in 1m Southwell handicap in April; since changed hands for 7,000gns and needs to bounce back for new trainer.
runners.comment: Below par at Southwell final run for William Muir and Chris Grassick; more is needed here

runners.name: Island Bear
runners.spotlight: Fair maiden who wasn't discredited with fifth of nine in 1m Nottingham handicap 18 days ago; not out of things eased 1lb with blinkers added.
runners.comment: Not discredited with fifth at Nottingham 18 days ago; not out of things with blinkers on

runners.name: Electrical
runners.spotlight: Made a winning debut in 6f Newbury novice last July; has failed to kick on in three runs since though, gelded before beating only one on his handicap debut/return at Haydock (1m) in April; has something to prove.
runners.comment: Beat one on his handicap debut/return at Haydock (1m) in April; has something to prove

runners.name: Aigeas
runners.spotlight: Debut scorer at Deauville (6.5f) in October but well below that form both subsequent runs, 11th of 13 in 6f Leicester novice on his return in April; since gelded and needs to take a big step forward on his handicap debut with hood/tongue-tie also added.
runners.comment: Debut scorer at Deauville but well below that form both subsequent runs; since gelded

races.race_name: Late Arron James Supporting Pancreatic Cancer Handicap

runners.name: Bela Sonata
runners.spotlight: A dual 7f winner in first half of 2025; hasn't returned in much form though, beating only one in 7f AW Lingfield handicap 25 days ago; hard to make a case for.
runners.comment: A dual 7f winner in first half of 2025; hasn't returned in much form though

runners.name: Kisskodi
runners.spotlight: Progressed well in 2025 when a four-time winner at 6f/7f; has gone with promise on both starts this spring, suffering a poor run before staying on into fifth of 14 at Goodwood (7f) 15 days ago; can race off a 1lb lower mark here so a bold showing is on the cards.
runners.comment: Four-time winner in 2025; shaped well when fifth at Goodwood latest; bold showing on cards

runners.name: Dapper Gee Gee
runners.spotlight: Gained a second C&D victory when getting up late in 11-runner handicap last month; up 2lb but that form is working out well so he must enter calculations.
runners.comment: Got up late over C&D last month; up 2lb but form franked so he must enter calculations

runners.name: Receipt
runners.spotlight: Made a winning start in handicaps at Southwell (7f) in April but he raced very keenly when beating just two in 7f Kempton handicap the following month; still low mileage so shouldn't be written off on his Flat turf debut.
runners.comment: Very keen when beating two at Kempton latest; not written off on his Flat turf debut

runners.name: Racingbreaks Ryder
runners.spotlight: Went winless in 2025 but he posted some good placed efforts, signing off with a solid third in 1m Leicester handicap in October; not out of it if fully tuned up for his seasonal return so worth a market check.
runners.comment: Winless in 2025 but some good placed efforts; not out of it if fully tuned up for return
runners.owner: Cottier, Racing Breaks & Old Gold Racing

runners.name: Slipper Time
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark in 7f Salisbury novice in August but failed to beat a rival at Kempton on his final run in 2025 and went freely when beating one on his return in 1m Newmarket handicap last month; needs to become more tractable.
runners.comment: Failed to beat rival final run in 2025 and fared little better on his return at Newmarket

runners.name: This Farh
runners.spotlight: In fine form since returned to turf this spring, winning at Leicester (7f, good) in April and at Beverley (7.4f, good to firm) ten days ago; up another 3lb but this gutsy front-runner is a likely player once more.
runners.comment: Record of 2-3 since returned to turf and this gutsy front-runner is a likely player

runners.name: Brave Byreflection
runners.spotlight: C&D scorer for Alice Haynes last July; yet to fire for her new yard though in 1m handicaps at Kempton and Southwell in April; lots more is required.
runners.comment: C&D scorer for Alice Haynes last July; yet to fire for her new yard though so more needed

runners.name: Connie's Rose
runners.spotlight: Course specialist who just missed out on an eighth course victory when beaten a neck over C&D eight days ago; very much one to consider despite a 2lb rise.
runners.comment: Course specialist; beaten neck over C&D eight days ago so she's very much one to consider

runners.name: Bold Impact
runners.spotlight: A fair 7f scorer for Ralph Beckett in 2024; yet to match that form for his current yard, though not disgraced when seventh in 1m Windsor handicap 12 days ago; possibilities from a falling mark.
runners.comment: Not disgraced when seventh in Windsor h'cap 12 days ago; possibilities from a falling mark

races.race_name: V3 UK- Principal Contractor Handicap (Chepstow Mile Series Qualifier) (Div I)

runners.name: Judge Frank
runners.spotlight: Both wins came in September 2024; tends to race prominently and if his natural exuberance can be harnessed then he would not be far away.
runners.comment: Both wins came in September 2024; tends to race prominently.

runners.name: Fifty Sent
runners.spotlight: Returned to form when winning two classified events at Southwell (1m) in March but he's been well held back in handicaps in his last two runs; didn't run badly at Nottingham (8.3f, good) in April but that took his turf record to 1-18.
runners.comment: Won two classified events at Southwell (1m) in March but held back in handicaps since.

runners.name: Mooretown Lad
runners.spotlight: Sole success came in August 2024 when trained in Ireland; has tumbled down the weights since his British debut in December; is a hostage to fortune as he tends to be held up and requires a fast pace to be seen to best effect.
runners.comment: Sole success came in August 2024 when trained in Ireland; has not been so good since.

runners.name: Fitzmaurice
runners.spotlight: Down to a basement mark but this 12-race maiden has been struggling to get competitive this year and others make greater appeal.
runners.comment: Down to a basement mark but this 12-race maiden has been struggling to get competitive.

runners.name: A Rose Adaay
runners.spotlight: Best effort when third here in a maiden (6f, soft) last September; well held in three handicaps since; steps up in trip and needs to show improvement.
runners.comment: Best effort when third here in a maiden in September; well held in three handicaps since.

runners.name: Eye Of The Water
runners.spotlight: Some fair efforts on the AW during the winter and he was placed on multiple occasions last year; ran okay at Bath (1m, good to soft) in April but he's without a win since August 2023.
runners.comment: Ran okay at Bath in April but he's without a win since August 2023.

runners.name: Scarfo
runners.spotlight: Long time since his sole win and hasn't offered much this year; hard to make a case for.
runners.comment: Long time since his sole win and hasn't offered much this year; hard to make a case for.

runners.name: Saturnalia
runners.spotlight: Both wins came on AW in 2023 when trained by Richard Hughes; has gone into freefall since and was last of 11 at Bath last month.
runners.comment: Both wins came on AW in 2023 when trained by Richard Hughes; has gone into freefall since.
runners.owner: Ms H N Pinniger And Partner

runners.name: Liberty Bird
runners.spotlight: Both wins came in 2024 when trained by Charlie Johnston; has lost her form and now with her third trainer since; pulled up at Windsor last time (bled from the nose).
runners.comment: Both wins came in 2024 when trained by Charlie Johnston; has lost her form since.

races.race_name: V3 UK- Principal Contractor Handicap (Chepstow Mile Series Qualifier) (Div II)

runners.name: Uncle Albert
runners.spotlight: Sole success came in March 2024 when trained by Kieran Cotter in Ireland; has lost his way this year and makes his British/stable debut; probably best watched for now.
runners.comment: Sole success came in March 2024 when trained in Ireland; makes his British/stable debut.

runners.name: King Of The Dance
runners.spotlight: Generally consistent but doesn't win too often; hasn't been far away of late and regularly makes the frame; has place claims.
runners.comment: Generally consistent but doesn't win too often; hasn't been far away of late.

runners.name: Whiskey Sunrise
runners.spotlight: Eight-race maiden but has shown he can find a race; best effort was when beaten a nose at Kempton (1m, AW) in January and if can replicate that form on turf then he comes into the equation; has left Pat Phelan since his last run in March; sports a first-time tongue-tie and has to be of interest for new yard.
runners.comment: Best effort was when beaten a nose at Kempton in January; stable debut

runners.name: Prince Ali
runners.spotlight: All six wins have been on AW; cheekpieces return but he doesn't convince.
runners.comment: All six wins have been on AW; cheekpieces return but he doesn't convince.

runners.name: Nutcracker
runners.spotlight: 11-race maiden but has gone close in classified events at Wolverhampton (8.5, AW) on his last two starts; needs to convert that form to handicaps on turf.
runners.comment: 11-race maiden but has gone close in classifieds at Wolverhampton on his last two starts.

runners.name: Charencey
runners.spotlight: His losing run goes back to July 2024 and his mark has plummeted since but he could build on April's fifth at Nottingham; not discounted.
runners.comment: His losing run goes back to July 2024 and his mark has plummeted since.

runners.name: Marisitta
runners.spotlight: Still a maiden and lacks consistency to boot; returning cheekpieces replace first-time hood worn last time; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Still a maiden and lacks consistency to boot.

runners.name: Homeland
runners.spotlight: Scored for Michael Dods at Ripon (1m2f, good) last July off 5lb higher; sold to join current yard for 4,000gns in October; fifth of 12 on stable debut at Wolverhampton (9.5f, AW) in December; unraced since but on a decent mark.
runners.comment: Fifth on stable debut at Wolverhampton in December; unraced since but on a decent mark.

runners.name: Rival
runners.spotlight: Last of two wins came in September 2022; seemed to find his level again last year when making the frame four times; has been absent since last August though and needs to hit the ground running.
runners.comment: Last of two wins came in September 2022; seemed to find his level again last year.

races.race_name: Capital Windscreens Supporting Autistic Society Handicap

runners.name: On Key
runners.spotlight: 0-20 but he arrives in good nick, second of 13 in 6f Brighton handicap eight days ago; well in the mix again off an unchanged mark.
runners.comment: 0-20 but he arrives in good nick; in the mix again off an unchanged mark

runners.name: Too Much Trevor
runners.spotlight: Yet to score in 30 attempts but she comes here in decent nick, third of ten in 7f handicap here eight days ago; back up 4lb now, though.
runners.comment: Yet to score in 30 attempts but she comes here in decent nick; back up 4lb now, though

runners.name: Autumn Angel
runners.spotlight: Had usual headgear left off when ending a long losing sequence over C&D 16 days ago; up 3lb but must enter calculations.
runners.comment: Headgear left off when ending losing run over C&D latest; up 3lb but enters calculations

runners.name: Be An Angel
runners.spotlight: Scored at Leicester (6f) last May but she didn't prove the easiest to catch right later in 2025; has gone well fresh though, so not out of things on her seasonal return.
runners.comment: Scored at Leicester (6f) last May but she didn't prove the easiest to catch right after

runners.name: Diamondsinthesand
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner (around 6f) in 2025 but he's yet to hit top form in a trio of runs this season, seventh of 13 at Nottingham (5f) 18 days ago.
runners.comment: A three-time winner in 2025 but yet to hit top form in a trio of runs this season

runners.name: Punchbowl Flyer
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D scorer but his good form has dipped of late, beating only one at Wolverhampton (7f) last time; needs to get back on track.
runners.comment: Dual C&D scorer but his good form has dipped of late; needs to get back on track

runners.name: Sub Thirteen
runners.spotlight: A fair 6f scorer for Tony Carroll but largely below par this season for his present handler, 13th of 14 in 6f Windsor handicap two weeks ago; others are preferred.
runners.comment: Fair 6f scorer for Tony Carroll but largely below par this season for his present handler

runners.name: Margorie
runners.spotlight: Gained a first win at Windsor (6f) in September but she has failed to build on it since handicapping, ninth of 11 over 7f here 16 days ago; mark is falling but she still needs to take a big step forward.
runners.comment: Gained first win at Windsor in September but has failed to build on it since handicapping

runners.name: Kaleidoscope Eyes
runners.spotlight: Opened his account for current yard at Ffos Las (7f) in September but off since beating one in 7f Wolverhampton handicap following month; needs to hit the ground running on his seasonal return.
runners.comment: Off since beating one at Wolverhampton in October; needs to hit the ground running

runners.name: The Bear Of Saints
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced maiden; not disgraced with fifth in 5.6f Bath maiden on his final run for Joe Tickle in September 2024; since gelded and has his fitness to prove on his comeback run for a new yard.
runners.comment: Lightly raced maiden; off since September 2024 so has his fitness to prove for a new yard

runners.name: Snuggle
runners.spotlight: A fair 6f scorer for Luke Dace in 2024; off ten months but shaped well for his new stable with fourth of 12 in C&D handicap 16 days ago; not without interest off a reduced mark.
runners.comment: Shaped well for new stable after ten months off with C&D fourth; not without interest

races.race_name: Capital Windscreens Supporting Mental Health Handicap

runners.name: Cloudside Rock
runners.spotlight: Took ten goes to get off the mark but it was a convincing win at Ffos Las (1m4f, soft) in August and he doubled his tally when bagging Southwell handicap (1m3f, AW) on his April return; back up 4lb but he might be capable of better and holds leading claims.
runners.comment: Convincing win (1m4f, soft) in August and also won on return; holds leading claims

runners.name: Electric Bass
runners.spotlight: 0-13 and his six runner-up placings may suggest something's afoot; did dead-heat at Kempton (1m4f, AW; demoted to second) in November and he comes here in form having chased one home again at Southwell in April and Bath last time; handles good to soft; can make his presence felt.
runners.comment: 0-13 and has finished runner-up six times; in form and handles some ease in the ground

runners.name: Big Win
runners.spotlight: Unraced at two; just five starts for Harry Charlton and following 18,000gns sale it was a good start for these connections when second at Bath (1m6f, good to firm) a fortnight ago; likely softer ground is an unknown but he attracted support ahead of that stable debut and should cope with this drop back in trip; player.
runners.comment: Good start for yard when second (1m6f, good to firm) latest; player despite ground query

runners.name: Rosco Rogers
runners.spotlight: AW campaigner who is having his first realistic go on turf; went close at Wolverhampton (9.5f) and Kempton (1m4f) in his first two handicaps but didn't progress across this winter's campaign; last two starts saw big defeats in the spring but maybe wind surgery will help and a tongue-tie is now added to his cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Last two runs were not good but maybe wind surgery/tongue-tie will help; rare turf start

runners.name: Marinakis
runners.spotlight: Generally consistent in his time with Ian Williams when he scored three times on AW from 1m to 1m2f; second start for this yard wasn't devoid of promise at Kempton (1m4f) 17 days ago but suitability for the combination of this trip and likely ground is yet to be determined.
runners.comment: Second go for yard wasn't devoid of promise; suitability for trip/likely ground not proven

runners.name: Wonderful Wendy
runners.spotlight: Withdrawn from her intended debut last July on account of soft ground; modest form in three qualifying runs from 1m2f to 1m4f (AW/good) but starts her handicapping career at a modest level and no surprise if she can do better in this sphere; heed any market signals.
runners.comment: Modest form in three qualifying runs; no surprise if she can do better in this sphere

runners.name: Port Of Entry
runners.spotlight: Very lightly raced ex-French 6yo who didn't set the world alight for Andre Fabre in 2023, and after a fairly encouraging comeback for this yard at Kempton in April he was tailed off back there (also 2m, AW) one month later; tongue-tie added.
runners.comment: Fairly encouraging comeback for new yard but tailed off next time; tongue-tie added

runners.name: Baileys Warrior
runners.spotlight: Dual winning mare (2m, AW) but she was running poorly when last seen in the autumn; nothing wrong with this mark if the break has recharged her batteries but this is a rare turf outing and probably doesn't want too much rain.
runners.comment: Running poorly when last seen; mark fine if break has helped but easy turf is a query

runners.name: Bug Boy
runners.spotlight: Good record here down the years, with form figures of 21319 highlighting two wins at 1m4f (good); also scored twice over this trip in 2023 (including on good to soft) but no wins since and he only saw the track once last year; still worth a market check ahead of his return.
runners.comment: Course form figures of 21319 highlight two 1m4f wins; check market ahead of his return

races.race_name: Marjorie Thompsett Auntie Marj Memorial Amateur Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Malangen
runners.spotlight: Won over 2m here (good; from 13lb higher) in June 2024; seems to be gradually working back to form, a staying-on third to Fine Point here (2m, good; 100-30 favourite) latest; hopes of reversal over 2m4f on 7lb better terms.
runners.comment: Staying-on third to Fine Point here (2m, good; 100-30 fav) latest; return to 2m4f helpful.
runners.owner: T Hunter & D A Harrison Racing

runners.name: Byron Hill
runners.spotlight: Sole hurdles win at Carlisle (2m1f, soft; from 8lb higher) in October 2024; consistent this year, without necessarily looking ready to win again; third to better-fancied stablemate Cosmic Soul at Sedgefield (2m4f, good) nine weeks ago.
runners.comment: Consistent this year, without looking ready to win again; third to a stablemate latest.

runners.name: My My My Delilah
runners.spotlight: 12-race maiden; pulled up over 2m here (soft) on penultimate start, and weakened quickly at Kelso (2m, good; sixth, beaten 21l) subsequently; continues to ease in the weights, now 17lb lower than back in January.
runners.comment: 12-race maiden; weakened quickly at Kelso (2m, good; sixth, btn 21l) latest; easing mark.

runners.name: Rickety Gate
runners.spotlight: Back below last winning mark (Carlisle, 2m4f) but pulled up at Ayr on penultimate start, and weakened into seventh at Cartmel (2m6f; beaten over 13l) 12 days ago; others more convincing at this stage.
runners.comment: Weakened into seventh at Cartmel (2m6f; beaten 13l+) 12 days ago; others more convincing.

runners.name: Ushuaia Dancer
runners.spotlight: Only win came courtesy of the stewards' at Newcastle (2m6f, heavy) in January 2024; was returning from a lengthy absence when runner-up to a Skelton-runner at Newcastle (2m4f; 18-1) in March; realistic prospect of easing conditions would aid his cause.
runners.comment: Returning from a lengthy absence when runner-up at Newcastle (2m4f; 18-1) in March.

runners.name: Cuillin Ridge
runners.spotlight: Ridden out to score at Huntingdon (3m1f, novices' handicap; good to soft; from 6lb lower) in March; has since left Henry Daly for £12,500; headgear removed, and interesting to see what the market makes of his chance off a three-month break; merits respect.
runners.comment: Ridden out to score at Huntingdon (3m1f; from 6lb lower) in March; since sold for £12,500.

runners.name: C'Mon So
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in a point; five-race rules maiden; beaten 50l, 54l, and 36l, in three hurdle starts (2m-2m4f maiden/novices'); significant improvement is needed to make an impression on this handicap debut.
runners.comment: Five-race rules maiden; beaten 50l, 54l, and 36l, in three hurdle starts; handicap debut.

runners.name: Road To Rosley
runners.spotlight: Yet to win (0-12) and beaten almost 50l in a Cartmel maiden (2m6f; 100-1) a fortnight ago; no reason to expect better for this return to handicapping; the first one to dismiss.
runners.comment: Yet to win (0-12) and beaten almost 50l in a Cartmel maiden (2m6f; 100-1) a fortnight ago.

runners.name: Fine Point
runners.spotlight: Steady progress since joining this trainer, resuming winning ways over 2m here (good; 7-2, with Malangen third) latest; raised 7lb since, and tackles 2m4f for the first time; noteworthy that Patrick Mullins travels over from Ireland to ride.
runners.comment: Resumed winning ways over 2m here (good) latest; 7lb higher; Patrick Mullins is booked.

runners.name: Follow The Leader
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in three maiden hurdles (massive prices) in late 2024/early 2025; productive in points (form 212) this spring, chasing home Getupearly at Witton Castle (3m) last month; tough to know what to expect but isn't totally dismissed.
runners.comment: Productive in points (form 212) this spring, runner-up at Witton Castle (3m) last month.

races.race_name: Most Important 3-Year-Old Race Today Juvenile Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Tiny Riot
runners.spotlight: Made progress - at his own rate - on the Flat, seeming to handle the extra yardage when fifth of nine on handicap debut at Nottingham (1m2f, good; 14-1) in April; bit to find with Sudbury Hill on Flat figures, but it would be foolhardy to dismiss a Skelton-trained jumps newcomer.
runners.comment: Fifth of nine on Flat handicap debut at Nottingham (1m2f, good; 14-1) in April; possible.

runners.name: Mount Eden
runners.spotlight: Didn't offer much on the Flat for Donnacha O'Brien (0-4) prior to changing hands; his half-brothers Waver and Gold Seal were both successful over hurdles (2m); a first ever Hexham runner for the County Wexford handler Shay Slevin.
runners.comment: Modest on the Flat for D. O'Brien (0-4); half-brother to two hurdle winners; Irish raider.

runners.name: Cracking Anna
runners.spotlight: Flat form for Michael Bell isn't overly encouraging, beaten in excess of 8l in a 1m2f Class 6 at Yarmouth (good; 12-1) six weeks ago; dam won over hurdles though (2m5f) and she's in pretty safe hands with Greenall and Guerriero; receives the 7lb fillies' allowance.
runners.comment: Flat form for Michael Bell isn't overly encouraging; dam won over hurdles though (2m5f)

runners.name: Steel Fixer
runners.spotlight: Flat maiden (0-6) but marginally higher-rated than stablemate Jambearee, and the fact that Nathan Moscrop takes the ride suggests he's their principal hope; raced too freely and weakened on AW at Newcastle (1m2f) on Tuesday; wears first-time cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Flat maiden (0-6); raced freely and weakened on AW at Newcastle (1m2f) on Tuesday evening.

runners.name: Sudbury Hill
runners.spotlight: Productive on the Flat (best RPR 82), with plenty to admire about the manner in which he applied himself going down to King Of Berkshire at Redcar (1m2f, good to firm) on penultimate start; trainer/jockey supplied the wide-margin winner of this (One Horse Town) last year; looks the one to beat.
runners.comment: Fairly useful on Flat; trainer/jockey supplied the wide-margin winner of this last year.

runners.name: Jambearee
runners.spotlight: Lowly rated on the Flat (0-6); never better than midfield in added cheekpieces at Thirsk (1m4f, Class 5; beaten over 10l, 40-1) three weeks ago; trainers sole representative in this in 2024 was an early casualty; stablemate of Steel Fixer.
runners.comment: Lowly rated on Flat (0-6); trainers representative in this in 2024 was an early casualty.

races.race_name: Marrill Group Stamping Futures Handicap Chase

runners.name: Conquer The Breeze
runners.spotlight: Made it 2-2 for Adam Nicol, winning easily over 2m here (good; Dream Jet 35l away in third) 18 days ago; the handicapper has taken another hit - 13lb higher - but it mightn't be enough to thwart the hat-trick bid; the only slight reservation being this 2m4f trip.
runners.comment: 2-2 for A. Nicol, winning easily over 2m here latest; 13lb higher in pursuit of hat-trick.

runners.name: Cosmic Blizzard
runners.spotlight: 0-18 under rules; just failed at Kelso (3m, good; beaten a neck) last October, but has had issues since, twice pulling up at Newcastle (bled from the nose on both occasions), and beaten 83l at Perth (3m) over three weeks ago; best watched.
runners.comment: Just failed at Kelso (3m, good; beaten neck) last October; had issues since; best watched.
runners.owner: County Set Four & L V Russell

runners.name: Out On Her Own
runners.spotlight: Maiden under rules (0-10), but she posted a series of consistent efforts over hurdles; tried to make all in an added hood over C&D (chase debut) latest, succumbing to the stronger finish of Passengerontheship; is 5lb better off with 7l too find; the task isn't impossible.
runners.comment: Succumbed to the stronger finish of Passengerontheship over C&D latest; now 5lb better off

runners.name: Dream Jet
runners.spotlight: Dual course winner (2m), who sits 5lb below his last winning mark; finished a remote third to Conquer The Breeze here latest, so it's difficult to work out how he goes about making up such a deficit, even acknowledging more favourable terms.
runners.comment: Dual course winner (2m); remote third to Conquer The Breeze here latest; focus elsewhere.

runners.name: Passengerontheship
runners.spotlight: Saw it out well to get off the mark over fences (C&D), beating Out On Her Own by 7l 18 days ago; a 5lb rise looks fair - competitive off similar marks over hurdles - so he'll be a live contender.
runners.comment: C&D winner, beating Out On Her Own by 7l, 18 days ago; player from a 5lb higher mark.

runners.name: The Clonmel Kid
runners.spotlight: Won on second chase start at Newcastle (2m4f, good; from 4lb lower) in April; still bang there when a bad mistake at the last proved costly here (3m, good; 9-2) latest; risks are attached in this slightly more competitive contest.
runners.comment: Bang there when bad mistake at the last proved costly here (3m, good; 9-2) latest; risks.

runners.name: Halpha Soleil
runners.spotlight: Yet to win over fences (0-13); third at Doncaster (3m, soft; on first start since wind surgery) in February, but well beaten at Sedgefield a fortnight later; absent since (107 days) and now 5lb lower in the handicap; others far safer.
runners.comment: 0-13 over fences; well beaten at Sedgefield (2m5f) in February; absent since; easing mark.

runners.name: Similar Story
runners.spotlight: No win since October 2024; now rated 12lb lower, but her form figures this year (5UP036) offer little encouragement, with even her Kelso third (3m, good; 22-1) a laboured one; blinkers replace cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Form figures this year (5UP036) offer little encouragement; blinkers are added.

runners.name: Manhattan Valley
runners.spotlight: Point winner (May 2024); little impact over hurdles (0-4) and it was a similar story on chase debut at Ffos Las (2m4f, good to soft; 16-1) in April; now 3lb lower, with Jack Tudor taking over in the saddle.
runners.comment: No impact on rules chase debut at Ffos Las (2m4f, good to soft; 16-1) in April; 3lb lower.

races.race_name: Hexham Racecourse Holiday Home & Caravan Park Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Sir Carnegie
runners.spotlight: Dug deep to resume winning ways at Perth (2m4f, good to soft; 15-8 favourite) over three weeks ago; that was his second run there in the space of 24 hours (third over 3m previous day); 4lb higher with blinkers applied, but Shane Fenelon's 5lb claim lessens that burden; key player.
runners.comment: Dug deep at Perth (2m4f; from 4lb lower) latest; blinkers newly applied & claimer ridden.

runners.name: Always Busy
runners.spotlight: 1lb above last winning mark (3m, Southwell); knocking on the door at Taunton (3m4f; third) on penultimate start, and might have had a role to play in the Norfolk National at Fakenham (3m5f) latest but for falling three out.
runners.comment: Role to play in Norfolk National at Fakenham (3m5f) latest but for falling at third-last.

runners.name: Giovanni Change
runners.spotlight: Admirable Market Rasen specialist (six wins there); won over 3m at Wetherby (good; from 2lb lower) in March, but hasn't been at his best since; uncharacteristically laboured (never travelling) when remote third latest; bit to prove.
runners.comment: Uncharacteristically laboured when remote third at Market Rasen (3m) latest; bit to prove.
runners.owner: Readers & Wiggy, The 8 Amigos & J Burns

runners.name: Lakefield Flyer
runners.spotlight: Both wins have come at Downpatrick (2m3f); plugged on to finish a modest second at Plumpton (2m1f, good; beaten 21l) 11 days ago; that should have given him confidence though - fell at Killarney prior - and this extra yardage could suit.
runners.comment: Plugged on to finish a modest second at Plumpton (2m1f, good; beaten 21l) 11 days ago.

runners.name: West Lawn
runners.spotlight: Took advantage of a final-fence error from The Clonmel Kid to get off the mark over fences at the 20th attempt over C&D (good; 10-1) 18 days ago; effectively 6lb higher from out of the weights today and her task is tougher; others preferred.
runners.comment: Off the mark over fences at 20th attempt over C&D (good; 10-1) 18 days ago; 10lb wrong

runners.name: Lights Are Green
runners.spotlight: Won off this mark (beat No Regrets by a neck) over C&D in May 2023; struggled last year, often pulling up, but gave an indication he's back in better shape when third to the in-form Halfway House Lad at Cartmel (3m1f, good; 20-1) a fortnight ago; worthy of inspection.
runners.comment: Third to in-form Halfway House Lad at Cartmel (3m1f) a fortnight ago; worthy of inspection

runners.name: Foxinthebox
runners.spotlight: Won back-to-back 3m points at Overton and Pusk, prior to finishing a creditable third in a hunter chase at Perth (3m; beaten 4l, 12-1) 24 days ago; with the suspension of hunter chases, he now switches back to a handicap for Susan Corbett.
runners.comment: Creditable third in a hunter chase at Perth (3m; 12-1) 24 days ago; now with Susan Corbett

runners.name: No Regrets
runners.spotlight: Stayed on to win this off topweight back in 2023, and gamely back in the winners' enclosure over C&D (from 5lb lower) latest; his jumping was immaculate that day, with his trainer stating that he had drawn benefit from being able to dominate; this rates as a tougher test.
runners.comment: 2023 winner; gamely back in the winners' enclosure over C&D (from 5lb lower) latest.

runners.name: Tell Me Again
runners.spotlight: Placed in a 3m point at Loughanmore for Tom Dreaper last October; badly hampered and unseated rider on stable debut, but left toiling behind Passengerontheship (beaten 78l) over 2m4f here 18 days ago; tough to consider on that evidence.
runners.comment: Toiled behind Passengerontheship (btn 78l) over 2m4f here 18 days ago; tough to consider.

races.race_name: ogledigital.co.uk Google Ads Tailed Off Too? Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Glory And Honour
runners.spotlight: Won over fences here (2m, good; in first-time blinkers - two finished) last June; runner-up at Newcastle (2m4f) in April, but low key at Kelso subsequently; returns to hurdles for the first time since finishing tailed off over C&D last October; others more appealing.
runners.comment: Low key over fences at Kelso recently, and also has a fair bit to prove back over hurdles.

runners.name: Two Auld Pals
runners.spotlight: Handled testing conditions best, bounding clear to make it 2-3 over C&D (soft; a 23l winner) four weeks ago; the handicapper has reacted, and he's now 9lb higher moving up into a Class 4; still enters calculations though, with 10lb claimer Gregor Walkingshaw retaining the ride.
runners.comment: Bounded clear over C&D (a 23l winner) four weeks ago; 9lb higher, and up in grade; player.

runners.name: Well Educated
runners.spotlight: Four Hexham wins (2m-2m4f) and is rated 15lb lower than when last successful at Kelso (2m, Class 2) in May 2024; returned from a 204-day absence with a decent effort, runner-up to Banderas, over 2m4f here latest; respected, even if some of the others have more potential.
runners.comment: Returned from a 204-day absence with a decent effort, runner-up, over 2m4f here latest.

runners.name: Freddy Robinson
runners.spotlight: Dug in to score on his last spin over hurdles at Market Rasen (2m, good to soft; from 3lb lower) in December; runner-up three times in succession on the Flat at Beverley recently, beaten 2l there (1m4f; 13-8 favourite) just ten days ago.
runners.comment: Won on last spin over hurdles; runner-up on the Flat at Beverley (1m4f) just ten days ago.

runners.name: Stay In The Game
runners.spotlight: Made the breakthrough over hurdles at the seventh attempt, getting the better of the long-odds-on favourite in a match at Huntingdon (2m, good to firm; 6-1) 12 days ago; what that form amounts to is questionable, and this Class 4 contest is much more competitive.
runners.comment: Downed long-odds-on favourite in a match at Huntingdon (2m) 12 days ago; this is tougher.

runners.name: Pyramid Place
runners.spotlight: 9-39 hurdles, but no win since quickening clear in a four-runner Class 2 at Kempton (2m5f, good; from 19lb higher) in November 2024; pulled up over fences (bled from nose) on final start for Harriet Dickin nearly four months ago; best watched on stable debut (£6,000 purchase).
runners.comment: Pulled up over fences (bled from nose) on final start for Harriet Dickin; best watched.

races.race_name: Tricia Hughes Memorial Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Robert d'Ores
runners.spotlight: Three C&D wins, the most recent when keeping on well to repel Lelantos (from 2lb higher) last October; struggling for form in 2026 and has undergone further wind surgery since his last appearance five weeks ago; isn't the likeliest.
runners.comment: Three C&D wins; struggling for form in 2026 and has undergone further wind surgery.

runners.name: Ebselysees
runners.spotlight: Back in the groove since joining the doubly represented Susan Corbett, winning back-to-back at Perth (3m/3m2f); raised a further 7lb since latest win, but that is effectively offset by the returning Rebekah Duffy; worthy of serious consideration in hat-trick mission.
runners.comment: Won back-to-back at Perth (3m/3m2f); raised further 7lb since latest win; contender.

runners.name: Abbey Scope
runners.spotlight: Added to debut bumper win, hitting the line strongly to score at the third attempt in a handicap hurdle at Kelso (2m5f, good) 31 days ago; moves up in trip (ideal on pedigree) from a 6lb higher mark, with that form franked at Southwell by the third-placed Larkfield Lusive.
runners.comment: Hit the line strongly at Kelso (2m5f; form franked since); up in trip from 6lb higher.

runners.name: Sunset Hill
runners.spotlight: Runner-up to Thursday's Hamilton scorer Perseus Way over 2m4f here (soft) on penultimate start, and bettered that on RPR's when third to Banderas subsequently; reliable but this step back up to 2m7f mightn't work in her favour; stable won this last year with Pinot Rouge.
runners.comment: Third to Banderas over 2m4f here (same mark) latest; step back up in trip is the risk.

runners.name: Jeteye
runners.spotlight: Rediscovered a spark, scrambling home over C&D (good; 12-1) 18 days ago; a 4lb rise takes him back to a triple-figure mark (traditionally struggles from that position) but he isn't one to write off, with this track tending to bring out the best in him.
runners.comment: Rediscovered a spark, scrambling home over C&D (good; 12-1) 18 days ago; now 4lb higher.

runners.name: Stylish Recruit
runners.spotlight: Game winner here (2m4f; from 2lb lower) in April; excuse next time (saddle slipped) but less competitive at Kelso (2m5f, good; fifth, beaten 10l) a fortnight ago; needs to bounce back against rivals with a fair amount of upside.
runners.comment: Dual course winner (2m-2m4f); less competitive at Kelso (2m5f; fifth) a fortnight ago.

runners.name: Dinons
runners.spotlight: Very useful at his peak, and his last win came from a mark 13lb higher than today's at Uttoxeter (3m, good) in July 2024; hampered and unseated rider when last seen at Newcastle six months ago; difficult to assess how much ability remains at the age of 13.
runners.comment: Hampered and unseated rider when last seen at Newcastle six months ago; advancing years.

races.race_name: attheraces.com/marketmovers Handicap

runners.name: Bobacious
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who gave good account when placed behind King's Hand over C&D (good) on last month's stable debut; that run came after a layoff and he remains on a good mark; handles good to soft (unraced on anything slower); no surprise if he makes a bold bid.
runners.comment: Front-runner; ran well when placed behind King's Hand over C&D on recent stable debut

runners.name: King's Hand
runners.spotlight: Signed off for Sir Mark Prescott with 1m2f AW win here in January and has performed well on all four starts for new stable, most notably when recovering from a slow start to win over C&D (1m2f, good) last month; still unexposed on turf but he often races too freely and is a bit of a character.
runners.comment: Did well to win over C&D last month, after a slow start, but is a bit of a character

runners.name: Bintkend
runners.spotlight: Unexposed filly; did not live up to pedigree in her three qualifying races but kept on for third behind a fairly useful filly in a Wolverhampton maiden (9.4f, AW) in February and could have significant improvement to come in handicaps.
runners.comment: Unexposed and well-bred filly; open to significant improvement on handicap debut

runners.name: A Major Payne
runners.spotlight: Placed three consecutive times before leading close home to open account at Nottingham (1m2f, good; front pair clear) on Wednesday, carries 5lb penalty here but should be fine on the forecast slower ground; ought to be right in the thick of things again.
runners.comment: Carries 5lb penalty for Wednesday's Nottingham win but ought to be in the shake-up again

runners.name: Kokanee
runners.spotlight: Yet to make any significant further progress since winning handicap debut at Beverley (1m2f, good) in spring 2025 but went close at Pontefract (1m, good to soft) two starts ago and should cope if the ground remains soft.
runners.comment: Not making any progress but went close over 1m two starts ago and this trip also suits

runners.name: Kaaranah
runners.spotlight: Seemingly boosted by cheekpieces when winning twice at Wolverhampton (9.4f, AW) in April and endured a wide trip before keeping on steadily for sixth at Kempton (1m, AW; no headgear, as today) last month; versatile ground-wise on turf; can't be ignored.
runners.comment: Won twice on AW in April and is versatile ground-wise on turf; can't be ignored

runners.name: Birthday Angel
runners.spotlight: Scored on the Flat at Brighton (1m2f, good to soft) in October and added two hurdle wins to his tally the following month; probably still on a workable Flat mark and seems versatile regarding ground but has fitness to prove after 172-day absence.
runners.comment: In good form on Flat/over hurdles in the autumn but has fitness to prove after break

races.race_name: Buy A Share At daretodreamracing.co.uk Handicap

runners.name: Sovereign Bay
runners.spotlight: Caught the eye (repeatedly denied a clear run) when keeping on late for fourth of 11 on 1m Haydock handicap and turf debut (good to soft) 15 days ago; possibly not entirely straightforward (has taken a keen hold on most starts) but it's likely he has the ability to win off this mark.
runners.comment: Pulls hard but an eyecatcher on recent handicap and turf debut; gets the vote

runners.name: Royal Bodyguard
runners.spotlight: Stepped up on reappearance when third of ten over 1m at Windsor (good to firm) a fortnight ago, although he faded inside the final furlong and this sometimes keen goer wouldn't be sure to benefit from this slightly longer distance.
runners.comment: Competitive off his mark but wouldn't be sure to benefit from tthis step back up in trip

runners.name: Del Corso
runners.spotlight: Third around 1m at Nottingham (good) and Yarmouth (good to firm) switched to handicaps this spring; below the level of those efforts on soft ground last year but it was his debut; has a useful pedigree and too soon to suggest he's reached his limit.
runners.comment: Third in both handicaps and too soon to suggest he's reached his limit

runners.name: Takeitorleaveit
runners.spotlight: Winner over 1m at Bath on soft last autumn and returned to the same venue to double his tally on reappearance (good to firm); a wide trip provides a possible excuse for a lesser effort at Windsor since (Fans Favourite ahead of him).
runners.comment: Two 1m Bath wins, including on soft; possible excuse for lesser run last time

runners.name: Fans Favourite
runners.spotlight: Showed he can be competitive in handicaps at the second attempt when returning from five months off to finish third of 14 over 1m at Windsor 12 days ago; raced only on good/good to firm on turf; claims with Mason Paetel taking a handy 5lb off.
runners.comment: Solid return from a break when third of 14 at Windsor recently; Mason Paetel takes 5lb off

runners.name: Daretobedifferent
runners.spotlight: Came good at the 12th time of asking when seeing off six rivals at Nottingham (extended 1m, good) last Sunday; not obviously well treated under a 6lb penalty.
runners.comment: Not obviously well treated under a 6lb penalty for last weekend's breakthrough win

runners.name: Rajendra
runners.spotlight: Tough to argue his opening mark is generous on bare form but he's more than hinted at ability and appeals as a potential improver back from a 100-day break.
runners.comment: Has hinted at ability in his qualifying runs and appeals as a likely improver in handicaps

races.race_name: Free Bets On attheraces.com Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C And D) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Cold Fish
runners.spotlight: Third on debut at Chelmsford (1m2f, AW) last December and again showed promise when occupying the same position at Goodwood (1m, good) last month; shaped last time as though the return to 1m2f would suit; leading player.
runners.comment: Promising 3rd on both runs (1m2f/1m); this could be his best trip for now; leading player

runners.name: Relentless Hero
runners.spotlight: He's shown ability on all three starts, most recently beaten just under 3l when a front-running fourth of 12 at Leicester (1m2f, good to firm; unraced on softer); he's one to consider.
runners.comment: Ran well from the front when fourth at Leicester on third start and he's one to consider

runners.name: Cherringham
runners.spotlight: Sixth foal; sister to 1m2f winner Mighty Quiet (RPR 78), half-sister to winners Silent City (1m; 88) and Maurice Dancer (7f AW 2yo; 69); dam from family of top miler Soviet Song who raced in these colours; newcomer from a powerful yard and the standard set isn't overly daunting; interesting.
runners.comment: Newcomer from a powerful yard and the standard set isn't overly daunting; interesting

runners.name: Probation
runners.spotlight: He hasn't threatened on his first two starts (9.4f/1m2f, AW/good to firm) but has shown promise and made the frame on each occasion; open to improvement and could be in the shake-up.
runners.comment: He's made the frame on both starts; open to improvement and could be in the shake-up

runners.name: Parc Dosne
runners.spotlight: 4,000euros yearling, resold 30,000euros yearling; second foal; half-brother to 1m/1m2f winner Quai De Bethune (RPR 107); dam unraced, out of half-sister to French 1,000 Guineas/Coronation Stakes winner Ervedya; with a respected trainer (who also saddles Relentless Hero); could go well on debut.
runners.comment: Half-brother to 1m/1m2f winner Quai De Bethune (RPR 107); could go well on debut

runners.name: Celestial Cen
runners.spotlight: 2,000euros 3yo; fifth foal; half-sister to US 7f dirt 2yo winner Golden Sights (RPR 93); dam US stakes-placed 5.5f turf 2yo winner, half-sister to US 8.5f Grade 1 winner; in good hands and the betting should guide on debut; trainer also saddles the once-raced Fabled Spirit.
runners.comment: 2,000euros 3yo; half-sister to US 7f dirt 2yo winner; the betting should guide on debut

runners.name: Liberate
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal; half-brother to French winners Chandler's Song (1m1f) and Woodwind (1m/9.5f); dam 7f/1m winner (RPR 93); his in-form trainer can ready one first time and the betting could be informative on debut.
runners.comment: Half-brother to two French winners; the betting could be informative on debut

runners.name: Ataturk
runners.spotlight: Well beaten at big odds at Newbury on both starts (1m/1m2f, soft/good) and this half-brother to connections' smart stayer Kyle Of Lochalsh will be of greater interest when stepped up in trip in handicaps.
runners.comment: Well beaten on both starts; he'll be of greater interest when stepped up in trip

runners.name: Fabled Spirit
runners.spotlight: Ran green throughout and always behind on recent debut at Leicester (1m2f, good to firm); however, there's potential in his pedigree and he was backed in to 11-1; trainer also saddles the newcomer Celestial Cen; interesting to see how he goes in the betting this time.
runners.comment: Green throughout and always behind on recent debut, when backed; watch the market

races.race_name: Free Race Replays On attheraces.com Handicap

runners.name: Zoustar Dreams
runners.spotlight: Fresh cheekpieces may have helped when she took advantage of a reduced mark to open her account at Catterick (6f, good to firm) one week ago; up 6lb but started quickly to make all that day so has to be feared with the potential to bag the favoured stands' rail if clearing Jane Of The Jungle; yet to be tested on ground worse than good but her dam recorded best effort on soft.
runners.comment: Cheekpieces and reduced mark when off the mark (6f, good to firm) one week ago; contender

runners.name: Rosieisme Darling
runners.spotlight: Untried on softer than good ground; three runs on turf and the best of them came last time out when third of 12 at Windsor (6f, good) 19 days ago; was mucked about that day and this dual 5f AW winner should be fine back down in trip, especially given a likely good gallop despite the small field.
runners.comment: Best turf run when third (6f, good) 19 days ago; softer ground a query but 5f likely isn't

runners.name: My Mate Mackley
runners.spotlight: Gelded ahead of his handicap debut and it was a good step forward when on the pace before sharing the spoils at Chepstow (5f, good to soft; Jane Of The Jungle fourth) 16 days ago; that was his first go at this trip and the handicapper could only dish out a 1lb nudge to this unexposed sprinter.
runners.comment: Gelded and took a step forward on h'cap debut when dead-heating latest; unexposed sprinter

races.race_name: Free Tips On attheraces.com Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C And D) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Princess Maitha
runners.spotlight: Well-bred filly who made a promising debut for Charlie Johnston when clear second to useful rival at Catterick (7f, soft) last summer; not seen since but has a good draw for this stable debut; leading contender.
runners.comment: Made promising debut on soft ground last summer; major player on stable debut

runners.name: Show Me Gold
runners.spotlight: Runner-up on both AW starts, latterly in 13-runner maiden at Kempton (7f) in January; that form isn't anything to get too excited about but she has a big role to play here if suited by the switch to turf.
runners.comment: Runner-up twice on AW during the winter; contender if suited by switch to turf

runners.name: Drymee
runners.spotlight: Belied 150-1 odds when promising fourth for Joseph Parr at Leicester (7f, good) in September but, having been gelded, was disappointing when weakening seventh of nine for new stable (same owners) at Kempton (7f, AW) last month; needs that recent outing to have brought her on.
runners.comment: Made promising 2yo debut at big odds but last month's reappearance was underwhelming

runners.name: Sayidah Aleen
runners.spotlight: Last of four on debut for Jack Channon (8.6f, AW) in March but seemingly aided by cheekpieces when keeping-on second (no threat to winner) for new stable here (7f, good) last month; each-way claims at least from a favourable draw.
runners.comment: Seemingly aided by cheekpieces when keeping-on second here (7f) on stable debut

runners.name: Call Alert
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal; dam 11.6f/1m4f winner (RPR 88), half-sister to prolific 6f-1m4f winner Spiritual Air; drawn on the wing for this debut run; considered only if lively in the betting.
runners.comment: Out of a fairly useful middle-distance winner; considered only if lively in betting

runners.name: Nothing But Love
runners.spotlight: 1,000gns 2yo; second foal; half-sister to 1m2f winner Approval (RPR 97); dam unraced half-sister to winners Ehtiraas (1m) and Saraha (1m4f AW); hooded for belated debut; seems unlikely to meet the standard required.
runners.comment: Hooded for belated debut; seems unlikely to meet the standard required

runners.name: Ernie McCrew
runners.spotlight: Not beaten that far on either appearance this spring (7f, AW/turf) but low-grade handicaps are probably next on his agenda.
runners.comment: Not beaten that far on first two starts but low-grade handicaps beckon

runners.name: Vicit Venture
runners.spotlight: Not disgraced when eighth of 12 at Windsor (6f, good to firm) last month, his second start, but makes limited appeal here.
runners.comment: Not disgraced in 6f Windsor maiden last month but makes limited appeal here

runners.name: Don Pablo Colina
runners.spotlight: Displayed promise when midfield on both AW starts last month (1m/7f) but will probably be of greater interest when switched to run-of-the-mill handicaps.
runners.comment: Displayed promise on both AW starts last month but is probably one for handicaps

runners.name: Arc At Her
runners.spotlight: Showed nothing more than minor promise on her two starts last month (1m/8.3f) and, judged on pedigree, she'll probably need longer trips to be seen to best effect.
runners.comment: Showed nothing more than minor promise on first two starts
runners.owner: Gee Gee's Racing & Hold My Beer

runners.name: Mr Jeffrey
runners.spotlight: Well beaten at huge odds on both starts this spring (7f/6f); impossible to recommend.
runners.comment: Well beaten at huge odds on both starts this spring

runners.name: Turton
runners.spotlight: Safely held on both starts, more recently when sixth of seven at Nottingham (8.3f, good) on Sunday; needs to take a big step forward.
runners.comment: Safely held on both starts (7f/8.3f); needs to take a good step forward

runners.name: Dovecote
runners.spotlight: In good hands and has a good pedigree but struggled to get competitive on debut at Chelmsford (7f, AW) in November; gelded in February; needs to be a completely different proposition today.
runners.comment: In good hands and has good pedigree but struggled to get competitive on debut
runners.owner: A D G Oldrey & G C Hartigan

races.race_name: Eikon Helping Young People In Surrey Handicap

runners.name: Pixie Diva
runners.spotlight: Dual sprint winner on AW; 0-5 on turf but ran well when favourite for big-field event at Thirsk (5f, good; finished 1l third) most recently; warrants respect off same mark.
runners.comment: 0-5 on turf but ran well when favourite for Thirsk race last time; respected off same mark

runners.name: Charlie Mason
runners.spotlight: Modest strike-rate in the last 14 months but he is generally consistent and is versatile as regards surfaces; form figures off current mark read 222; could go one better, with Myla Coppins taking off 7lb.
runners.comment: Record off current mark reads 222; could go one better, with Myla Coppins claiming 7lb

runners.name: Queue Dos
runners.spotlight: Four AW wins; 0-12 on turf but has largely consistent form in this sphere, creditable third at Bath (5.7f, good to firm) most recently; each-way possibilities.
runners.comment: 0-12 on turf but has largely consistent form in this sphere, third at Bath last time

runners.name: Brazen Idol
runners.spotlight: Missed 2025; failed to back up reappearance effort last time, kept to Kempton AW; effective on turf in the past but hasn't raced in this sphere for over two years.
runners.comment: Effective on turf in the past but hasn't raced in this sphere for over two years

runners.name: Monsieur Kodi
runners.spotlight: Failed to back up the form of his February win last time, kept to 7f on AW; multiple sprint winner on turf and made the frame in last four attempts in this sphere; solid.
runners.comment: Multiple sprint winner on turf and has made the frame in last four attempts in this sphere

runners.name: Raffles Angel
runners.spotlight: Unraced on ground softer than good; absent since an excusable effort (hampered start, poorly positioned) last August; 4-9 on turf and 1-1 over C&D; one to consider.
runners.comment: Absent since excusable effort last August; 4-9 on turf and 1-1 over C&D; one to consider

runners.name: Hierarchy
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent on AW this year, aside from winning in February; well treated on best turf form but hasn't scored in this sphere since 2023.
runners.comment: Well treated on best turf form but hasn't scored in this sphere since 2023

runners.name: Tyger Bay
runners.spotlight: Far from solid on 2026 AW form, notwithstanding a March win, and rarely seen on turf in the last two years; others preferred.
runners.comment: Not solid on 2026 AW form and rarely seen on turf in last two years

races.race_name: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Handicap

runners.name: Beau Jardine
runners.spotlight: Denied clear run before keeping on to snatch respectable fourth over C&D (good) last month but remains without a win since 2024 and is not the percentage call.
runners.comment: Kept on for respectable C&D fourth last month but remains without a win since 2024

runners.name: No Gain
runners.spotlight: Largely in good form in cheekpieces for new stable this year and was an emphatic course winner (7.6f, good; previously ran well on good to soft) on latest outing 18 days ago; 6lb higher now but still taken very seriously.
runners.comment: Emphatic 7.6f course winner last month; taken very seriously off this 6lb higher mark

runners.name: Fulford Cross
runners.spotlight: Led close home off today's mark here (7f, AW) in December but was below that form on all three AW starts since and is not yet proven on turf; others have more obvious claims.
runners.comment: Narrow AW winner here in December but not in the same form since and not proven on turf

runners.name: Ballybaymoonshiner
runners.spotlight: Rarely runs on turf but was close third at Chepstow (6f, good to firm) just under a year ago; returned from break with encouraging run at Southwell (7f, AW) in April, keeping on from a long way off the pace for sixth, and has possibilities if coping with the forecast slow ground.
runners.comment: Ran quite well on AW in April, after a layoff; a possible if suited by today's ground

runners.name: Daddy Chill
runners.spotlight: Seven-race maiden; had excuse when well-beaten 50-1 shot on last month's stable debut (saddle slipped) but his headstrong tendencies remains a serious worry.
runners.comment: Had excuse when well beaten last time but headstrong tendencies remain a serious worry

runners.name: Drafted
runners.spotlight: Three-time AW winner; 0-5 for new stable since February but put in a good front-running shift before fading into fourth behind No Gain here (7.6f, good) last month; each-way claims if handing the forecast slower ground.
runners.comment: 0-5 for new stable since February but ran well when fourth behind No Gain here last month

runners.name: Zambezi Diamond
runners.spotlight: Six-race maiden; set good pace and belied 66-1 odds when fourth in Southwell maiden (6f, AW) in April, after a long layoff, but was well beaten in a recent Bath handicap (5.7f, good to firm); back up in trip here; remains tricky to weigh up.
runners.comment: Six-race maiden; ran well in April, after long layoff, but well beaten since

runners.name: Kessaar Power
runners.spotlight: Out of form on AW when last seen in January/February but ought to cope well if the ground remains soft and is well drawn to seize the favoured nearside rail; worth a second look.
runners.comment: Out of form on AW in early part of year but has a good draw and handles slow ground well

races.race_name: Belmont Stakes Presented By NYRA Bets (Grade 1) (Dirt)

runners.name: Renegade
runners.spotlight: Impressive winner of Grade 1 Arkansas Derby at Oaklawn, where he changed gear and ran on strongly (a very fast finishing split); neck second to Golden Tempo in Grade 1 Kentucky Derby, bumped early on inside but working through to challenge and running on, no match for winner close home; should give another good account and could be ready for an improved effort after five-week break.
runners.comment: Impressive Grade 1 winner at Oaklawn, touched off in Kentucky Derby; must go well again
runners.owner: Robert Low, Lawana L Low & Repole Stable

runners.name: Golden Tempo
runners.spotlight: Benefited from pace meltdown when winning Grade 1 Kentucky Derby by a nose from Renegade, virtually tailed off early before relentless progress last 3f, then running on strongly to lead close home; has more stamina than his contemporaries but surely needs similar kind of race to put that in play again; trained with great skill by Cherie Devaux.
runners.comment: Came from long way back to win Kentucky Derby by a nose from Renegade; same set-up needed

runners.name: Chief Wallabee
runners.spotlight: Highly talented colt who gives impression he is keeping something back; in frame in Grade 1 events for Florida Derby at Gulfstream Park in March and Kentucky Derby (1m2f) in May; hesitated when short of room at top of straight when 3l fourth to Golden Tempo on latter occasion; should be thereabouts but needs to be braver.
runners.comment: Good 3l fourth to Golden Tempo in Grade 1 Kentucky Derby, short of room; place claims

runners.name: Commandment
runners.spotlight: Admirable colt with a tremendous attitude to racing; fourth consecutive victory when nose winner (from The Puma) of Grade 1 Florida Derby at Gulfstream Park in March, closing well off a slow pace; run best ignored in Kentucky Derby, starting slowly and forced wide in hottest part of race; could easily pick up winning thread, provided earlier races have not left their mark.
runners.comment: Game Grade 1 Florida Derby winner, best forgiven 7th in Kentucky Derby; could bounce back

runners.name: Growth Equity
runners.spotlight: Really likeable colt who is thriving at present; maiden winner at Aqueduct in March before Grade 3 Peter Pan score on same track in May (1m1f) when he subdued very useful Talk To Me Jimmy by 2l while strong at finish; should be suited by the extra furlong and has place claims, if improving again.
runners.comment: Improving 2l Grade 3 Peter Pan winner (1m1f) at Aqueduct last month; place claims

runners.name: Powershift
runners.spotlight: Second favourite for Grade 3 Tampa Bay Derby after promising second (to Emerging Market) in a maiden, but beaten nearly 10l into sixth; impressive in maiden at Churchill Downs (8.5f) last month, drawing nearly 3l clear in taking style (fast time); short of the required standard on form, but should be suited by the longer trip and could outrun his odds from the front.
runners.comment: Confirmed earlier promise in Churchill Downs maiden win, drawing clear; could surprise

runners.name: Emerging Market
runners.spotlight: Remarkable time performance to win maiden at Tampa Bay in February, then tenacious, despite inexperience, when following up in Grade 2 Louisiana Derby (9.5f) at Fair Grounds with Golden Tempo in third; only tenth in Kentucky Derby, close to strong pace and tiring badly in stretch; it was a big ask at Churchill Downs on only third start and he could have learned a good deal from the experience.
runners.comment: Grade 2 Louisiana Derby winner; only 10th in Kentucky Derby, not much room; better to come

runners.name: Vitruvian Man
runners.spotlight: Keeneland maiden winner last October and placed in two allowance races later in 2025; 47-1, best effort when 9.5l third to So Happy in Grade 1 Santa Anita Derby (1m1f) in April, staying on from rear; stamina glutton who will be very much at home over the extra furlong here; minor place prospects.
runners.comment: Outsider when 9.5l third in 1m1f Grade 1 at Santa Anita, staying on; minor place prospects
runners.owner: Glenn Sorgenstein WC Racing Inc & Run Fast Racing

runners.name: Ottinho
runners.spotlight: Game winner of 1m1f maiden at Aqueduct on second start as juvenile; beaten more than 10l and never able to challenge when both third in Listed race there and second to Further Ado in Grade 1 Blue Grass Stakes at Keeneland; stays well but surely needs a destructive pace up front to claim a place.
runners.comment: Beaten more than 10l last two starts when third in Listed and 2nd in Grade 1; lacks pace

races.race_name: Just A Game Stakes Presented By Resolute Racing (Grade 1) (Fillies & Mares) (Turf)

runners.name: Classic Q
runners.spotlight: A winner over C&D; hit the bar in a Grade 3 three months ago; got home in front by a head in an allowance Grade 2 at Churchill Downs five weeks ago; should prove competitive on first attempt at a big race.
runners.comment: Having her first crack at a big race and looks a likely player.
runners.owner: Gary Barber, Blue Crevalle Racing & Eclipse T'Bred Partners

runners.name: Segesta
runners.spotlight: Too good for Fast Market and Deep Satin at Keeneland two months ago, getting the job done in the Jenny Wiley Stakes by three-quarters of a length; successful five times from 12 career runs and on a hat-trick; she has won after a layoff in the past and ought to be considered.
runners.comment: She has defied a similar absence before and capable of going well.

runners.name: Deep Satin
runners.spotlight: A course winner; behind Segesta when last two months ago at Keeneland.
runners.comment: A course winner; behind Segesta when last two months ago at Keeneland.
runners.owner: John D Gunther & Eurowest Bloodstock Services

runners.name: And One More Time
runners.spotlight: Narrow success in an allowance listed at Aqueduct 50 days ago; managed four wins in her last seven outings; can be in the mix on first attempt at a big race.
runners.comment: Having her first crack at a big race and is of interest.

runners.name: Sandtrap
runners.spotlight: A good winner in an allowance race 49 days ago at Aqueduct on debut for this trainer; excellent winning ratio overseas - on target once in two runs.
runners.comment: Scored by 4 1/2l 49 days ago; impressive strike-rate overseas, winning once from two runs.

runners.name: Buttercream Babe
runners.spotlight: Finishing third was a good effort last month at Belmont At The Big A in an allowance listed; a winner over C&D; must be respected.
runners.comment: Capable of making an impact; on the premises last month.

runners.name: Mandanaba
runners.spotlight: Wide-margin winner in a newcomers' race at Chantilly in 2024; on target three times in six runs overseas, earning decent prize-money; market might reveal clues to her prospects on this first try in this jurisdiction.
runners.comment: Resounding success 571 days ago; one to watch in the market making debut on these shores.

runners.name: Fast Market
runners.spotlight: Looking for the third success of her career; finished sixth in the allowance Grade 1 Jenny Wiley Stakes albeit only beaten just under 3l when behind the reopposing Segesta at Keeneland two months ago.
runners.comment: Not far away in Keeneland allowance Grade 1 two months ago.

races.race_name: True North Stakes (Grade 3) (Dirt)

runners.name: Book'em Danno
runners.spotlight: Not beaten far at Aqueduct over 7f (Grade 2, had Be You (third) and Acoustic Ave (last) behind) two months ago; suited by this C&D; a winner of a Grade 3 here last June; down in class; should not be far away.
runners.comment: Has to be taken seriously; C&D has proven suitable before.

runners.name: Bentornato
runners.spotlight: Came out on top in the Breeders' Cup Sprint by just over 2l at Del Mar, managing to get across to lead from stall ten in October; has now won twice on the spin following success last time; an easier task than his previous race; should go close.
runners.comment: Should not be far away; claimed Breeders' Cup Sprint in October.
runners.owner: Leon King Stable Corp & TCC Stables LLC

runners.name: Imagination
runners.spotlight: A winner of a Grade 2 in September and has maintained his consistent level of form; finished fourth in the Grade 1 Churchill Downs Stakes albeit only beaten just over 5l at Churchill Downs five weeks ago; could have less on his plate in this; the yard is going well; is respected.
runners.comment: Can be in the mix; fair effort in Churchill Downs Grade 1 five weeks ago.
runners.owner: SF Racing LLC, Starlight Racing Et Al

runners.name: Listenupshance
runners.spotlight: Not beaten too far (3l) at Santa Anita over 6f (allowance optional claimer) five weeks ago; consistent performer and resume shows that he is a proven winner (seeking a fourth career win); moves up into an allowance race.
runners.comment: Not disgraced 35 days ago; a proven winner with a career wins-runs record of 3-11.

runners.name: Faust
runners.spotlight: Produced a string of consistent efforts; on target twice in his last three outings; beaten a head over 6f at Laurel Park (allowance Grade 3) last month; class rise to contend with.
runners.comment: Not beaten far three weeks ago; has chalked up two wins from his last three.

runners.name: Acoustic Ave
runners.spotlight: Running consistently and achieved three wins at this venue; cracking effort in defeat when close runner-up over 6f at Belmont At The Big A (allowance Grade 3) last month; steps up to allowance company.
runners.comment: Not beaten far last month; has maintained his consistent level of form.

runners.name: Be You
runners.spotlight: Fair effort (beaten 4l) in a Listed race at Churchill Downs 35 days ago; has maintained his consistent level of form and successful four times from 14 career runs; class hike now; into allowance company; now tackles shorter distance.
runners.comment: Fair effort last month; resume shows that he is a proven winner (seeking a fifth career win).

runners.name: Pentathlon
runners.spotlight: Not disgraced when third at Belmont At The Big A over 1m (allowance Grade 3) 34 days ago; successful three times in his last five starts; has winning track form; drops in trip.
runners.comment: Not beaten that far in third last month; managed three wins in his last five outings.
runners.owner: Phipps Stable, St Elias Stable & Woodford Racing Llc

runners.name: Illuminare
runners.spotlight: Won by just under 5l at Keeneland 50 days ago in an allowance optional claimer; bidding for a hat-trick; has won here; moves up into an allowance race.
runners.comment: Scored by just under 5l 50 days ago; has won previously at this course.

races.race_name: Jaipur Stakes (Grade 1) (Turf)

runners.name: Ag Bullet
runners.spotlight: Good effort in the Matriarch Stakes when 3l third in November (Del Mar) upped to 1m; on target twice in her last four outings; running consistently; has had some time off; must be respected as she can go well fresh.
runners.comment: Looks likely to be involved given her record when fresh.

runners.name: Reef Runner
runners.spotlight: Slowly away in an allowance listed (Gulfstream Park) in December; career wins-runs record of 7-22; back from an absence here.
runners.comment: On target in December; resume shows that he is a proven winner (seeking a eighth career win).

runners.name: Governor Sam
runners.spotlight: Left punters frustrated after going off favourite when fourth over 5f at Gulfstream Park (handicap) five weeks ago; a winner over C&D; likely to go well if replicating his best form.
runners.comment: Has won in classy company before and he's an attractive contender.
runners.owner: Bregman Family Racing LLC & Swinbank Stables LLC

runners.name: Litigation
